Clinician Observations, Notes, and Diagnostic Impressions

title: ADOS-2 Module 1 Assessment Summary
description: A comprehensive observational summary template for the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition (ADOS-2) Module 1. This module is
prompt:
(The template below is designed for psychologists administering the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition, also known as the ADOS-2, Module 1 assessment. This is a standardised, semi-structured, play-based observational assessment designed to evaluate social communication, reciprocal social interaction, play, and restricted/repetitive behaviours in children who do not consistently use phrase speech. Using the transcript, contextual notes, and observations from the session, you will generate a detailed summary of behavioural observations made during the standardised assessment activities. The summary should capture the child's presentation, behaviours across all domains, and clinical impressions in a way that balances neurodiversity-affirming language with standardised ADOS-2 terminology. Use the DSM-5-TR criteria and ADOS-2 framework when describing observations. Organise the summary under the following headings.)
#### ADOS-2 Module 1 Algorithm: [Specify: Few to No Words / Some Words. If unknown, print: “Unable to determine.”] Child Information: * Child Name: [Print the client’s first and last name.] * Date of Birth: [Print the client’s date of birth in Day Month, Year format.] * Chronological Age: [Print the client’s age on the day of the assessment in Years, Months format.] * Date of Assessment: [Print the date of the assessment in Day Month, Year format.] Attendance: [CHILD NAME] attended the session with [CAREGIVER(S)]. Initial Presentation: [Describe the child's presentation on arrival, e.g., calm / curious / anxious / distressed / engaged / withdrawn / tired. Describe engagement with the clinician on entry, e.g., approached readily / stayed close to caregiver / avoided eye contact / needed encouragement to enter the assessment room. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] BEHAVIOURAL OBSERVATIONS DURING ADOS-2 MODULE 1 **LANGUAGE AND COMMUNICATION** **Overall Expressive Language Level:** [Describe the child's expressive language, e.g., nonverbal / single words only / occasional short phrases / primarily jargon / echolalia present / use of communicative sounds without words. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Frequency of Spontaneous Vocalisations Directed to Others:** [Describe how often the child directed vocalisations, sounds, or words toward others, e.g., frequent spontaneous vocalisations / occasional vocalisations when motivated / rare or no vocalisations directed to others. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Intonation of Vocalisations or Verbalisations:** [Describe the quality and variation of intonation by the child, e.g., varied and appropriate intonation / limited prosodic variation / unusual or flat intonation / stereotyped or repetitive vocalisations. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Immediate Echolalia:** [Describe any immediate repetition of words or phrases by the child, e.g., no echolalia observed / occasional echolalia / frequent immediate repetition of clinician's speech. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Stereotyped / Idiosyncratic Use of Words or Phrases:** [Describe any unusual, repetitive, or context-inappropriate use of language by the child, e.g., no unusual language patterns / some repetitive phrases / idiosyncratic or scripted language observed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Use of Another's Body to Communicate:** [Describe instances where the child used the clinician's or caregiver's hand/body to communicate wants or needs, e.g., did not use others' bodies instrumentally / occasionally guided clinician's hand to objects / frequently used hand-leading to communicate requests. (Note: Do not describe use of another's body unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Pointing:** [Describe the child's use of pointing gestures, e.g., used pointing to request and/or share interest / pointing primarily for requests / limited or absent pointing. (Note: Do not describe pointing unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Gestures:** [Describe the range and quality of gestures by the child, e.g., used varied conventional gestures (waving, nodding, reaching) / limited gesture repertoire / minimal or absent gesture use / unconventional gestures observed. (Note: Do not describe gestures unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **RECIPROCAL SOCIAL INTERACTION** **Unusual Eye Contact:** [Describe the child’s patterns of eye contact, e.g., frequent and well-integrated eye contact / brief and inconsistent / fleeting glances only / avoidance of eye contact / atypical quality of gaze. (Note: Do not describe eye contact unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Response to Name:** [Describe the child's response when name was called, e.g., responded promptly and consistently / required repeated prompts / inconsistent response / no clear response to name. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Facial Expressions Directed to Others:** [Describe the range and social directedness of the child’s facial expressions, e.g., varied and socially directed facial expressions / limited range of expressions / neutral or flat affect / expressions not clearly directed to others. (Note: Do not describe facial expressions unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Integration of Gaze and Other Behaviours During Social Overtures:** [Describe coordination of eye contact with vocalisations, gestures, or affect during social bids, e.g., well-integrated coordination of gaze, gesture, and vocalisation / some integration but inconsistent / limited integration / isolated behaviours without coordination. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Shared Enjoyment in Interaction:** [Describe the child's expression and sharing of positive affect, e.g., clearly shared enjoyment with clinician/caregiver through gaze, smiles, and vocalisations / showed pleasure but did not share with others / limited or absent shared enjoyment. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Response to Joint Attention:** [Describe the child's ability to follow the clinician's gaze or point to a distal object/event, e.g., consistently followed joint attention bids / required additional cues / inconsistent response / did not follow joint attention attempts. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Initiating or Maintaining Social Interaction:** [Describe the child's attempts to engage socially, e.g., frequently initiated interaction with clinician/caregiver / occasional social initiations / rarely initiated / did not initiate social interaction independently. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Quality of Social Overtures:** [Describe the appropriateness and integration of the child’s social bids, e.g., integrated and appropriate social overtures / some social interest but poorly integrated / unusual or limited quality / minimal or absent social overtures. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Amount of Reciprocal Social Communication:** [Describe the child’s overall level of back-and-forth social engagement, e.g., sustained reciprocal exchanges / brief exchanges that required support / limited reciprocity / minimal or absent reciprocal communication. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Overall Quality of Rapport:** [Describe the nature of the relationship established between the child and the clinician, e.g., warm and comfortable rapport / some rapport established with support / limited rapport / minimal social connection. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Showing:** [Describe instances of the child showing objects to share interest, e.g., spontaneously showed objects to clinician/caregiver / showed objects when prompted / limited or no showing behaviour observed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Spontaneous Initiation of Joint Attention:** [Describe any unprompted attempts by the child to direct others' attention to objects/events of interest, e.g., spontaneously initiated joint attention through gaze, gesture, and/or vocalisation / occasional initiations / rare or absent spontaneous joint attention. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **PLAY** **Functional Play:** [Describe the child's use of toys in conventional ways, e.g., demonstrated varied functional play with toys / some functional play / primarily exploratory or sensory engagement with toys. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Imagination/Creativity:** [Describe evidence of pretend or symbolic play by the child, e.g., engaged in simple pretend actions with support / spontaneously used objects symbolically / no pretend play observed / play remained concrete and object-focused. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Flexibility and Range of Play:** [Describe the variety and adaptability of the child’s play, e.g., demonstrated flexible and varied play across activities / some rigidity or repetitiveness in play / limited play repertoire / highly restricted play. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Transitions Between Activities:** [Describe the child's ability to shift between tasks, e.g., transitioned smoothly with minimal distress / required support for transitions / became distressed or resistant when activities changed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **STEREOTYPED BEHAVIORS AND RESTRICTED INTERESTS** **Unusual Sensory Interest in Play Material/Person:** [Describe the child’s atypical sensory exploration, e.g., no unusual sensory interests / some sensory seeking (visual inspection, sniffing, mouthing) / pronounced sensory interest that interfered with engagement / sensory avoidance observed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Hand and Finger and Other Complex Mannerisms:** [Describe the child’s repetitive motor movements, e.g., no unusual mannerisms / occasional hand flapping, finger posturing, or body movements / frequent and prominent motor mannerisms. (Note: Do not describe repetitive motor movements unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Self-Injurious Behaviour:** [Describe any self-harm behaviours by the child, e.g., no self-injurious behaviour observed / occasional instances (specify) / frequent self-injury requiring intervention. (Note: Do not describe self-injurious behaviour unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Unusually Repetitive Interests or Stereotyped Behaviours:** [Describe the child’s repetitive use of objects or stereotyped actions, e.g., no unusual repetitive behaviours / some repetitive actions (spinning, lining up, flicking objects) / prominent stereotyped or ritualistic behaviours. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Frequency and Impact of Repetitive Behaviours:** [Describe how often these repetitive behaviours occurred and their effect on the child’s engagement in the assessment, e.g., occasional and easily redirected / frequent and somewhat interfered with activities / pervasive and significantly limited engagement. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **REGULATION, ATTENTION, AND BEHAVIOUR** **Attention and Focus:** [Describe the quality of the child’s attention, e.g., sustained attention to most activities / distractible but redirectable / quickly disengaged from tasks / over-focused on specific items to exclusion of social interaction. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Activity Level:** [Describe the child’s overall energy and movement, e.g., age-appropriate activity level / markedly overactive or restless / markedly passive or withdrawn / fluctuated throughout session. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Emotional Regulation:** [Describe the child’s capacity to manage emotions, e.g., generally calm and regulated / occasional frustration that settled with support / episodes of distress (describe triggers, duration, and what helped) / difficulty recovering from distress. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Behavioural Concerns:** [Note any aggression, tantrums, bolting, or other safety concerns by the child, including context and how managed. If none present, state: “No significant behavioural concerns noted during assessment.” (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **CAREGIVER INTERACTION AND REPORT** **Caregiver's Interaction Style:** [Describe how the caregiver engaged with the child during session, e.g., warm and responsive / highly directive / anxious or worried / observant but allowed child independence / required guidance to step back at times. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Caregiver Reports During Session:** [Briefly note any relevant information shared by the caregiver about communication, social interaction, repetitive behaviours, sensory responses, daily functioning, or developmental concerns. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **CLINICAL IMPRESSIONS AND SUMMARY** **Summary of Observed Strengths:** [Describe positive observations and capacities of the child, e.g., showed enjoyment in play with familiar adults; demonstrated emerging joint attention with support; used some non-verbal communication such as reaching and gesture; responded to simple routines; engaged in functional play with preferred toys, etc. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Summary of Observed Differences and Areas of Support Need:** [Describe patterns consistent with autism characteristics, e.g., differences in social reciprocity and social communication, including reduced eye contact, limited spontaneous social initiation, reduced integration of gaze with other communicative behaviours, limited use of gestures or pointing, restricted range of play, and presence of repetitive behaviours and/or sensory sensitivities. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Overall Clinical Impressions:** [Provide an integrated observational summary using neurodiversity-affirming language balanced with clinical terminology. DO NOT PROVIDE DEFINITIVE DIAGNOSES. This section is to provide a summary of characteristics that assists the clinician to provide a diagnosis. Examples:] [Option 1 - ASD characteristics observed: "Based on today's structured observational assessment using ADOS-2 Module 1, [CHILD NAME] demonstrated a pattern of differences in social communication and reciprocal social interaction, along with restricted/repetitive behaviours and interests, that are consistent with an autistic neurotype / autism spectrum presentation. Specifically, [he/she/they] showed [list key observations across domains]. These observations should be integrated with developmental history, caregiver report, and other assessment data to inform the clinician’s diagnostic formulation."] [Option 2 - Mixed presentation: "While [CHILD NAME] demonstrated some features associated with autism (e.g., [list specific observations]), other areas appeared more typical for [his/her/their] developmental level (e.g., [list areas]). Further integration with comprehensive developmental history, caregiver interview, and additional assessment measures is required by the clinician to determine if the pattern of differences meets criteria for autism spectrum disorder."] [Option 3 - Below threshold: "Although [CHILD NAME] showed some individual features that can be associated with autism (e.g., [list]), the overall pattern of behaviours observed during today's assessment did not reach the threshold typically associated with autism spectrum disorder on the ADOS-2 Module 1. [His/Her/Their] social communication skills, reciprocal social interaction, and play skills appeared broadly within developmental expectations, with [describe notable strengths]. These findings will be considered alongside other assessment data to help inform the clinician’s diagnosis."] **ADOS-2 Module 1 Algorithm Scores (if available):** [If scores have been calculated, include here:] Social Affect (SA) Total: [X] Restricted and Repetitive Behavior (RRB) Total: [X] Overall Total (SA + RRB): [X] ADOS-2 Classification: [autism / autism spectrum / non-spectrum] Comparison Score: [1-10] ([minimal / low / moderate / high level of autism spectrum-related symptoms]) [If scores not yet calculated, print: "Formal scoring and classification will be completed following the session using the ADOS-2 algorithm and will be integrated into the comprehensive diagnostic report."] **RISK, SAFETY, AND CONSENT** **Risk and Safety:** [If no concerns, print: "No immediate safety concerns were identified during today's assessment."] [If concerns present: Briefly describe any risk behaviours, safety concerns, elopement attempts, aggression, or self-injurious behaviours, and note actions taken or still to be taken. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Child's Engagement:** [Describe the child's apparent willingness to participate, response to breaks and boundaries, and overall tolerance of the assessment process, e.g., tolerated assessment well overall / showed some distress but was able to continue with breaks / required shortened session due to distress. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **PLAN AND NEXT STEPS** **Further Assessment:** [Describe what additional assessment components are needed or planned, as stated by the clinician or caregiver. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Feedback Session:** [Note plan for sharing results with family in a Feedback Session, as stated by the clinician or caregiver. Include time and date for that session, if known. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Additional Clinical Notes:** [Note any factors that may have influenced performance, e.g., child's illness, sleep deprivation, unfamiliar environment, caregiver anxiety, need for additional observation, or any modifications to standard administration that were necessary. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: ADOS-2 Module 2 Assessment Summary
description: A comprehensive observational summary template for the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition (ADOS-2) Module 2. This module is
prompt:
(The template below is designed for psychologists administering the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition, also known as the ADOS-2, Module 2 assessment. This is a standardised, semi-structured, play-based observational assessment designed to evaluate social communication, reciprocal social interaction, play, and restricted/repetitive behaviours in children who use phrase speech but are not yet verbally fluent. Using the transcript, contextual notes, and observations from the session, you will generate a detailed summary of behavioural observations made during the standardised assessment activities. The summary should capture the child's presentation, behaviours across all domains, and clinical impressions in a way that balances neurodiversity-affirming language with standardised ADOS-2 terminology. Use the DSM-5-TR criteria and ADOS-2 framework when describing observations. Organise the summary under the following headings.)
#### ADOS-2 Module 2 Algorithm: [Specify: Algorithm 1 (younger than 5 years) / Algorithm 2 (5 years or older). If unknown, print: “Unable to determine.”] Child Information: * Child Name: [Print the client's first and last name.] * Date of Birth: [Print the client's date of birth in Day Month, Year format.] * Chronological Age: [Print the client's age on the day of the assessment in Years, Months format.] * Date of Assessment: [Print the date of the assessment in Day Month, Year format.] Attendance: [CHILD NAME] attended the session with [CAREGIVER(S)]. Initial Presentation: [Describe the child's presentation on arrival, e.g., calm / curious / anxious / distressed / engaged / withdrawn / tired. Describe the child’s engagement with the clinician on entry, e.g., approached readily / stayed close to caregiver / avoided eye contact / needed encouragement to enter the assessment room. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] BEHAVIOURAL OBSERVATIONS DURING ADOS-2 MODULE 2 **LANGUAGE AND COMMUNICATION** **Overall Expressive Language Level:** [Describe the child's expressive language, e.g., uses short phrases (2-3 words) / uses longer phrases with some sentence structure / emerging sentence use but not yet fluent / primarily phrase speech with limited elaboration. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Overall Level of Non-Echoed Language:** [Describe the child’s spontaneous, meaningful language use, e.g., used flexible, meaningful phrase speech / mix of spontaneous and echoed language / primarily echoed or scripted phrases. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Speech Abnormalities Associated with Autism:** [Describe any unusual qualities of speech from the child, e.g., no notable speech abnormalities / unusual intonation or prosody / repetitive or stereotyped language / idiosyncratic phrasing. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Immediate Echolalia:** [Describe any immediate repetition of words or phrases by the child, e.g., no echolalia observed / occasional echolalia / frequent immediate repetition of clinician's speech. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Stereotyped/Idiosyncratic Use of Words or Phrases:** [Describe any unusual, repetitive, or context-inappropriate use of language by the child, e.g., no unusual language patterns / some repetitive phrases / idiosyncratic or scripted language observed / delayed echolalia. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Conversation:** [Describe the child's ability to engage in conversational exchanges, e.g., engaged in reciprocal exchanges with some turn-taking / responded to questions but did not elaborate or initiate topics / limited conversational reciprocity / difficulty maintaining conversational flow. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Reporting of Events:** [Describe the child's ability to describe past events or experiences, e.g., provided clear descriptions of events when prompted / gave brief responses with limited detail / difficulty reporting events coherently / required significant prompting to describe experiences. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Asking for Information:** [Describe the child's use of questions, e.g., spontaneously asked questions to gain information / asked some questions with prompting / rarely or did not ask questions of clinician. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Descriptive, Conventional, Instrumental, or Informational Gestures:** [Describe the range and quality of gestures by the child, e.g., used varied gestures including pointing, showing, descriptive gestures / limited gesture repertoire / minimal or absent gesture use / gestures not well-integrated with language. (Note: Do not describe the use of gestures unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **RECIPROCAL SOCIAL INTERACTION** **Unusual Eye Contact:** [Describe the child’s patterns of eye contact, e.g., frequent and well-modulated eye contact / brief and inconsistent / fleeting glances only / reduced eye contact during social bids / atypical quality of gaze. (Note: Do not describe eye contact unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **Responsive Social Smile:** [Describe the child’s social smiling in response to others, e.g., readily smiled in response to social bids / some social smiling but limited / minimal responsive smiling / smiles not clearly socially directed. (Note: Do not describe social smiling unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **Facial Expressions Directed to Others:** [Describe the range and social directedness of facial expressions, e.g., varied and socially directed facial expressions / limited range of expressions / neutral or restricted affect / expressions not clearly directed to others. (Note: Do not describe facial expressions unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **Integration of Gaze and Other Behaviours During Social Overtures:** [Describe the child’s coordination of eye contact with vocalisations, gestures, or affect during social bids, e.g., well-integrated coordination of gaze, gesture, and vocalisation / some integration but inconsistent / limited integration / isolated behaviours without coordination. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Shared Enjoyment in Interaction:** [Describe the child's expression and sharing of positive affect, e.g., clearly shared enjoyment with clinician/caregiver through gaze, smiles, and vocalisations / showed pleasure but did not consistently share with others / limited or absent shared enjoyment. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Response to Name:** [Describe the child's response when name was called, e.g., responded promptly and consistently / required repeated prompts / inconsistent response / delayed or minimal response to name. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Requesting:** [Describe how the child made requests, e.g., used verbal requests appropriately / combined verbal and gestural requests / primarily gestural requests / limited or unusual requesting behaviours. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Giving:** [Describe instances of the child giving objects to clinician/caregiver, e.g., spontaneously gave objects in social context / gave objects when prompted / limited or no giving behaviour observed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Showing:** [Describe instances of the child showing objects to share interest, e.g., spontaneously showed objects to clinician/caregiver / showed objects when prompted / limited or no showing behaviour observed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Amount of Reciprocal Social Communication:** [Describe the overall level of back-and-forth social engagement by the child, e.g., sustained reciprocal exchanges / brief exchanges that required support / limited reciprocity / minimal or absent reciprocal communication. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Overall Quality of Rapport:** [Describe the nature of the relationship established between the child and the clinician, e.g., warm and comfortable rapport / some rapport established with support / limited rapport / minimal social connection / rapport took time to establish. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **PLAY** **Functional Play with Objects:** [Describe the child's use of toys in conventional ways, e.g., demonstrated varied and appropriate functional play / some functional play / primarily exploratory or repetitive manipulation of objects. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Imagination/Creativity:** [Describe evidence of pretend or symbolic play, e.g., engaged in creative pretend play with varied themes / engaged in simple pretend actions with support / limited imaginative elements / no pretend play observed / play remained concrete. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Quality of Social Overtures:** [Within play contexts, describe social quality, e.g., used play as a context for social interaction / some social engagement during play / play was solitary with limited social component. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Flexibility and Range of Play:** [Describe the variety and adaptability of play, e.g., demonstrated flexible and varied play across activities / some rigidity or repetitiveness in play themes / limited play repertoire / highly restricted or repetitive play. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **RESTRICTED AND REPETITIVE BEHAVIOURS** **Unusual Sensory Interest in Play Material/Person:** [Describe atypical sensory exploration by the child, e.g., no unusual sensory interests / some sensory seeking (visual inspection, sniffing, mouthing) / pronounced sensory interest that interfered with engagement / sensory avoidance observed. Further integration with comprehensive developmental history, caregiver interview, and additional assessment measures is required by the clinician to determine if the pattern of differences meets criteria for autism spectrum disorder."] [Option 3 - Below threshold: "Although [CHILD NAME] showed some individual features that can be associated with autism (e.g., [list]), the overall pattern of behaviours observed during today's assessment did not reach the threshold typically associated with autism spectrum disorder on the ADOS-2 Module 2. [His/Her/Their] social communication skills, reciprocal social interaction, and play skills appeared broadly within developmental expectations, with [describe notable strengths]. These findings will be considered alongside other assessment data to help inform the clinician's diagnosis."] **ADOS-2 Module 2 Algorithm Scores (if available):** [If scores have been calculated, include here:] Algorithm Used: [Algorithm 1 (younger than 5 years) / Algorithm 2 (5 years or older)] Social Affect (SA) Total: [X] Restricted and Repetitive Behavior (RRB) Total: [X] Overall Total (SA + RRB): [X] ADOS-2 Classification: [autism / autism spectrum / non-spectrum] Comparison Score: [1-10] ([minimal / low / moderate / high level of autism spectrum-related symptoms]) [If scores not yet calculated, print: "Formal scoring and classification will be completed following the session using the ADOS-2 algorithm and will be integrated into the comprehensive diagnostic report."] **RISK, SAFETY, AND CONSENT** **Risk and Safety:** [If no concerns, print: "No immediate safety concerns were identified during today's assessment."] [If concerns present: Briefly describe any risk behaviours, safety concerns, elopement attempts, aggression, or self-injurious behaviours, and note actions taken or still to be taken. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **Child's Engagement:** [Describe the child's apparent willingness to participate, response to breaks and boundaries, and overall tolerance of the assessment process, e.g., tolerated assessment well overall / showed some distress but was able to continue with breaks / required shortened session due to distress. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: “Unable to determine from available information.”)] **PLAN AND NEXT STEPS** **Further Assessment:** [Describe what additional assessment components are needed or planned, as stated by the clinician or caregiver. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Feedback Session:** [Note plan for sharing results with family in a Feedback Session, as stated by the clinician or caregiver. Include time and date for that session, if known. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Additional Clinical Notes:** [Note any factors that may have influenced performance, e.g., child's illness, sleep deprivation, unfamiliar environment, caregiver anxiety, need for additional observation, or any modifications to standard administration that were necessary. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: ADOS-2 Module 3 Assessment Summary
description: A comprehensive observational summary template for the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition (ADOS-2) Module 3. This module is
prompt:
(The template below is designed for psychologists administering the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition, also known as the ADOS-2, Module 3 assessment. This is a standardised, semi-structured observational assessment designed to evaluate social communication, reciprocal social interaction, imaginative play, and restricted/repetitive behaviours in verbally fluent children and young adolescents. Using the transcript, contextual notes, and observations from the session, you will generate a detailed summary of behavioural observations made during the standardised assessment activities. The summary should capture the child's presentation, behaviours across all domains, and clinical impressions in a way that balances neurodiversity-affirming language with standardised ADOS-2 terminology. Use the DSM-5-TR criteria and ADOS-2 framework when describing observations. Organise the summary under the following headings.)
#### ADOS-2 Module 3 Child Information: * Child Name: [Print the client's first and last name.] * Date of Birth: [Print the client's date of birth in Day Month, Year format.] * Chronological Age: [Print the client's age on the day of the assessment in Years, Months format.] * Date of Assessment: [Print the date of the assessment in Day Month, Year format.] Attendance: [CHILD NAME] attended the session with [CAREGIVER(S)]. Initial Presentation: [Describe the child's presentation on arrival, e.g., calm / curious / anxious / distressed / engaged / withdrawn / tired. Describe engagement with the clinician on entry, e.g., approached readily / separated from caregiver easily / stayed close to caregiver / avoided eye contact / needed encouragement to enter the assessment room. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] BEHAVIOURAL OBSERVATIONS DURING ADOS-2 MODULE 3 **LANGUAGE AND COMMUNICATION** **Overall Level of Non-Echoed Language (A1):** [Describe the child's spontaneous, flexible use of language, e.g., used fluent and varied language across contexts / some repetitive or scripted elements alongside spontaneous speech / frequently used stereotyped or formulaic utterances with limited flexible language. Note the degree to which the child's language is self-generated versus echoed, scripted, or overly formal. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Speech Abnormalities Associated with Autism — Intonation, Volume, Rhythm, and Rate (A2):** [Describe any atypical qualities in the child's speech, e.g., no notable speech abnormalities / mildly unusual prosody or intonation / markedly unusual intonation, volume, rhythm, or rate of speech that is atypical for the child's age and language level. Note any high-pitched voice, unusual stress patterns, or mechanical or monotone quality. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Immediate Echolalia (A3):** [Describe any immediate repetition of words or phrases by the child, e.g., no immediate echolalia observed / occasional repetition of the clinician's words or phrases / frequent immediate echoing of clinician's speech. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Stereotyped/Idiosyncratic Use of Words or Phrases (A4):** [Describe any unusual, repetitive, or context-inappropriate use of language by the child, e.g., no stereotyped or idiosyncratic language observed / some repetitive or formulaic phrases / frequent use of odd, idiosyncratic, or scripted language, including delayed echolalia, neologisms, pronoun errors, or arbitrary associations between words and meanings. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Offers Information (A5):** [Describe the degree to which the child spontaneously offered information, elaborated on topics, or provided relevant details during conversation and activities, e.g., regularly offered information spontaneously and with relevance / occasionally volunteered information / rarely or never spontaneously offered information without prompting. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Asks for Information (A6):** [Describe the child's use of questions directed to the clinician to gain information or maintain conversation, e.g., spontaneously asked relevant questions / asked occasional questions, primarily when directly prompted / rarely or did not ask questions of the clinician. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Reporting of Events (A7):** [Describe the child's ability to describe past or non-routine events, e.g., provided clear, coherent, and detailed descriptions of events when prompted / gave brief or vague responses with limited elaboration / had difficulty reporting events in a meaningful or organised way / required significant prompting to produce any account of events. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Conversation (A8):** [Describe the child's ability to engage in reciprocal conversational exchanges, e.g., engaged in fluid, reciprocal conversation with appropriate turn-taking and topic maintenance / responded to questions but did not elaborate or initiate topics / difficulty sustaining conversational flow or responding to conversational leads from the clinician. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Descriptive, Conventional, Instrumental, or Informational Gestures (A9):** [Describe the range and quality of gestures used by the child, e.g., used varied and contextually appropriate gestures (e.g., pointing, nodding, demonstrating) integrated with speech / limited gesture repertoire / minimal or absent gesture use / gestures not well-integrated with verbal communication. (Note: Do not describe use of gestures unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **RECIPROCAL SOCIAL INTERACTION** **Unusual Eye Contact (B1):** [Describe the child's patterns of eye contact, e.g., frequent and well-modulated eye contact that improved across the session / brief and inconsistent / fleeting glances only / reduced or avoidant eye contact during social bids / atypical quality of gaze. (Note: Do not describe unusual eye contact unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **Facial Expressions Directed to Others (B2):** [Describe the range and social directedness of the child's facial expressions, e.g., varied and appropriate facial expressions directed to the clinician / limited range of expressions / neutral or flat affect / expressions not clearly directed to or coordinated with others during interaction. (Note: Do not describe use of facial expressions unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **Language Production and Linked Non-Verbal Communication (B3):** [Describe the degree to which the child's verbal communication was integrated with non-verbal behaviours such as gaze, gesture, and facial expression, e.g., well-integrated coordination of language with non-verbal communicative behaviours / some integration but inconsistent / limited integration between verbal and non-verbal channels / language and non-verbal behaviours appeared largely disconnected. (Note: Do not describe the coordination of non-verbal behaviours unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")]. **Shared Enjoyment in Interaction (B4):** [Describe the child's expression and sharing of positive affect during the session, e.g., clearly shared enjoyment with the clinician through gaze, smiles, and vocalisations / showed pleasure during activities but did not consistently direct this to others / limited or absent sharing of positive affect with the clinician. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Comments on Others' Emotions / Empathy (B5):** [Describe the child's awareness and responsiveness to others' emotions, e.g., demonstrated clear understanding of and empathy for others' emotional states / communicated some understanding of emotions in others / limited or absent spontaneous comments on others' emotions or empathic responses. Note any specific examples from activities such as the Cartoons or Emotions tasks. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Insight into Typical Social Situations and Relationships (B6):** [Describe the child's understanding of social relationships, conventions, and situations, e.g., demonstrated good insight into typical social relationships and contexts / showed some understanding of social situations / limited insight into typical social conventions or the nature of relationships. Note any relevant responses from activities such as Friends, Relationships, and Marriage; Loneliness; or Social Difficulties and Annoyance. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Quality of Social Overtures (B7):** [Describe the appropriateness and integration of the child's social bids, e.g., integrated and appropriate social overtures / some social interest but overtures were poorly integrated or unusual in quality / minimal or absent social overtures. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Amount of Social Overtures / Maintenance of Attention (B8):** [Describe the frequency and persistence of the child's social initiations and sustained engagement, e.g., frequent social initiations and maintained engagement with the clinician / occasional initiations / rarely or did not initiate social interaction or sustain attention to social interaction. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Quality of Social Response (B9):** [Describe how the child responded to the clinician's social bids, e.g., responsive and appropriate to social overtures / inconsistently responsive / minimal or absent response to social bids from the clinician. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Amount of Reciprocal Social Communication (B10):** [Describe the overall level of back-and-forth social engagement across the session, e.g., sustained reciprocal social exchanges / brief exchanges that required considerable clinician support / limited reciprocity / minimal or absent reciprocal communication. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Overall Quality of Rapport (B11):** [Describe the nature of the relationship established between the child and the clinician across the session, e.g., warm and comfortable rapport that developed readily / some rapport established with support and time / rapport improved as the session progressed / limited rapport / minimal social connection. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **PLAY AND IMAGINATION** **Imagination / Creativity (C1):** [Describe evidence of imaginative and creative thinking observed across relevant activities, including Make-Believe Play, Joint Interactive Play, Telling a Story From a Book, Cartoons, and Creating a Story, e.g., demonstrated spontaneous and varied imaginative play with creative elaboration / showed some imaginative elements with support / limited imagination or creativity / play and storytelling remained concrete, repetitive, or reliant on clinician scaffolding. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **STEREOTYPED BEHAVIOURS AND RESTRICTED INTERESTS** **Unusual Sensory Interest in Play Material / Person (D1):** [Describe any atypical sensory exploration by the child, e.g., no unusual sensory interests observed / some sensory seeking behaviours (e.g., visual inspection, sniffing, mouthing of objects) / pronounced sensory interest in materials or the clinician's person that interfered with engagement. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Hand and Finger and Other Complex Mannerisms (D2):** [Describe any repetitive motor movements or complex mannerisms, e.g., no unusual mannerisms observed / occasional hand flapping, finger posturing, or other body movements / frequent and prominent motor mannerisms. Note any facial grimacing or other atypical movements if observed. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Self-Injurious Behaviour (D3):** [Describe any self-harm behaviours, e.g., no self-injurious behaviour observed / occasional instances (specify) / frequent self-injury requiring intervention. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Excessive Interest in or References to Unusual or Highly Specific Topics or Objects, or Repetitive Behaviours (D4):** [Describe any intense, narrow, or unusual interests and repetitive behaviours, e.g., no excessive or unusual interests observed / showed notable focus on specific topics or objects, including frequency and nature of references / insistence on sameness or restricted play themes. Note any repeated references to specific characters, topics, or media across activities. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Compulsions or Rituals (D5):** [Describe any ritualistic or compulsive behaviours, e.g., no compulsions or rituals observed / some rigid adherence to routines or sequences / notable compulsive behaviours or rituals that interfered with assessment activities. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **REGULATION, ATTENTION, AND BEHAVIOUR** **Overactivity / Agitation (E1):** [Describe the child's overall activity level and any signs of agitation, e.g., age-appropriate activity level throughout / some restlessness or fidgeting that was manageable / markedly overactive or agitated in a way that interfered with assessment activities. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Tantrums, Aggression, or Disruptive Behaviour (E2):** [Note any tantrums, aggressive behaviour, or significant disruption during the session, including context and how managed. If none present, state: "No significant behavioural concerns noted during assessment." (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Anxiety (E3):** [Describe any signs of anxiety observed during the session, e.g., no notable anxiety observed / mild anxiety at the start of the session that settled as rapport was established / notable anxiety across multiple activities that affected the child's engagement and performance. Include triggers and any observable behavioural or physical signs if relevant. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **CAREGIVER INTERACTION AND REPORT** **Caregiver's Interaction Style:** [Describe how the caregiver engaged with the child during the session, e.g., warm and responsive / highly directive / anxious or worried / observant but allowed child independence / required guidance to step back at times. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Caregiver Reports During Session:** [Briefly note any relevant information shared by the caregiver about communication, social interaction, repetitive behaviours, sensory responses, daily functioning, or developmental concerns. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **CLINICAL IMPRESSIONS AND SUMMARY** **Summary of Observed Strengths:** [Describe positive observations and capacities, e.g., demonstrated fluent verbal language; engaged in reciprocal conversation in preferred topics; showed enjoyment during play activities; demonstrated some imaginative and creative thinking; showed emerging understanding of others' emotions; rapport improved across the session, etc. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Summary of Observed Differences and Areas of Support Need:** [Describe patterns consistent with autism characteristics, e.g., differences in social reciprocity and social communication including reduced or atypical eye contact, limited spontaneous social initiation, reduced integration of non-verbal and verbal communication, limited gesture use, restricted range of imaginative play, repetitive references to specific topics or interests, and/or presence of repetitive behaviours or sensory sensitivities. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Overall Clinical Impressions:** [Provide an integrated observational summary using neurodiversity-affirming language balanced with clinical terminology. DO NOT PROVIDE DEFINITIVE DIAGNOSES. This section is to provide a summary of characteristics that assists the clinician to provide a diagnosis. Examples:] [Option 1 — ASD characteristics observed: "Based on today's structured observational assessment using ADOS-2 Module 3, [CHILD NAME] demonstrated a pattern of differences in social communication and reciprocal social interaction, along with restricted/repetitive behaviours and interests, that are consistent with an autistic neurotype / autism spectrum presentation. Specifically, [he/she/they] showed [list key observations across domains]. These observations should be integrated with developmental history, caregiver report, and other assessment data to inform the clinician's diagnostic formulation."] [Option 2 — Mixed presentation: "While [CHILD NAME] demonstrated some features associated with autism (e.g., [list specific observations]), other areas appeared more typical for [his/her/their] developmental level (e.g., [list areas]). Further integration with comprehensive developmental history, caregiver interview, and additional assessment measures is required by the clinician to determine if the pattern of differences meets criteria for autism spectrum disorder."] [Option 3 — Below threshold: "Although [CHILD NAME] showed some individual features that can be associated with autism (e.g., [list]), the overall pattern of behaviours observed during today's assessment did not reach the threshold typically associated with autism spectrum disorder on the ADOS-2 Module 3. [His/Her/Their] social communication skills, reciprocal social interaction, and imaginative play appeared broadly within developmental expectations, with [describe notable strengths]. These findings will be considered alongside other assessment data to help inform the clinician's diagnosis."] **ADOS-2 Module 3 Algorithm Scores (if available):** [If scores have been calculated, include here:] Social Affect (SA) Total: [X] Restricted and Repetitive Behaviour (RRB) Total: [X] Overall Total (SA + RRB): [X] ADOS-2 Classification: [autism / autism spectrum / non-spectrum] Comparison Score: [1–10] ([minimal / low / moderate / high level of autism spectrum-related symptoms]) [If scores not yet calculated, print: "Formal scoring and classification will be completed following the session using the ADOS-2 algorithm and will be integrated into the comprehensive diagnostic report."] **RISK, SAFETY, AND CONSENT** **Risk and Safety:** [If no concerns, print: "No immediate safety concerns were identified during today's assessment."] [If concerns present: Briefly describe any risk behaviours, safety concerns, elopement attempts, aggression, or self-injurious behaviours, and note actions taken or still to be taken. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Child's Engagement:** [Describe the child's apparent willingness to participate, response to transitions between activities, and overall tolerance of the assessment process, e.g., tolerated assessment well overall / showed some distress but was able to continue / required shortened session due to distress. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **PLAN AND NEXT STEPS** **Further Assessment:** [Describe what additional assessment components are needed or planned, as stated by the clinician or caregiver. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Feedback Session:** [Note plan for sharing results with family in a Feedback Session. Include time and date for that session, if known. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Additional Clinical Notes:** [Note any factors that may have influenced performance, e.g., child's illness, sleep deprivation, unfamiliar environment, caregiver anxiety, need for additional observation, or any modifications to standard administration that were necessary. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: ADOS-2 Module 4 Assessment Summary
description: A comprehensive observational summary template for the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition (ADOS-2) Module 4. This module is
prompt:
(The template below is designed for psychologists administering the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule, Second Edition, also known as the ADOS-2, Module 4 assessment. This is a standardised, semi-structured observational assessment designed to evaluate social communication, reciprocal social interaction, imagination/creativity, and restricted/repetitive behaviours in verbally fluent older adolescents and adults. Using the transcript, contextual notes, and observations from the session, you will generate a detailed summary of behavioural observations made during the standardised assessment activities. The summary should capture the individual's presentation, behaviours across all domains, and clinical impressions in a way that balances neurodiversity-affirming language with standardised ADOS-2 terminology. Use the DSM-5-TR criteria and ADOS-2 framework when describing observations. Note that there may be a parent, caregiver, or other support person in attendance during the assessment, and any relevant comments they make should be noted. Organise the summary under the following headings.)
#### ADOS-2 Module 4 Individual Information: * Individual Name: [Print the client's first and last name.] * Date of Birth: [Print the client's date of birth in Day Month, Year format.] * Chronological Age: [Print the client's age on the day of the assessment in Years, Months format.] * Date of Assessment: [Print the date of the assessment in Day Month, Year format.] Attendance: [INDIVIDUAL NAME] attended the session with [SUPPORT PERSON(S) / note if attended alone]. Initial Presentation: [Describe the individual's presentation on arrival, e.g., calm / curious / anxious / engaged / withdrawn / guarded. Describe engagement with the clinician on entry, e.g., approached readily / required encouragement / made eye contact readily / avoided eye contact / appeared socially at ease / appeared socially tentative. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] BEHAVIOURAL OBSERVATIONS DURING ADOS-2 MODULE 4 **LANGUAGE AND COMMUNICATION** **Overall Level of Non-Echoed Language (A1):** [Describe the complexity and spontaneity of the individual's spoken language during the session, e.g., used sentences correctly including some complex speech with two or more clauses / some relatively complex speech but with recurrent grammatical errors not associated with dialect / non-echoed speech mostly comprised of utterances of at least three words but without complex language / non-echoed language made up mostly of simple phrases. Note that the rating should reflect the majority of the individual's utterances, not merely the best ones. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Speech Abnormalities Associated with Autism — Intonation, Volume, Rhythm, and Rate (A2):** [Describe any atypical qualities in the individual's speech delivery, e.g., appropriately varying intonation, reasonable volume, and normal speech rate with regular rhythm / slight variation in pitch and tone, relatively flat or exaggerated intonation but not obviously peculiar, or slightly unusual volume or rate / speech that is markedly abnormal in intonation, volume, rhythm, or rate, such as slow and halting delivery, inappropriately rapid speech, jerky or irregular rhythm, odd intonation, or consistently abnormal volume. Note that articulation difficulties are not coded here. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Immediate Echolalia (A3):** [Describe any immediate repetition of words or phrases by the individual, e.g., does not repeat others' speech / occasional echoing / echoing words and phrases regularly but with some spontaneous language / speech primarily consisting of immediate echolalia. Note that repetitions serving as a lead-in to a response or used as a memory aid in specific tasks are excluded from this rating. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Stereotyped/Idiosyncratic Use of Words or Phrases (A4):** [Describe any unusual, repetitive, or context-inappropriate use of language, e.g., rarely or never uses stereotyped or idiosyncratic words or phrases / use of words or phrases tends to be more repetitive or formal than expected but not oddly so, or occasional stereotyped utterances alongside substantial spontaneous and flexible language / often uses stereotyped phrases or odd words alongside other language / frequently uses odd or stereotyped speech with rare non-stereotyped spontaneous speech. Note any instances of delayed echolalia, neologisms, pronoun errors across persons, or arbitrary associations between words and meanings. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Offering Information (A5):** [Describe the degree to which the individual spontaneously offered personal information new to the clinician, e.g., spontaneously offered information about their thoughts, feelings, or experiences on several occasions / occasionally offered information spontaneously about their thoughts, feelings, or experiences / rarely or never offered information spontaneously, except about circumscribed interests or preoccupations, or offered information only about facts or general knowledge. Note that comments about facts alone are not coded here, and lists of multiple factors are counted as one instance unless part of compulsive listing behaviour. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Asking for Information (A6):** [Describe the individual's spontaneous expression of interest in the clinician's thoughts, feelings, or experiences, e.g., asked the clinician about their thoughts, feelings, or experiences several times / occasionally, or at least once, asked the clinician about their thoughts, feelings, or experiences / responded appropriately to the clinician's comments about their own thoughts, feelings, or experiences but did not spontaneously inquire about them / rarely or never expressed interest in the clinician's thoughts, feelings, or experiences. Note that questions about test materials or unrelated topics are not coded here. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Reporting of Events (A7):** [Describe the individual's ability to select and describe an event comprehensibly without requiring specific probes, e.g., reported a specific non-routine event (e.g., a holiday, a trip) that is not part of a preoccupation, providing a reasonable account without specific probes / provided a reasonable account of a routine event (e.g., a typical daily routine) without specific probes, including accounts from the Demonstration Task / provided an account of routine or non-routine events but dependent on specific probes, or described only an event that seemed unlikely to be real / inconsistent or insufficient responses even to specific probes. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Conversation (A8):** [Describe the individual's ability to engage in reciprocal conversational exchanges, e.g., conversation flowed readily building on the clinician's dialogue, with the individual's speech providing both a response and additional content that allowed the clinician to continue (i.e., sequences of at least four elements) / speech included some spontaneous elaboration or conversational leads for the clinician's benefit, but less than expected for the individual's language level or limited in flexibility / little reciprocal conversation sustained by the individual, who may have followed their own train of thought rather than participating in an interchange / little spontaneous communicative speech with very limited responses to conversational initiations from the clinician. This rating should consider all opportunities for conversation, not merely the best. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Descriptive, Conventional, Instrumental, or Informational Gestures (A9):** [Describe the range and quality of gestures used by the individual, e.g., spontaneous use of several descriptive gestures that enact or represent objects or events, which may be typical or idiosyncratic but must be communicative, along with conventional or instrumental gestures / some spontaneous use of descriptive gestures but exaggerated or limited in range or context (e.g., occurring only during the Demonstration Task), or frequent use of conventional or instrumental gestures with rare or no descriptive gestures / some spontaneous use of informational, conventional, or instrumental gestures but rare or no descriptive gestures / no or minimal spontaneous use of gestures of any type. Note that pointing is included here as an instrumental gesture and is not coded separately in Module 4. (Note: Do not describe gesture use unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Emphatic or Emotional Gestures (A10):** [Describe the use of emphatic or emotional gestures by the individual, e.g., a variety of appropriate emphatic or emotional gestures well integrated with speech / some emphatic or emotional gestures but exaggerated or limited in frequency, appropriateness, integration, or style / odd, excessive, or awkwardly integrated emphatic or emotional gestures / no or minimal emphatic or emotional gestures. Note that the rating should be based on the timing of gestures as they occur during speech; integration of gesture with gaze is coded separately under Language Production and Linked Non-Verbal Communication or Quality of Social Overtures. (Note: Do not describe gesture use unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **RECIPROCAL SOCIAL INTERACTION** **Unusual Eye Contact (B1):** [Describe the individual's patterns of eye contact, e.g., appropriate, flexible, and socially modulated gaze used for a variety of communicative purposes and meshed with other forms of communication / poorly modulated eye contact that was limited in flexibility, appropriateness, or context when initiating, terminating, or regulating social interaction. Note whether eye contact improved as the session progressed, and note that eye contact between the individual and any support person present is not coded here. (Note: Do not describe eye contact unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Facial Expressions Directed to the Examiner (B2):** [Describe the range and social directedness of the individual's facial expressions, e.g., directed a range of appropriate facial expressions to the clinician to communicate affective states (e.g., enjoyment, frustration) or cognitive states (e.g., puzzlement, scepticism) / some direction of facial expressions to the clinician, such as expressions indicating emotional extremes, or an individual with a limited range of expressions who directs almost all of them to the clinician / did not direct appropriate facial expressions to the clinician. Note that facial expressions directed to objects, other people in the room, or that are undirected are not rated here; appropriate or slightly exaggerated expressions should be coded even if there are also odd expressions. (Note: Do not describe facial expressions unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of it, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the client's verbal content alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Language Production and Linked Non-Verbal Communication (B3):** [Describe the degree to which the individual's vocalisations were accompanied by coordinated non-verbal behaviours such as gaze, facial expression, and gesture, e.g., vocalisations usually accompanied by subtle and socially appropriate changes in body language, eye contact, and facial expressions / vocalisations accompanied by abnormal, limited, or less than usual frequency or range of gesture, gaze, and facial expression, or use of one modality almost exclusively / little or no non-verbal communication linked with vocalisations. Note that this item should be coded based on the vocalisations used, reflecting the most typical occurrences rather than merely the best ones. (Note: Do not describe the coordination of non-verbal behaviours unless the transcript contains explicit clinician observations of this, or contextual or clinical notes describe it directly. Do not infer from the verbal content of the client's speech alone. If no such evidence is present, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Shared Enjoyment in Interaction (B4):** [Describe the individual's directed expression of pleasure during the session, e.g., showed definite pleasure appropriate to context during interactive participation or conversation with the clinician across more than one task or conversational topic / showed some pleasure appropriate to context during interactions with the clinician, or showed definite pleasure during one interaction / showed little or no expressed pleasure during interaction with the clinician, but showed pleasure in speech, actions, or non-interactive components / little or no expressed pleasure during the evaluation. Note that this item reflects the individual's ability to indicate pleasure to the clinician, not their general emotional state. (If unable to make a judgment based on the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, print: "Unable to determine from available information.")] **Communication of Own Affect (B5):** [Describe the individual's ability to convey their own emotions using words, facial expressions, tone of voice, vocalisations, and body language including gestures, e.g., communicated a range of their own emotions effectively across descriptions and spontaneous comments / some description of experiencing several emotions but with limited communication effectiveness, or effective communication of at least one emotion / some communication about at least one emotion / minimal or no communication of their own affect. Note that descriptions elicited during the socioemotional questions (e.g., Emotions task) as well as spontaneous comments may be rated here, and that the rating should reflect the range of emotions and the effectiveness of communication rather than the specific emotions present.
