Let me know if you have any questions. See you next time!

\[Clinician Name]

title: Client Session Summary FOR PARENTS AND CAREGIVERS
description: To be given to a PARENT OR CAREGIVER after a therapy session between a clinician and a child client, this template allows the clinician to easily
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by clinicians providing therapy to a child client. The document is to be provided to the parent or caregiver of the child client following the session. Use casual, informal language, but keep the tone polite, professional, and warm. Do not use jargon and technical terms. Avoid including direct quotes. Make sure the wording used feels supportive, non-judgmental, easy to understand, and uses neuro-diversity affirming language where appropriate, in helping the parent or caregiver understand the work that the clinician is doing with the child client. The main focus of this document is to summarise the work being done by the clinician with the child client. It is not about summarising specific things discussed by the child client, unless that child client understands that such things will be discussed with the parent or caregiver. The template will strictly adhere to the following format:)

Hi [Parent or Caregiver’s First Name],

This is a brief summary of my session with [Client’s First Name] today.

[summary] Session Summary


[Provide a clear and concise summary of the main interventions, strategies, and topics given or discussed by the clinician. Only mention things that were described by the child client in session if that child client knows and understands that this information is being shared with the parent or caregiver. If you are unsure about whether the child understands that the information will be shared, do not include it in your output. YOU MUST RESPECT THE CHILD CLIENT’S PRIVACY BY KEEPING EVERYTHING ELSE CONFIDENTIAL IF THERE HAS BEEN NO UNDERSTANDING IN THE SESSION THAT CERTAIN INFORMATION FROM THE CLIENT WILL BE SHARED. Keep it simple and easy to read, avoiding technical language. Write only one paragraph of less than six sentences.]

[activities_for_client_s_first_name_to_do] Activities For [Client’s First Name] To Do


* [List all the tasks, exercises, or reflections that the clinician has suggested, and that the child client has agreed to do before the next session. Be specific, and write them in a friendly, encouraging tone. These might include evidence-based self-help strategies, self-care activities, tasks that need to be completed (such as obtaining a new referral, visiting a different specialist), or anything else discussed. Remember that you are writing this to the parent or caregiver, so mention any ways that the parent or caregiver can help or encourage the child client to complete all the tasks, exercises, or reflections suggested by the clinician.]


[next_appointment] Next Appointment


[List details of the next appointment between the clinician and the child client, including time, date, and location. If no future appointment was made in the session, do not type the subheading title, and do leave this section blank.]

[Print a positive, affirming sentence that is encouraging and supportive of the child client, and includes a global summary of the value of the therapeutic work being undertaken between the clinician and the child client.]

If you have any questions, please feel free to get in touch.

[Clinician's First and Last Name.]

sections.label: Activities For [Client’s First Name] To Do

title: Clinical Case Review / Meeting Summary
description: For meetings with stakeholders in relation to a client / patient, this template is designed to create formal minutes for use in clinical records.
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by clinicians who attend a meeting with one or more key stakeholders who collectively work with an individual who has mental health challenges. The meeting and transcript may involve multiple speakers. Review the transcript and identify which speaker corresponds to who. When documenting statements, concerns or observations, clearly identify who made each point. Using the provided transcript and any contextual notes, you will generate a detailed, but concise summary of the meeting. Organise the summary under the following headings:)

[clinical_case_review_meeting_summary] Clinical Case Review / Meeting Summary

**Date:** [Print the date of the meeting in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

**Time:** [Print the time of the meeting in 24-hour time HH:MM format.]

**Client / Patient:** [Print the name of the key person / client being discussed in the meeting. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

**Location:** [Print the location of meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[meeting_attendees] Meeting Attendees

* [List all the meeting attendees. For each attendee, include their name, profession, organisation, contact details and detail their involvement with the client, if known.]

[apologies] Apologies

* [List all the people who were not able to attend the meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[agenda] Agenda

* [List the agenda items; or summarise the purpose of, or reason for, the meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[discussion_and_key_issues] Discussion And Key Issues

* [Provide detailed discussion points and key issues from the meeting e.g., issues regarding the client’s mental health challenges and care plan; relevant observations, insights, or updates shared by the stakeholder(s); any identified barriers or concerns related to the client’s progress or care; any differences in viewpoints, emphasis or goals between the stakeholders, etc. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[actions_and_key_decisions] Actions And Key Decisions

* [Provide a list of action items, agreements and decisions made. Detail any deadlines or timelines for these tasks if specified. Detail any clarifications or consensus on roles, responsibilities, or next steps (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[additional_notes] Additional Notes

* [Describe any supplementary comments, observations, or relevant context that emerged during the meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[next_meeting] Next Meeting

* **Date:** [Print the date of the next meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Time:** [Print the time of the next meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* **Location:** [Print the location of the next meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]


(Review the entire note to confirm that statements are consistently attributed to the correct person throughout the note. Correct any inconsistencies to maintain clear and accurate attribution.)

title: Cognitive Behaviour Therapy (CBT)
description: For sessions using a CBT framework, this template provides a four part CBT model formulation, a summary of a session with a structure consistent with
prompt:
​​(The template below is designed to be used by therapists working within a cognitive behavioural therapy, or CBT, framework with clients who present with a variety of mental health issues. Using the transcript from the session, you will generate a detailed summary of the session from a CBT perspective. Ensure the summary is concise and adheres to CBT terminology. Only include direct quotes from the transcript if they are pivotal to the therapeutic process. Organise the summary under the following headings.)



[presentation] Presenting Problem(s)

* \[Summarise the client's main concerns or difficulties discussed in the session. Highlight any relevant updates to symptoms, behaviours, or situations since the previous session. Detail the client’s current emotional state, and indicate the type and results of any assessments used by the therapist in session, e.g. Subjective Units of Distress, or SUDS; Depression Anxiety Stress Scale, or DASS; and others. Detail any relevant life events or current circumstances contributing to the client’s presentation.]



[session_agenda] Session Agenda

* \[Identify the key topics or goals agreed upon for discussion at the start of the session.]



[content] Session Content

* \[Review of homework: Client's progress with tasks from the previous session, including barriers and successes. (only include if mentioned in the transcript)]
* \[Key topics discussed: Core issues explored and how they relate to the client’s presentation and  treatment goals.]



[cbt_formulation] CBT Formulation

(Below is a four-part model Situation, Thoughts, Emotions, Behaviours. For each circumstance discussed, use the CBT model to describe the cycle, populating the table. Create as many tables as you need for each circumstance or situation) 

\[Situation: Describe the triggering external circumstances or event that started a cycle.]

\[Thoughts: Describe the automatic thoughts that occur in response to the situation.]

\[Emotions: The feelings that are generated by the thoughts]

\[Behaviours: The actions or reactions that result from the emotions and physical feelings. Note any feedback loops that cause further difficult situations]

| 
   Situation
 | 
   Thought
 | 
   Emotion
 | 
   Behaviour
 |
| ---------------------- | -------------------- | -------------------- | ---------------------- |
| 
   
          | 
   
        | 
   
        | 
   
          |

[unhelpful_thinking_style] Unhelpful Thinking Style

* \[Note any thoughts the client had that are associated with cognitive distortions. Use direct quotes from the transcript. Note if the therapist identified or challenged these thoughts in the transcript or not. Note if any more helpful alternative thoughts were developed, including a quote of the more helpful thought  (Use as many dot points as you need to detail thoughts and their associated unhelpful thinking style and more helpful alternative thoughts)]



[core_beliefs] Core beliefs

* \[Note any core beliefs, schemas or foundational beliefs, and any associated experiences, that were explored in the transcript. Use as many dot points as you need to detail core beliefs, and quotes if available. (only include if mentioned in the transcript)]



[interventions_used] Interventions Used

* \[Detail the specific CBT techniques or interventions applied (e.g., cognitive restructuring, thought records, behavioural activation and experiments, problem-solving, exposure, etc.). Summarise how these interventions were received and any feedback from the client, including level of understanding of the interventions shown by the client. Detail any notable shifts in thinking or behaviour expressed by client. Use as many dot points as needed to list separate interventions.]



[key_insights_or_themes] Key Insights or Themes

* \[Highlight important themes, insights, realisation or "aha moments" that emerged during the session. Include any shifts in the client’s understanding of their difficulties or progress toward therapy goals.]



[therapeutic_relationship] Therapeutic Relationship

* \[Briefly comment on the therapeutic alliance or dynamics observed in the session. Note any areas where rapport was strengthened or challenges arose.]



[homework_or_action_plan] Homework or Action Plan

* \[Describe homework or tasks assigned to the client, including rationale and expected outcomes. Note any feedback or concerns from the client about the assignments. (only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise leave blank)]



[risk] Risk Assessment

* \[Summarise any risk factors, such as self-harm or suicidal ideation. (if current suicidal thoughts or behaviours are present, highlight text in red). List protective factors and any action plans if risk is present. (only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise leave blank)]



[nextsteps] Next Steps

* \[Outline the preliminary agenda or goals for the next session, based on today's discussion. Provide the time, day and date of any future session agreed upon between the therapist and the client.]



[summary] Summary

* \[Provide a brief recap of key points from the session in no more than four sentences which summarise the session.]

title: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy for Insomnia (CBT-I)
description: For use by a clinician using a cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) framework, this template provides a detailed summary of a session
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by a clinician working within a cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia, or CBT-I, framework. Following analysis of a transcript of a therapy session between a clinician and a client, you will generate a detailed summary of that session. Use language and concepts consistent with the practice of evidence-based cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia. The template will follow the following format:)

[presenting_concerns_and_sleep_status_upd] Presenting Concerns and Sleep Status Update

[Describe the client’s self-reported sleep patterns since the last session, including: Sleep onset latency (time taken to fall asleep), frequency and duration of nocturnal awakenings, early morning awakenings, total sleep time and sleep efficiency (if sleep diary was reviewed), subjective sleep quality and daytime functioning (e.g., fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability), any significant changes or stressors affecting sleep (e.g., life events, medical concerns, changes in routine). Describe any other significant events or situations from the client’s life that the client mentions in the session, regardless of the perceived impact on the client’s sleep.]

[review_of_previous_session_and_homework] Review of Previous Session and Homework

[Describe the key topics discussed from the last session. Describe the client’s adherence to previously assigned strategies (e.g., sleep restriction, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, etc.). Describe challenges or barriers encountered in implementing any strategies. Describe insights or improvements noted by the client. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)]

[session_focus_and_interventions] Session Focus and Interventions

**Psychoeducation:** [Describe education and psychoeducation provided by the clinician. Describe any discussions on sleep homeostasis, circadian rhythms, sleep drive, or the impact of cognitive and behavioural factors on sleep. Describe any explanation of sleep restriction, stimulus control, relaxation techniques, or cognitive restructuring. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)]

**Cognitive Interventions:** [Describe any identification and restructuring of unhelpful thoughts about sleep (e.g., catastrophic thinking, excessive worry about consequences of poor sleep) discussed between the clinician and the client. Describe any reframing of sleep-related beliefs (e.g., “I must get eight hours of sleep to function”) discussed between the clinician and the client. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)]

**Behavioural Interventions:** [Describe any sleep restriction therapy (adjustments to sleep window) discussed between the clinician and the client. Describe any stimulus control strategies (e.g., getting out of bed when unable to sleep, reducing sleep-incompatible activities in bed like watching TV) discussed between the clinician and the client. Describe any relaxation and mindfulness techniques (e.g., progressive muscle relaxation, diaphragmatic breathing) discussed between the clinician and the client. Describe any sleep hygiene modifications discussed between the clinician and the client. Describe any conversations between the clinician and the client about addressing unhelpful daytime behaviours (e.g., managing naps, caffeine/alcohol use, exercise, and other lifestyle factors impacting sleep). (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)]

[client_response_and_engagement] Client Response and Engagement

[Describe the client’s reactions, insights, and level of motivation to implement changes. Describe the client’s emotional responses, challenges, or level of resistance to any suggested management strategy. Describe the client’s strengths and adaptive, helpful coping strategies.]

[homework_and_next_steps] Homework and Next Steps

* [Describe any specific homework assigned by the clinician (e.g., sleep diary completion, implementing stimulus control strategies, testing cognitive restructuring). Describe the goals for the next session (e.g., adjusting sleep schedule, further cognitive restructuring, enhancing relaxation strategies) discussed between the clinician and the client. Describe any plans for overcoming anticipated barriers discussed between the clinician and the client.]
* [Print the time and date of any future session between the clinician and the client.]

[clinical_impressions_and_plan] Clinical Impressions and Plan

[Describe the clinician’s observations related to the client’s progress based on the client’s response to treatment. Describe any adjustments to the treatment plan made by the clinician. Describe any additional considerations or referrals mentioned by the clinician. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)]

sections.label: Presenting Concerns and Sleep Status Update
Review of Previous Session and Homework

title: Compassion Focused Therapy (CFT)
description: For clinicians working within a CFT framework, this template provides a summary of a session with a client with a structure consistent with
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by therapists working within a Compassion Focused Therapy, or CFT, framework with clients who present with a variety of mental health issues. Ensure the summary uses Compassion Focused Therapy terminology and concepts. Focus on capturing the key CFT elements including the three affect regulation systems and client progress in developing compassion for self and others. Analyse the transcript and provide responses according to the instructions in the section headings below. )



[presentation] Presenting Problem(s)


\[Describe the client's presentation during the session in a brief paragraph. Highlight any relevant updates to symptoms, behaviours, or situations since the previous session. Comment on the client's emotional state at the beginning of the session, particularly as it relates to their threat, drive, and soothing systems. Document any visible signs of distress, shame, or self-criticism that were apparent. Note any life events or current circumstances contributing to the client’s presentation. Summarise the client’s main concerns or difficulties discussed in the session.]



[content] Session Content


\[Summarise the core issues explored in the session and how they relate to the client’s presentation and treatment goals. Detail the specific CFT topics, themes, and issues discussed. Include information about any specific compassion-related themes such as self-criticism, shame, or difficulties with self-soothing. Note any events or situations the client reported where they experienced activation of their threat system or attempted to apply compassionate responding. Include any reflections on previous homework completion and learning gained from practice.]



[cft_conceptualisation] CFT Conceptualisation


(Provide a case conceptualisation from a CFT perspective based on information or insights gained in this session.)

* Emotion regulation systems: \[For the core issues discussed, describe the client's three emotion regulation systems, threat, drive, and soothing. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]
* Self-criticism: \[Describe the client's self-critical thoughts. Identify key blocks, fears, or resistances to compassion. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]
* \[Note relevant developmental experiences that may have shaped these patterns, particularly early experiences of threat, safety, attachment, and compassion. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]



[interventions] Interventions


(Detail the specific therapeutic interventions applied, with a focus on CFT techniques. For each intervention, briefly describe how it was implemented and the client's response. If mentioned in the transcript, include relevant information about:)
\[Psychoeducation, such as the three circles model, evolutionary approach, multiple selves
Compassionate skills, which may include compassionate reasoning, compassionate behaviour, compassionate imagery, compassionate feeling and compassionate sensation
Compassionate mind training exercises such as soothing rhythm breathing and compassionate imagery
Chair work for accessing different parts of self, such as the critical self, threatened self, and compassionate self
Compassion formulation development/refinement
Work with the "compassionate self" including embodiment, qualities, wisdom
Mindfulness and attention training exercises
Compassionate letter writing or imagery
Body-focused compassionate exercises, including posture, facial expressions, voice tone
Addressing fears, blocks, and resistances to compassion
Threat system de-activation strategies
Validation and normalisation of emotional responses from an evolutionary perspective
Exploration of the functions of self-criticism and shame
Other CFT-specific interventions]



[client_response] Client Response


\[Describe in a paragraph of 2-3 sentences how the client engaged with and responded to the CFT exercises and interventions. Briefly note their willingness to participate in compassion-focused exercises and any difficulties they experienced with the compassion work. Briefly note any shifts in emotional state that occurred during compassion exercises, including both positive responses such as feelings of warmth, safety, or connection and activation of fears or resistances to compassion such as unworthiness, anxiety, or grief. Document any insights the client gained about their own patterns of self-criticism, shame, or the three emotion regulation systems of threat, drive, and soothing. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]



[progress] Progress


\[In a brief paragraph of 3-4 sentences, describe the client's progress toward their treatment goals or shifts in the client’s understanding of their difficulties. This could include developments in their capacity for self-compassion and compassion for others, or reductions in shame, self-criticism, or threat-based responding. Document progress in developing their soothing system and compassionate attributes such as care for well-being, sensitivity, distress tolerance, empathy, and non-judgment. Highlight any changes in their ability to recognise and step back from the "critic" and access their "compassionate self." Document any improvements in the client's ability to tolerate difficult emotions without avoidance or overidentification. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]



[risk] Risk Assessment


\[Summarise any risk factors, such as self-harm or suicidal ideation. (If current suicidal thoughts or behaviours are present, highlight text in red). List protective factors and any action plans if risk is present. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]



[homework] Homework


\[Detail the homework assignments given to the client for practice between sessions. Explain the rationale for each assignment and how it connects to the client's treatment goals and current stage in therapy. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]



[nextsteps] Plan

* Next Session: \[Outline the plan for the next session based on today's discussion. Indicate how the plan builds upon the current session and addresses the client's ongoing treatment needs. Note any specific CFT exercises or techniques that will be introduced or further developed in the next meeting. (Only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise leave blank)]
* Next Appointment: \[Provide the time, day and date of any future session agreed upon between the therapist and the client.]



[summary] Summary


\[Provide a brief recap of key points from the session in no more than four sentences which summarise the session.]

title: Comprehensive Psychological Evaluation Report
description: A report that details the findings of a comprehensive psychological assessment. When contextual information is provided by the clinician, combined
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by clinicians conducting a comprehensive psychological evaluation with an individual. It will take the format of a detailed report aimed at a variety of professionals and professions. For example, it could be used for medical reasons, legal reasons, insurance reasons, etc. Therefore, the tone and language of the report must be formal and use language consistent with evidence-based psychiatric and psychological assessments. The language in your output must be formal - do not use contractions and other types of informal language. The report will adhere to the following structure:)

\[Print the name of the recipient of the report.] (If the recipient’s name is not stated, just write "Recipient Name".)

\[Print the address of the recipient of the report.] (If the recipient’s address is not stated, just write "Recipient Address".)

Re: \[Print the client's first and last name.]

DOB: \[Print the client's date of birth in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

Address: \[Print the client's address.]

Dear \[Recipient name (using appropriate title if applicable).] (If the recipient’s name is not stated, just write "Recipient Name".),

The following report is a comprehensive psychological evaluation of \[client's first and last name].

\[Provide a summary of the purpose of the evaluation and the client’s associated presenting concerns. Describe any relevant personal information e.g., age and gender of client; cultural background; and any modifications or assistive measures required for the evaluation (such as the need for an interpreter) or other context that is relevant to the assessment.]

[information_sources] Information Sources

* \[List all the sources of information, including dates, clinical interviews, psychometric assessments.]
* \[List the documents and other sources of information by third parties provided to the clinician to inform the contents of the report (only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank).]

[history] Personal and Developmental History

\[Provide a detailed summary of the client’s personal and developmental history. Include information such as early development, family history and structure, education and employment history, occupation, current living arrangements, and social support. Describe any history of trauma, including family or intergenerational trauma, or significant adversity, or any type of traumatic event. Describe any family history of any illness or medical condition, including psychiatric illness and neurodevelopmental conditions. Include any information about family suicides or family addiction behaviours (e.g., alcoholism, other drug abuse).] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[medical_status_and_medical_history] Medical Status and Medical History

\[Describe previous non-psychiatric diagnoses and medical conditions, including information about when the diagnosis was made and by whom. Provide information given by the client that describes responses to treatment, such as effective outcomes, side effects, and adverse reactions.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[mental_health_history] Mental Health History

\[Describe previous mental health and psychiatric diagnoses and conditions, including information about when the diagnosis was made and by whom. Provide information given by the client that describes responses to treatment, such as effective outcomes, side effects, and adverse reactions. Describe any previous psychological interventions, including with therapists, online programs, or group programs. Describe any past hospitalisations for mental health reasons, including locations, names of key medical personnel involved, and dates of these hospitalisations.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[medications] Medication History

\[Describe all medications that the client is currently taking, including psychotropic and non-psychotropic. Include information about doses and frequencies. Describe any medication history that details information such as response to any past medication, side effects, and adverse reactions. Assess adherence to previous medication regimens.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[drug_and_alcohol_use_history] Drug and Alcohol Use History

\[Provide a detailed summary of the client’s drug and alcohol use history. Describe the client’s current level of alcohol consumption, cigarette or vape use, and describe if the client consumes any other recreational or illicit substance. Describe any past or current misuse of prescription medications.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[other_addiction_history] Other Addiction History

\[Provide a detailed summary of the client’s other addiction history. Describe any other potentially problematic addictive behaviours mentioned by the client (e.g., gambling, video gaming, Internet, social media, food, pornography). Describe this information even if the client does not view the behaviour as problematic or as an addiction.] (Only include if addictive behaviours were explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[legal_and_forensic_history] Legal and Forensic History

\[Provide a detailed summary of the client’s legal and forensic history. Describe any previous history mentioned by the client that involves illegal activity, interactions with the justice system, imprisonment, interactions with the family law system, or domestic violence behaviour.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[presentation] Current Presentation

\[Provide a detailed summary of the client’s current presentation and current functioning. Include information related to the purpose of the psychological evaluation and the client’s presenting concerns e.g., type and nature of current symptoms; length of time with current symptoms; external stressors that are relevant to the client’s current presentation; and interpersonal or relationship factors that are relevant to the client’s current presentation. Include other information related to the impact on current functioning for the client (e.g., occupational, family, social, academic). Describe key lifestyle indicators that are potentially relevant to the purpose of this evaluation and the client’s presenting concerns. Describe the client’s current sleep pattern and sleep hygiene. Describe the client’s current diet, including binge behaviours, caffeine intake, and consumption of junk food. Describe the client’s current level of physical exercise or general activity levels. Describe the client’s level of social functioning, including information about quality and quantity of social and family relationships, the client’s efforts at connecting socially with others, and the extent of any social isolation.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)

\[Provide a narrative summary in a few sentences of the client's mental status, and, where applicable, taking into account: Appearance (the client's general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire); behaviour (the client's interaction with their surrounds, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport); speech (the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the client's speech); mood and affect (the client's reported mood and emotional state, using their own words if possible, and the range and appropriateness of the client's emotional response during the appointment, noting any discrepancies with the client's stated mood; and any observations about emotional expression made by the clinician); thought process (the client's coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts); thought content (the content of the client's thoughts, noting the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any); perception (hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the client, if any); cognition (the client's orientation to time / place / person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension); and insight and judgment (the client's understanding of their mental health presentation and their decision-making capacity, noting any lack of awareness or denial).] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[strengths_coping_skills_recreational_int] Strengths, Coping Skills, Recreational Interests, and Hobbies

\[Provide a detailed summary of the client’s strengths, coping skills, recreational interests, and hobbies. Describe the client’s strengths, positive coping skills, recreational interests, hobbies, and other protective factors. Only list things that are accepted as helpful and useful in promoting a healthy lifestyle, and that are likely to assist in managing mental health. Do not list anything that is considered harmful to mental health (e.g., consuming alcohol, other addictive behaviour).] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[scales] Psychometric Assessment

\[Provide any psychometric assessment results. For each test, start a new paragraph, and include: the name of the assessment, a brief description of the psychometric assessment, the date of test administration, and relevant purpose to the current evaluation. Provide full quantitative results (e.g., scores, percentiles). Provide the full interpretation. Provide any implications of results in the clinical context related to the current evaluation. (Repeat this process for as many assessments that were administered.) If no psychometric assessments were administered, leave this section blank.]

[functional_assessment] Functional Assessment

\[Describe the client’s level of current functioning across multiple domains: daily living skills; social and interpersonal functioning; occupational or academic functioning; and self-care and independence.]

[formulation_impression] Formulation / Impression

\[Provide a detailed bio-psycho-social psychiatric formulation, including predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating and protective factors. If applicable, also include a psychodynamic formulation of the client's presentation. Include evidence from the session notes, contextual notes, psychometric assessments; and transcripts to enhance the details of your formulation.]

[diagnosis] Diagnosis

\[Describe the clinician's diagnostic impressions and observations, based on DSM-5-TR and ICD-11 criteria. Provide the rationale given by the clinician for this diagnosis (or diagnoses), including details of relevant criterion that the client meets for such a diagnosis, and the results of psychometric testing which may provide evidence for or against a diagnosis being applied. Include the diagnostic code of any such diagnoses. Describe if the clinician has stated that these diagnoses are new or confirmation of diagnoses previously given. Detail any comorbidities or differential diagnosis considerations provided by the clinician. Outline any relevant cultural, context, or trauma-informed considerations.]

[risk] Risk

\[Based on information in the session notes, contextual notes, and transcripts, make a risk assessment about the client in a statement (e.g., suicidal or homicidal ideation, self-harm or harm to others, and risk of relapse, if any). Include any information related to past suicide attempts or past self-harming behaviour. Include any information about psychosocial stressors contributing to the level of risk. Include any information about protective factors that mitigate the risk.]

[nextsteps] Recommendations

\[Describe any recommendations made by the clinician. Such information may include the following:]

* \[Specify if the clinician has made any evidence-based recommendations for medication reviews or changes. Provide the clinician's rationale for these recommendations. If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify if the clinician has made any suggested non-pharmacological interventions, such as psychotherapy or lifestyle modifications. Provide the clinician's rationale for these recommendations. If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify if the clinician has made or suggested any referrals to other specialists or services. Provide the clinician's rationale for these recommendations. If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify any diagnostic-specific based recommendations that were discussed, including ways to manage the diagnosis or general recommendations that were discussed around the diagnosis. If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify any lifestyle or personal decision-based recommendations that were discussed. If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify if the clinician has suggested any workplace or academic modifications. If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify if the clinician has suggested any plans for follow-up and monitoring (e.g., next appointment details). If the clinician has not made any such recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Detail if any Safety Plan has been made by the clinician in the event that the client poses a risk to safety, including steps to take in a crisis. If the clinician has not made any Safety Plan, leave blank.]

[prognosis] Prognosis

\[If applicable, describe the clinician’s clinically informed opinion about the likely course of the presenting condition(s), including: factors influencing the prognosis; likelihood of improvement with recommended interventions; and any limitations to prognostic certainty.]

[summary] Summary

\[Provide a paragraph summary of the psychological evaluation in no more than four sentences, including a summary of the clinician’s formulation and diagnosis.]

If you have any questions about the contents of this report, please feel free to contact me.

Yours sincerely

\[Clinician Name.] (If the clinician's name is not stated, just write "Clinician Name".)

sections.label: Strengths, Coping Skills, Recreational Interests, and Hobbies

title: Cosmetic Readiness Assessment
description: For a psychological assessment session before a cosmetic procedure is conducted.
prompt:
(The template below is intended to be used for an assessment session by mental health clinicians like psychologists. The objective of the session is for the psychologist to determine if the client is a suitable candidate for a cosmetic procedure.)

[introduction] Introduction

* [Explain limits to confidentiality, sharing info with referring cosmetic practitioner]
* [Cosmetic practitioner ultimate decision maker]
* [Nature of referral from the cosmetic practitioner]
* [Nature of type of cosmetic procedure they are seeking]

[presentation] Current Presentation

* \[Describe patient's current presentation or presenting complaint or primary reason for seeking this visit\, how long the issue\(s\) have been occurring\, etc\]\(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript\, contextual notes or clinical note\, otherwise leave blank\)
* [How long has the patient been thinking about this surgery/procedure?]
* [Why did the patient seek out the consultation with the cosmetic practitioner now?]
* [Previous attempts by the patient to manage the problem/appearance concerns]
* [How would the patient like to look different?]
* [How would the patient like to feel different emotionally, or about themself]
* [How is the patient paying for it?]
* [Describe the motivation for getting the cosmetic procedure]
* [Describe expectation the patient has about how they will look or feel if they get the cosmetic procedure]
* [What is the patient's Understanding of risks/benefits of the current procedure]
* [Effect of procedure on familial/cultural/gender identity] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank)
* [Coping strategies in event of adverse event/recovery period/undesirable outcome]
* [Any results from an assessment for body dysmorphic disorder, such as from a questionnaire]
* Severity/Impact: \[Describe current impact on functioning\, for example\, impact on work/school/activities of daily life\, etc\]\(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript\, contextual notes or clinical note\, otherwise leave blank\)

[past_cosmetic_procedures] Past Cosmetic Procedures

* \[Describe any past cosmetic procedures that the patient has had\. Were they satisfied or dissatisfied with the outcome?\] \]\(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript\, contextual notes or clinical note\, otherwise leave blank\)

[body_image] Body Image

* Describe any comments the patient made about how they feel about their appearance. Include specific areas of their appearance, if mentioned, that they like or dislike. Include a direct quote, if any, about an area of their body they dislike]
* [Describe how they felt about their appearance growing up]
* [Describe the use of the mirror, including any grooming, makeup or other appearance related behavours]
* [Describe if they avoid situations because of the way they look]
* [Describe if they experience distress, embarrassment, shame or self hatred associated with appearance ]

[current_life_situation] Current Life Situation

* Living situation: [Describe who they live with and their relationship with those they live with]
* Occupation: [Does the patient Work, study or engage in family duties]
* Recovery: [Capacity to take time off to recover from surgery/procedure?]

[family_history] Family History

* [Key family members and the quality of the relationships to them]
* [Relationships growing up / significant childhood events such as trauma]
* [Family history of psychopathology, mental illness, such as OCD, depression or body dysmorphic disorder]
* [Has the patient’s family experienced any trauma?]
* [Does that patient have any family member that agrees/disagrees about the procedure, or who have had cosmetic procedures?]

[social_history] Social History

* [Current and previous significant relationships, such as romantic relationships]
* [Does that patient have friends who agree/disagree about procedure?]
* [Who is available to support the patient in recovery from the procedure?]
* [Has anyone the patient knows had a cosmetic procedure]
* [Are there social or society influences to look good?]
* [Confidence socially]

[developmental_and_medical_history] Developmental & Medical history

* [Describe any medical, developmental history. Include any reference to Sleep, Appetite]
* Current Medications: [List current medications, including details such as dose and frequency] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank)

[history] Psychiatric History

* Psychiatric Diagnoses: \[Details of any psychiatric diagnoses\, are mental health concerns well managed \]\(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript\, contextual notes or clinical note\, otherwise leave blank\)
* Recent Substance Use: [Details of any recent substance use, including type, frequency, and duration] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank)
* [Describe if there was any discussion of how mental illness may affect cosmetic treatment]
* [History of seeing psychologists, psychiatrists or counselors, including years and treatment progress. Was it helpful?

[hospitalisations] Hospitalisations

* Medical history: \[Details of any recent medical issues\]\(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript\, contextual notes or clinical note\, otherwise leave blank\)
* Treatments / Hospitalisations: [Details of any recent interventions or treatments, including hospitalisations if any] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank)

[risk] Risk Assessment

* [Describe if the patient mentioned any of the following. Passive suicidal ideation & active ideation – frequency and intensity. History Suicidal threats/ plan/ method/ have you started planning, Previous attempts. Protective: What’s made you resist/ what are some of your reasons to live]  (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank)

[mse] Mental Status Examination

* Appearance: [Describe the patient's clothing, hygiene, and any notable physical characteristics (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Behaviour: [Observe the patient's activity level, interaction with their surroundings, and any unique or notable behaviors (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Speech: [Note the rate, volume, clarity, and coherence of the patient's speech (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Mood: [Record the patient's self-described emotional state, using their own words if possible (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Affect: [Describe the range and appropriateness of the patient's emotional response during the examination, noting any discrepancies with the stated mood (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Thoughts: [Assess the patient's thought process and thought content, noting any distortions, delusions, or preoccupations (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Perceptions: [Note any reported hallucinations or sensory misinterpretations, specifying type and impact on the patient (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Cognition: [Describe the patient's memory, orientation to time/place/person, concentration, and comprehension (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Insight: [Describe the patient's understanding of their own condition and symptoms, noting any lack of awareness or denial. Does the psychologist and the patient agree on what is causing body image dissatisfaction? Are there differences between how they see the problem and how family or friends see the problem? if so, this may suggest low insights (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* Judgment: [Describe the patient's decision-making ability and understanding of the consequences of their actions (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]

[summarise] Summarise

[Summarise any other details from the transcript in a paragraph with emphasis on current status, risk assessment and management, interventions, progress towards achieving goals/objectives, setbacks, obstacles]

title: Couples Therapy
description: Focusing on common relationship counselling approaches such as the Gottman Method, Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) and Behavioural Couple Therapy
prompt:
(Provide a summary of a session for a therapist providing couples therapy, such as Gottman Method, Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), and other evidence-based relationship counselling approaches. There will be a couple attending the session with the therapist. Review the transcript and identify which speaker corresponds to who. When producing the note, attribute statements clearly and correctly to each individual. Using the transcript, notes and contextual notes, generate a summary of the session from a couples therapy perspective. Ensure the summary reflects couples therapy terminology, interventions, and dynamics.) (Start the output at Presenting Concerns, do not print anything else prior) [presentation] Presenting Concerns \- \[Summarise the couple's primary concerns or relationship difficulties discussed in the session\, focusing on what they reported\. Note any shifts or developments since the previous session\. Detail any significant relationship events or conflicts that have occurred between sessions\. Include individual concerns raised by each partner related to the relationship\. Note any relevant life stressors or circumstances impacting the relationship\. \(Only include information explicitly mentioned in the transcript\, note or contextual note\.\)\] [content] Session Content \- \[Review of homework/relationship practice: Document the couple's progress with previously assigned relationship\-building exercises or communication tasks\, including barriers and successes\. \(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\.\)\] \- \[Key topics discussed: Outline the main issues or topics explored during the session\, how these relate to the couple's goals\, and any significant patterns or dynamics that emerged\.\] [relational_dynamics_and_conceptualisatio] Relational Dynamics and Conceptualisation \- \[Interaction patterns observed: Describe specific communication patterns\, conflict dynamics\, bid responses\, repair attempts\, emotional engagement\, or other notable interactions between partners during the session\. Note moments of attunement or disconnection\. If using the Gottman Method\, note specific Gottman concepts such as Four Horsemen\, bids for connection\, etc\.\, or EFT cycle positions where applicable\.\] \- \[Conceptualisation: For the primary issues explored during the session\. For Gottman\-based work\, include relevant elements of the Sound Relationship House\. For EFT\-oriented work\, describe the negative interaction cycle\, primary and secondary emotions\, and attachment needs\. For behavioural approaches\, describe functional analyses of relationship behaviours\. Use appropriate subheadings based on the therapeutic approach being used\.\] [interventions] Interventions \- \[Provide specific detail about all of the therapeutic interventions used with a focus on couples therapy techniques\, and the therapeutic rationale for the intervention\. This could include Gottman interventions\, EFT interventions\, behavioural interventions\, psychoeducation\, conflict management techniques\, connection\-building exercises\, communication skill training\, and emotion regulation strategies\. For each intervention\, identify the specific technique\, specify the therapeutic modality such as EFT\, detail specifically what was targeted\, and then summarise how the couple responded to each intervention\, including any notable shifts in thinking or behaviour\.\] \(Note: it is important that you are specific and provide a level of detail that is sufficient for another therapist to be able to continue therapy\. Use as many dot points as needed to list separate interventions\) [individual_partner_perspectives_and_prog] Individual Partner Perspectives and Progress \- \[Partner A's Perspective: Summarise key concerns\, insights\, and emotional experiences expressed by Partner A during the session\. \(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\.\)\] \- \[Partner B's Perspective: Summarise key concerns\, insights\, and emotional experiences expressed by Partner B during the session \(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\.\)\] \- \[Areas of Convergence/Divergence: Note where partners showed alignment in understanding or goals\, and where perspectives significantly differed \(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\.\)\] [key_themes_and_progress] Key Themes and Progress \- \[Highlight important themes\, insights\, realisation or "aha moments" that emerged during the session\. Include any shifts in the client’s understanding of their difficulties or progress toward therapy goals\. If there has been a shift\, demonstrate why this is so using examples\.\] \- \[Describe the couple’s progress and identify areas where progress is challenged or slower\. Note any improvements in communication patterns\, emotional connection\, conflict management\, intimacy\, or other targeted areas\] [homework_or_plan] Homework or Plan \- \[Describe homework or tasks assigned to the clients\, including rationale and expected outcomes\. Note any feedback or concerns from the clients about the assignments\. \(only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise leave blank\)\] [risk] Risk Assessment \- \[Summarise any risk factors\, such as self\-harm or suicidal ideation\. \(if current suicidal thoughts or behaviours are present\, highlight text in red\)\. List protective factors and any action plans if risk is present\. \(only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise leave blank\)\] [nextsteps] Next Steps \- \[Outline the preliminary agenda or goals for the next session\, based on today's discussion\. Provide the time\, day and date of any future session agreed upon between the therapist and the clients\.\] [summary] Summary \- \[Provide a brief recap of key points from the session in no more than three sentences which summarise the relationship dynamics\, interventions used\, responses observed\, and direction of treatment\.\]
(Review the entire note to confirm that statements are consistently attributed to the correct individual throughout the note. Correct any inconsistencies to maintain clear and accurate diarisation.)

title: Couples Therapy Intake Assessment Session
description: Capturing the initial intake/assessment session for couples therapy, this template is designed for use with individuals or couples attending their
prompt:
(Provide a comprehensive summary of an initial intake/assessment session for couples therapy. Review the transcript and identify which speaker corresponds to who. The session may involve a couple attending together, or an individual attending alone with their partner planning to join in future sessions. When producing the note, attribute statements clearly and correctly to each individual. Using the transcript, notes and contextual notes, generate a summary of the intake session from a couples therapy perspective. Ensure the summary reflects couples therapy terminology and captures all relevant background, assessment, and treatment planning information.) (Start the output at Session Attendees, do not print anything else prior.) **Session Attendees** [Identify who attended this intake session: both partners together, Partner A only, or Partner B only. If one partner was absent, note this and document any information shared about the absent partner's perspective or willingness to engage in therapy. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Presenting Concerns and Reasons for Seeking Therapy** [Summarise the primary concerns or relationship difficulties that brought the couple/individual to therapy. Detail the specific problems, conflicts, or relationship challenges described. Note the frequency, duration, and severity of these concerns as reported. Include each partner's perspective on what the main problems are, noting areas of agreement and divergence in how they view the issues. Describe any precipitating events or recent changes that prompted them to seek therapy now. Include any relevant context about urgency or crisis elements. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Relationship History and Timeline** [Provide a narrative summary of the couple's relationship history including: how they met, what initially attracted them to each other, length of relationship, relationship status (dating, cohabiting, married, separated, etc.), significant relationship milestones (engagement, marriage, moving in together, births of children, relocations, etc.), when things were last good between them, when they first noticed problems, how the relationship has evolved over time, and any pivotal events that significantly impacted the relationship. Note patterns of satisfaction and dissatisfaction across the relationship's trajectory. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Current Relationship Structure and Context**
* Living arrangements: [Current living situation - together, separately, etc.] * Children: [Number, ages, living arrangements, custody if applicable, stepchildren if relevant.] * Extended family involvement: [Relevant information about family of origin involvement, in-laws, etc.] * External stressors: [Financial pressures, work stress, health issues, cultural or religious factors, or other contextual factors impacting the relationship.] * Social support: [Quality of couple's support network, friendships, community connections, family assistance, etc.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)
**Communication and Conflict Patterns** [Describe how the couple typically communicates and manages conflict. Include: typical conflict triggers, how each partner behaves during conflict (pursues, withdraws, becomes critical, defensive, etc.), patterns of escalation or avoidance, capacity for repair after conflict, quality of everyday communication outside of conflict, and any observations of communication during the intake session. Note any specific patterns that emerged or were described, such as demand-withdraw patterns, criticism-defensiveness cycles, or communication strengths. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Emotional and Physical Connection** [Summarise the current state of emotional intimacy, including: how emotionally connected or disconnected partners feel, ability to be vulnerable with each other, emotional responsiveness and attunement, expressions of affection and appreciation, and quality of friendship/companionship within the relationship. Describe the current state of physical intimacy and sexual relationship if discussed, including any concerns, changes, or challenges in this area. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Individual Partner Backgrounds and Relevant History** **Partner A's Background:** [Summarise relevant individual history including: family of origin experiences and relationship models witnessed growing up, previous significant relationships and how they ended, relevant mental health history, substance use history if discussed, any history of trauma that impacts the current relationship, cultural or religious background relevant to relationship expectations, significant life experiences that shape their approach to relationships. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Partner B's Background:** [Summarise relevant individual history using the same areas as above. If Partner B was not present at intake, note any information Partner A shared about Partner B's background. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Mental Health, Substance Use, and Medical History** **Partner A:**
* Current mental health concerns: [e.g., depression, anxiety, trauma symptoms, etc.] * Mental health treatment history: [Previous therapy, medications, hospitalisations, etc.] * Substance use: [Current patterns, history of problematic use, etc.] * Relevant medical conditions: [Physical health issues impacting the relationship.] * Current medications: [Psychiatric or other relevant medications.]
Partner B: [Same categories as above] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.) **Previous Therapy and Treatment History** [Document any previous couples therapy: when, where, with whom, duration, problems addressed, outcome (successful, somewhat successful, no change, deteriorated). Include any previous individual therapy for either partner that is relevant to relationship issues. Note what was helpful or unhelpful in previous therapeutic experiences and what the couple/individual hopes will be different this time. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Relationship Strengths and Positive Factors** [Document specific relationship strengths, positive qualities each partner sees in the other, shared values and interests, what is still working well in the relationship, times when the couple functions effectively together, reasons for hope or motivation to work on the relationship, commitment level from each partner, and any demonstrated capacity for reflection, empathy, or willingness to take responsibility. Include specific examples that emerged during the session. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Each Partner's Goals and Expectations for Therapy** **Partner A's Goals:** [Specific, concrete goals Partner A articulated for therapy. What does Partner A hope will be different? What would success look like? Include both relationship goals and individual goals related to the relationship. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Partner B's Goals:** [Same as above for Partner B. If Partner B was not present, note this and include any information about Partner B's willingness/motivation as reported by Partner A. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Shared Goals and Convergence:** [Note areas where partners' goals align or complement each other. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Divergent Goals:** [Note areas where partners want different things from therapy or have conflicting expectations. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)] **Therapist Initial Impressions and Conceptualisation** [Provide the therapist’s initial clinical impressions based on the intake assessment. This may include: primary relational dynamics observed or described, preliminary hypothesis about underlying patterns (while remaining framework-flexible), attachment-related themes if evident, stage of relationship development/deterioration, factors maintaining current difficulties, individual factors impacting the relationship, systemic or contextual factors relevant to treatment, readiness and motivation for change in each partner, and compatibility of partners' goals with couples therapy. Note that you should only provide the therapist’s impressions and conceptualisation – do not make up your own impressions and conceptualisation. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Assessment of Suitability for Couples Therapy** [Summarise the therapist’s comments about the couple's appropriateness for conjoint couples therapy. Describe safety factors, severity of violence or abuse, power imbalances, both partners' willingness to participate, compatibility of goals, capacity to tolerate joint sessions, whether individual therapy is indicated first or concurrently, and any modifications needed to standard couples therapy approach. If the therapist believes that couples therapy is not appropriate, document reasons and recommendations for alternative treatment. Note that you should only provide the therapist’s assessment of suitability for couples therapy – do not make up your own assessment of suitability for couples therapy. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)] **Safety Assessment and Contraindications**
* Intimate partner violence: [Any history of physical, emotional, sexual, or financial abuse. Include frequency, severity, recency, injuries requiring medical attention, and whether either partner fears the other. Note if there are current restraining orders or legal involvement.] * Threats of separation/divorce: [Who has made threats, how recently, how seriously.] * Suicidal or self-harm risk: [Current thoughts, plans, intent, history. If current suicidal thoughts or active risk present, highlight in red font.] * Substance abuse severity: [Level of substance use by either partner and impact on relationship safety.] * Affairs and infidelity: [Current or recent affairs, impact on relationship, disclosure status.] * Contraindications for couples therapy: [Based on therapist comments, note if the couple presents with factors that make conjoint therapy inappropriate or require modification of approach.] * Protective factors that mitigate the risk: [Strengths, supports, reasons for living, commitment to safety, etc.]
(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.) **Risk Management and Follow-Up Actions** [Document any immediate risk management actions needed: safety planning, referrals to other services, coordination with other providers, crisis resources provided, or follow-up contacts planned. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Therapeutic Approach and Preliminary Treatment Planning** [Outline the preliminary therapeutic approach and treatment direction discussed by the therapist with the couple. This may include: which couples therapy framework(s) the therapist intends to use (e.g., Gottman Method, EFT, BCT, etc.) or if an integrative approach will be utilised, initial focus areas for treatment based on assessment, proposed session structure (frequency, duration, format), any psychoeducation provided about the therapy process and what to expect, discussion of confidentiality, no-secrets policy, or other therapy ground rules, and any assessments or homework assigned for completion before next session. Describe any response to the proposed treatment by either member of the couple, including resistance or acceptance. Note that you should only provide a summary of what the therapist or couple has indicated – do not make up your own ideas about what therapeutic approach or preliminary treatment planning should take place. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)] **Homework or Between-Session Tasks** [Document any homework, questionnaires, assessments, or tasks assigned to be completed before the next session (e.g., Gottman relationship questionnaires, reading materials, relationship timelines, individual reflection exercises). Include rationale for assignments and any instructions or concerns discussed. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Next Steps and Treatment Plan** [Outline agreed-upon next steps including: whether ongoing couples therapy will proceed or if individual sessions are recommended first, preliminary goals for upcoming sessions, session frequency and duration, any additional assessments to be completed, referrals to other services if needed, and date, time, and day of next scheduled appointment. Note if Partner B will be attending next session if not present at intake. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] **Summary** [Provide a concise summary of the intake session in no more than four sentences. Include: who attended, the primary presenting concerns, key relationship dynamics or patterns identified, and the preliminary treatment direction. This summary should give another clinician a quick overview of the couple's situation and the plan going forward.] (Review the entire note to confirm that statements are consistently attributed to the correct individual throughout the note. Correct any inconsistencies to maintain clear and accurate diarisation.)

title: Couples Therapy Session Summary and Homework Activities
description: A client-facing summary document to be provided to the couple (or individual attending partner) following a couples therapy session. The document
prompt:
(Generate a warm, supportive summary document for the client(s) following a couples therapy session. This document will be given to both partners. Use casual, informal language while maintaining a polite, professional, and warm tone. Do not use jargon or technical terms. Avoid including direct quotes. Make sure the wording feels supportive, non-judgmental, and easy to understand. Keep the entire document concise - it must fit on a single A4 page. Review the transcript and identify which speaker corresponds to who. If only one partner attended the session, adapt the document to address that individual directly. Generalise specific conflicts or criticisms rather than including detailed descriptions. Focus on insights, progress, and constructive action items while acknowledging the work being done.) (Use the following format strictly:) Hi [Client's First Name] and [Client's First Name], (If only one partner attended: Hi [Client's First Name].) Thanks for speaking with me today. 📋 **Session Summary** [Provide a clear and concise summary of the main issues that the client(s) discussed in today's session. Highlight key topics, any insights gained, and any emotions or themes that stood out. When describing what each partner shared, use natural attribution (e.g., "Sarah, you mentioned..." and "John, you shared..."). When describing shared insights or joint discussions, use collective language (e.g., "You both recognised..." or "Together, you explored..."). Keep it simple and easy to read, avoiding technical language. Generalise specific conflicts or criticisms to focus on patterns or themes rather than detailed descriptions of arguments. Write only one paragraph of less than seven sentences.] 🎯 **Activities For You To Do** * [List all the tasks, exercises, or reflections that were suggested and that the client(s) agreed to do before the next session. Be specific about who will do what, attributing tasks to specific partners by name when they are individual activities (e.g., "Sarah: Practice the 'soft startup' approach when bringing up concerns" or "John: Complete the daily appreciation exercise"). For shared activities that both partners will do together, make this clear (e.g., "Both of you: Schedule a 20-minute check-in conversation three times this week"). Write them in a friendly, encouraging tone. These might include communication exercises, relationship-building activities, individual reflection tasks, or anything else discussed. Only include activities explicitly mentioned in the transcript. If no activities were mentioned, leave this section blank and do not print the sub-heading.]
🕰️ **Next Appointment** [List details of the next appointment, including time, date, and location. If no future appointment was made in the session, do not type the subheading title, and leave this section blank.] [Print a positive, affirming sentence that is encouraging and supportive of both partners and the relationship work being done, taking into account the information provided in the transcript. The affirmation should acknowledge both partners' efforts and commitment to improving the relationship. Examples might include: "You're both showing real courage in addressing these challenges together" or "The commitment you're both bringing to this work is a real strength in your relationship" or "It takes a lot of bravery to be this honest with each other, and you're both doing that well."] If you have any questions, please feel free to get in touch. [Clinician's First and Last Name]

title: DAP (Data, Assessment, and Plan)
description: Designed to summarise a session between a mental health clinician and a client using the DAP (Data, Assessment, and Plan) note format.
prompt:
(Provide a summary of a session between a mental health clinician and a client using the DAP note format. DAP stands for Data, Assessment, and Plan. You must strictly adhere to the following layout:)
 

[data] Data


[Print this section in a single paragraph of less than six sentences. Describe the client’s self-reported mood, functioning, symptoms, and progress since last session. Note any relevant behavioural observations (e.g., affect, speech, posture, eye contact, engagement, etc.). Include a summary of any major events, triggers, or contextual stressors mentioned by the client. Summarise the relevant topics or content of conversations in the session. Document any psychometric results, risk indicators, or collateral information. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
 

[assessment] Assessment


[Print this section in a single paragraph of less than six sentences. Describe the clinician’s interpretation of the client’s presentation and progress, including any barriers to progress. Comment on therapeutic themes (e.g., cognitive distortions, avoidance, emotional regulations, etc.). Describe the level of insight, motivation, and engagement exhibited by the client. Note any changes in diagnosis, risk, or case formulation. Reference the evidence-based framework being applied (e.g., CBT, ACT, DBT, etc.), and describe the client’s response or feedback to any intervention. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
 

[nextsteps] Plan


[Print this section in a single paragraph of less than six sentences. Describe the interventions that were delivered by the clinician today (e.g., behavioural activation, grounding, cognitive restructuring, psychoeducation, etc.). Detail any homework, between-session tasks, or resources provided by the clinician. Identify any goals that were agreed upon between the clinician and the client. Note any risk management issues, or safety planning or referrals that were agreed upon between the clinician and the client. Specify the next appointment details and any ongoing monitoring plan. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: DARP (Data, Assessment, Response, and Plan)
description: Designed to summarise a session between a mental health clinician and a client using the DARP (Data, Assessment, Response, and Plan) note format.
prompt:

Consistent with typical DARP notes, only present key information, and each sentence must be as short and sharp as possible. You must adhere to the following layout:)
 

[data] Data


[Print this section in a single paragraph of less than six sentences. Describe the client’s key presenting concerns, mood, thoughts, symptoms, functioning, and behaviour. Describe any relevant psychosocial or environmental factors. Include a summary of any major events, triggers, or contextual stressors mentioned by the client.
