Discuss overall intellectual functioning and any discrepancies between verbal and nonverbal abilities. Compare current intellectual functioning to estimated premorbid abilities based on demographic factors, reading abilities, or other estimation methods. Note any patterns suggestive of decline from premorbid levels. (Relevant tests that the clinician might administer include the Test of Pre-morbid Functioning, Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scales or WAIS, or Wechsler Intelligence Scale for Children or WISC).] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Attention and Processing Speed** [Detail findings of standardised assessments related to attention and processing speed. (Relevant tests that the clinician might administer include CPT, Trail Making Test, WAIS Processing Speed index, D-KEFS, Conners Adult ADHD Rating Scales 2nd Edition or CAARS 2.] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Learning and Memory** [Report results of standardised assessments of learning and memory. (Relevant tests that the clinician might administer include Wechsler Memory Scale or WMS, WAIS working memory index etc.).] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Problem Solving and Executive Functioning** [Report results of standardised tests measuring problem-solving and executive functioning. (Relevant tests that the clinician might administer include Wisconsin Card Sorting Test or Trail Making Test). Discuss patterns of executive strengths and weaknesses and how these relate to everyday functioning and ability to compensate for other cognitive deficits.] (Only include if standardised test results are mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Language Functions** [Report results of standardised tests assessing receptive language, expressive language, naming, fluency, comprehension, repetition, reading, and writing. Note any language-based deficits that could impact communication or other cognitive domains.] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Visuospatial Abilities** [Detail results of standardised assessments examining visuospatial abilities and related skills. (Relevant tests that the clinician might administer include WAIS Visual Spatial Index, etc.).] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Motor and Sensory Functions** [Describe findings from assessments of fine motor speed, coordination, strength, and sensory functioning if evaluated. Note any motor or sensory deficits that could impact performance in other cognitive domains or everyday functioning.] (only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Academic Achievement** [If assessed, report findings related to reading, writing, mathematics, and other academic skills. Compare academic achievement to intellectual abilities and discuss any discrepancies suggesting learning disabilities or academic impact of cognitive deficits. (Relevant tests that the clinician might administer include the Wechsler Individual Assessment Test or WIAT).] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank). **Psychometric Assessments** (Report psychometric assessment results under this separate subheading after cognitive test results have been reported) [Provide any psychometric assessment results. For each test, include: the name of the assessment, a brief description of the psychometric assessment, the date of test administration, and relevant purpose to the current evaluation. Provide full quantitative results (e.g., scores, percentiles). Provide the full interpretation. Provide any implications of results in the clinical context related to the current evaluation. (Repeat this process for as many assessments as were administered.) If no psychometric assessments were administered, leave this section blank.] ### Formulation [Provide an evidence-based formulation based on the integration of background history, behavioural observations, and test results. Present a narrative integration of quantitative results and qualitative observations rather than simply listing scores. Use evidence-based frameworks to connect test results to real-world functioning and presenting concerns. Note patterns of strengths and weaknesses within and across domains. Compare current functioning to estimated premorbid abilities and expected levels based on demographic factors, if there is evidence for this comparison. Do not make claims about typical cognitive profiles or causality unless explicitly supported by empirical evidence. Include evidence from the session notes, contextual notes, psychometric assessments, and transcripts to enhance the details of your formulation.] ### Diagnostic Impressions [Describe the clinician's provisional diagnostic impressions and observations, based on DSM-5-TR and ICD-11 criteria. For neurocognitive disorders, specify the presumed etiology (e.g., Alzheimer's disease, vascular disease, traumatic brain injury) and severity level. List primary diagnoses first, followed by contributing or comorbid conditions. Provide the rationale given by the clinician for this diagnosis (or diagnoses), including details of relevant criteria that the client meets for such a diagnosis, and the test results which may provide evidence for or against a diagnosis being applied. Include the diagnostic code of any such diagnoses. Describe if the clinician has stated that these diagnoses are new or confirmation of diagnoses previously given. Document any differential diagnostic considerations that remain unresolved and require further investigation. Outline any relevant cultural, context, or trauma-informed considerations. (Do not over-interpret or make assumptions about possible explanations of the results or provide hypotheses about possible diagnoses. Adhere to what is known based on the results and the clinical observations. Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank).] ### Summary [Synthesise the assessment findings into a coherent neuropsychological conceptualisation that explains the client's presentation. Begin with a concise summary of key findings across domains. Discuss the pattern of cognitive strengths and weaknesses and whether these relate to the client's history, presenting concerns, and everyday functioning, only when direct evidence supports these relationships. Consider multiple contributing factors, including neurological, medical, psychiatric, educational, cultural, and psychosocial influences. Compare current findings to previous assessments if available to address questions of stability, improvement, or decline. Discuss how the findings address the original referral questions. Do not make specific claims about causality unless there is clear evidence to support such claims. Explain the implications of the findings for prognosis, treatment planning, and functional capabilities.] ### Recommendations (Provide detailed, specific recommendations based on the assessment findings. Ensure recommendations are evidence-based, practical, actionable, and tailored to the client's unique circumstances, strengths, and challenges. Use numbered or bulleted format for clarity. Use the section headings below, where appropriate. Start the section with a statement “Based on the assessment findings, the following recommendations are made to support [Client's Name]'s cognitive health and psychological well-being:") **Medical/Neurological Recommendations** [Suggest any indicated medical or neurological follow-up or referrals to specialists. Recommend medication reviews if appropriate. Suggest sleep studies, pain management, or other medical interventions that could improve cognitive functioning or quality of life.] **Cognitive Rehabilitation and Therapy** [Recommend specific cognitive rehabilitation approaches, compensatory strategies, or therapeutic interventions targeted to the identified cognitive deficits. Suggest specific therapy modalities (e.g., cognitive-behavioral therapy, memory training, attention process training) that would address cognitive or emotional needs. Specify the frequency, duration, and goals of recommended therapies.] **Educational/Vocational Recommendations** [Provide specific recommendations for educational or workplace accommodations based on the identified cognitive strengths and weaknesses. For students, detail appropriate academic supports, accommodations, or modifications. For employed individuals, suggest job accommodations, modifications, or considerations for vocational planning. Address questions of educational or occupational capacity if raised in the referral.] **Lifestyle and Environmental Modifications** [Recommend lifestyle modifications that could improve cognitive functioning or prevent further decline, such as exercise, diet, sleep hygiene, stress management, or cognitive engagement. Suggest environmental modifications or compensatory strategies to support daily functioning, such as memory aids, organisational systems, or assistive technology.] **Support and Resources** [Suggest appropriate support services, caregiver resources, or community programs that could benefit the client based on their needs. Recommend support groups, educational resources, or other psychosocial interventions that could improve adjustment and quality of life.] **Reassessment and Monitoring** [Indicate whether and when reassessment would be beneficial to monitor cognitive change, evaluate treatment effectiveness, or address unresolved diagnostic questions. Specify which cognitive domains or functions should be monitored most closely in the interim.] (Provide a paragraph in narrative form of 3-4 sentences, addressing the following:) [Indicate if test results and recommendations were discussed with the client upon completion of testing. Provide an observation of the client’s reaction to the results, e.g., “he was open to the feedback and seemed appreciative of the results.” Report any next steps agreed upon between the client and the neuropsychologist, such as additional follow up sessions. Report if the client’s questions were addressed as appropriate.] ### Conclusion [Provide a brief closing paragraph that summarises key findings and recommendations, and offers continued availability for questions or follow-up. Express appreciation to the referrer and invite further communication as needed, e.g. “ If you require further clarification on anything contained in this report, please do not hesitate to contact me.”] Sincerely, [Clinician Name] [Clinician Profession] [Practice Name]

title: Neuropsychological Assessment Session
description: Provides a summary of a neuropsychological assessment session, organising information about the client's presentation during testing, mental status,
prompt:
(The template below is designed for neuropsychologists to document assessment testing sessions with clients/patients. This template will organise information about the client's presentation during testing, mental status, tests administered, behavioural observations during specific tests, and any other relevant clinical observations. Analyse the transcript and provide responses according to the instructions in the section headings below. Use professional, clinically appropriate language that is congruent with neurocognitive and neurodevelopmental disorders in the DSM-5-TR and ICD-11. Ensure that all information is attributed to its source and document observations with specific examples where possible.)

[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)

[Provide a narrative summary of the client's mental status during the assessment session, taking into account: Appearance (any notes or comments made by the client or neuropsychologist about the client's general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire); behaviour (the client's interaction during testing, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport); speech (the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the client's speech); mood and affect (the client's reported mood and emotional state, using their own words if possible, and the range and appropriateness of the client's emotional response during testing, noting any discrepancies with the client's stated mood; and any observations about emotional expression made during testing); thought process (the client's coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts); thought content (the content of the client's thoughts, noting the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any); perception (hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the client, if any); cognition (the client's orientation to time/place/person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension); and insight and judgment (the client's understanding of their condition and the assessment process, noting any lack of awareness or denial).] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[test_session_context] Test Session Context

[Describe the testing environment, including any factors that could affect test performance (noise, interruptions, lighting, temperature, etc.). Note the client's physical state, including reports of fatigue, pain, hunger, or other physical complaints. Document the client's use of assistive devices (glasses, hearing aids, etc.) during testing. Note any medications taken the day of testing, including timing and dosage if reported. Describe the client's level of alertness and whether it fluctuated throughout the session. Document any breaks taken during the assessment and their frequency, duration, and reason. Report the testing session duration, if available.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[tests_administered] Tests Administered

[List all neuropsychological tests and assessment measures administered during this session in the order they were given. Examples of tests that may be used include the WAIS, WISC, WMS, Trail Making, CAARS, WIAT. Although the clinician may refer to tests using abbreviations, please report them using the full names of tests rather than abbreviations. Note any modifications to standard administration procedures and the reasons for these modifications. Document any tests that were attempted but not completed, including reasons for discontinuation.]

[general_observations] General Observations

[Describe the client's general approach to the assessments, including motivation, effort, level of cooperation, and response to the testing situation. Document the client's understanding of test instructions, need for repetition or clarification, and receptiveness to feedback or redirection. Note the client's awareness of their performance, including any expressed frustration, anxiety, or satisfaction with their performance. Describe the client's work style, including pace, carefulness, impulsivity, perseveration, or other notable characteristics. Document any test anxiety or performance anxiety observed. Note any significant behavioural changes over the course of the testing session.]

[observations] Observations

[For each test or assessment measure administered, provide specific behavioural observations that could impact interpretation of results. Document specific strategies the client used for different tasks, including any spontaneous verbalisation or self-talk during task performance. Note any error patterns, perseverations, confabulations, or other qualitative aspects of performance. Describe the client's response to success and failure on different tasks. Document any tasks that elicited notable emotional responses, frustration, or disengagement. Note any unexpected strengths or weaknesses observed during specific tests. Document any discrepancies between the client's subjective report of difficulty and their objective performance.]

[effort_and_validity_considerations] Effort and Validity Considerations

[Document observations related to the client's level of effort and engagement throughout the assessment. Note results of any specific performance validity measures administered. Describe any inconsistencies in performance that raise questions about validity or effort. Document any external factors that may have negatively impacted test performance unrelated to the client's cognitive abilities. Note any statements made by the client about their level of effort or difficulties maintaining engagement.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[client_s_self_report_during_testing] Client's Self-Report During Testing

[Document any spontaneous comments the client made about their performance or cognitive functions during the assessment. Note any cognitive symptoms or difficulties reported by the client during the testing session. Document the client's subjective experience of specific tests or cognitive domains. Note any connections the client made between test tasks and real-world difficulties they experience. Document any insights the client expressed about their cognitive strengths and weaknesses during the assessment.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[physical_and_sensory_observations] Physical and Sensory Observations

[Document any observations about the client's fine and gross motor functioning during the assessment. Note any tremors, coordination difficulties, or motor slowing observed. Describe any sensory difficulties observed or reported during testing. Document handedness and any ambidexterity observed. Note any pain behaviours or discomfort observed during the assessment. Document any fatigue or changes in energy level throughout the session.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[additional_clinical_observations] Additional Clinical Observations

[Document any observations about the client's emotional state throughout the assessment. Note any anxiety, frustration, apathy, irritability, or emotional lability observed. Describe any significant issues with arousal, alertness, or attention regulation. Document any unexpected events during the testing session. Note any other clinically significant observations not captured in previous sections.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[preliminary_impressions] Preliminary Impressions

[Document any preliminary clinical impressions reported by the clinician.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[nextsteps] Next Steps

[Document planned next steps, including additional testing sessions scheduled, specific domains requiring further assessment, or plans for the feedback session. Note any specific tests planned for future sessions based on observations from this session. Document any information or records requested from the client or their providers following this session. Note any immediate feedback or information provided to the client at the conclusion of this session. Document any referrals made based on observations from this session.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[summary] Session Summary

[Provide a concise summary of the assessment session in 3-4 sentences. Include the client's overall presentation, key tests administered, significant behavioural observations, and any critical next steps.]

title: Neuropsychology Feedback Session
description: For neuropsychologists providing feedback to a client following a comprehensive neuropsychological assessment. The template is designed to organise a
prompt:
(The template below is designed for neuropsychologists to document feedback sessions following neuropsychological assessment. This template will organise information about the client's response to assessment findings, their understanding of the results, plans for implementation of recommendations, and their mental status during the feedback session. Analyse the transcript and provide responses according to the instructions in the section headings below. Use professional, clinically appropriate language that is congruent with neurocognitive and neurodevelopmental disorders in the DSM-5-TR and ICD-11. Ensure that all information is attributed to its source, such as self-report, informant report, or clinician observation.)

[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)

[Provide a narrative summary of the client's mental status during the feedback session, taking into account: Appearance (the client's general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire); behaviour (the client's interaction during the session, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport); speech (the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the client's speech); mood and affect (the client's reported mood and emotional state, using their own words if possible, and the range and appropriateness of the client's emotional response during the appointment, noting any discrepancies with the client's stated mood; and any observations about emotional expression made by the clinician); thought process (the client's coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts); thought content (the content of the client's thoughts, noting the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any); perception (hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the client, if any); cognition (the client's orientation to time/place/person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension during the feedback session); and insight and judgment (the client's understanding of the assessment findings and their implications, noting any lack of awareness or denial).] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[summary_of_assessment_findings_discussed] Summary of Assessment Findings Discussed

[Summarise the key neuropsychological assessment findings and psychometric assessment results that were presented to the client during the feedback session. Include the main cognitive strengths and weaknesses identified, diagnostic impressions shared, and prognosis discussed. Detail how the findings were explained, including any analogies, visual aids, or other techniques used to facilitate understanding. Note any specific test results that were highlighted as particularly significant. Document how the findings relate to the client's presenting concerns and referral questions.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[reaction_and_understanding] Reaction and Understanding

[Describe the client's emotional and cognitive reactions to the assessment findings. Document their level of agreement or disagreement with the results and any questions or concerns they expressed. Note their understanding of the diagnostic impressions and cognitive profile, using their own words where possible. Detail any misconceptions that needed clarification during the session. Describe the client's level of insight into their cognitive strengths and weaknesses as demonstrated during the feedback discussion. Document any change in the client's perspective about their condition from pre-assessment to post-assessment.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[recommendations_discussed] Recommendations Discussed

[Detail the specific recommendations that were presented during the feedback session. For each recommendation, note how it was explained, the rationale provided, and the client's (and family's, if applicable) response. Categorise recommendations by type (e.g., medical follow-up, cognitive rehabilitation, educational/vocational accommodations, lifestyle modifications, support services). Document any prioritisation of recommendations discussed with the client. Note any potential barriers to implementation identified during the discussion and solutions proposed to address these barriers.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)
[Document the specific action steps agreed upon for implementing recommendations. Include who will be responsible for each step, anticipated timelines, and how progress will be monitored. Detail any referrals that were made during the session or that will be made as follow-up, including to whom and for what purpose. Note any resources (e.g., handouts, contact information for services, assistive technology options) that were provided during the session. Document any modifications made to the original recommendations based on discussion during the feedback session.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[report_distribution] Report Distribution

[Document to whom the written neuropsychological report will be sent and any specific consent obtained for information sharing. Note any discussions about confidentiality and limitations to confidentiality. Detail any sections of the report that were specifically reviewed verbatim during the session. Note any requests for additional copies or for the report to be sent to specific providers not originally identified.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[additional_support_provided] Additional Support Provided

[Describe any emotional support, validation, or counselling provided during the feedback session in response to the client's reactions. Document any psychoeducation provided about the client's condition, cognitive processes, or brain-behaviour relationships. Note any normalisation of the client's experiences or reassurance provided. Detail any hope-instilling statements or emphasis on strengths and resilience factors.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[follow_up_plans] Follow-up Plans

[Document arrangements for any planned follow-up appointments with the neuropsychologist. Note any agreed-upon check-ins to monitor progress with implementing recommendations. Detail plans for reassessment, if discussed, including approximate timeline and purpose. Document any contingency plans discussed for addressing new or worsening symptoms.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[client_s_questions_and_concerns] Client's Questions and Concerns

[List the specific questions asked by the client and the responses provided by the neuropsychologist. Document any concerns expressed by the client about the findings, recommendations, or future functioning.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[summary] Session Summary

[Provide a concise summary of the feedback session in 3-4 sentences. Include the client's overall response to the assessment findings, key recommendations discussed, the most significant action items agreed upon, and the general tone and productivity of the session.]

title: Neuropsychology Pre-Assessment Interview
description: For neuropsychologists interviewing a client as a part of a comprehensive neuropsychological assessment. The template is designed to organise
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by a neuropsychologist who is interviewing a client/patient as part of a comprehensive neuropsychological assessment. This template will organise the background information that is specifically relevant to understanding the client's neurocognitive functioning. Analyse the transcript and provide responses according to the instructions in the section headings below. Use professional, clinically appropriate language that is congruent with neurocognitive and neurodevelopmental disorders in the DSM-5-TR and ICD-11. Ensure that all information is attributed to its source, such as self-report, informant report, medical records, or previous assessment findings.)

[referral_reason_and_purpose_of_assessmen] Referral Reason and Purpose of Assessment

[Using paragraphs, describe the specific reasons for the neuropsychological assessment referral. Detail the referring provider's questions and concerns. Document any specific diagnostic questions to be addressed (e.g., dementia evaluation, post-concussion assessment, capacity evaluation). Note any legal, educational, vocational, or treatment planning purposes for the evaluation. Include any specific questions the client or family members hope to have answered through this assessment. Provide examples from the transcript that illustrate the key reasons for seeking assessment at this time.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)

[Provide a narrative summary of the client's mental status, and, where applicable, taking into account: Appearance (the client's general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire); behaviour (the client's interaction with their surrounds, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport); speech (the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the client's speech); mood and affect (the client's reported mood and emotional state, using their own words if possible, and the range and appropriateness of the client's emotional response during the appointment, noting any discrepancies with the client's stated mood; and any observations about emotional expression made by the clinician); thought process (the client's coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts); thought content (the content of the client's thoughts, noting the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any); perception (hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the client, if any); cognition (the client's orientation to time / place / person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension); and insight and judgment (the client's understanding of their mental health presentation and their decision-making capacity, noting any lack of awareness or denial).] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[presentation] Presenting Concerns

[Describe the primary cognitive, behavioral, emotional, or functional concerns that prompted the assessment as reported by the client. Document when these difficulties were first noticed and by whom. Detail the onset characteristics (sudden vs. gradual), progression pattern (stable, improving, worsening, fluctuating), and course over time. Note any precipitating events or circumstances associated with the onset of difficulties (e.g., head injury, medical event, emotional stressor). Describe the impact of these concerns on daily functioning, independence, work/school performance, and relationships. Provide specific examples from the transcript that illustrate the nature and severity of the current concerns.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[medical_and_psychiatric_history] Medical and Psychiatric History

[Using paragraphs, summarise the client’s medical and psychiatric history and highlight relevant diagnoses that could impact brain function or cognitive performance. Describe any reported neurological conditions or other health or mental health conditions. Include past and current treatments and interventions, medications, and substance use. Use subheadings for clarity and organisation.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[developmental_and_educational_history] Developmental and Educational History

[Describe the client's early development, including any birth complications, developmental delays, learning difficulties, or early interventions. Please include any history of early developmental trauma, family or domestic violence or emotional or physical abuse. Detail educational history, including highest level completed, academic performance, special education services, learning disabilities, behavioural issues in school, and educational accommodations. For older adults, focus on educational attainment and general academic functioning rather than detailed early developmental milestones unless they appear directly relevant to current presentation.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[occupational_history] Occupational History

[Document the client's work history, with emphasis on recent employment. Include types of positions held, duration of employment, job responsibilities (particularly cognitive demands), performance, and any work accommodations. Note any changes in work status, responsibilities, or performance related to cognitive or other health concerns. For retired individuals, include former occupation, when they retired, and reasons for retirement. For students or those who have not worked outside the home, adapt this section accordingly.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[family_history] Family History

[Describe any family history of neurological conditions (e.g., dementia, stroke, movement disorders), psychiatric conditions, developmental disorders, or substance use disorders, particularly among first-degree relatives. Note the specific relationship of affected family members to the client and their age of onset.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[psychosocial_history] Psychosocial History

[Document marital/relationship status, living situation, and social support system. Describe the quality and extent of social relationships and any changes in social functioning related to current concerns. Include information about significant life stressors, cultural factors, or other psychosocial elements that may impact functioning or assessment. Detail leisure activities, hobbies, and community involvement, noting any changes due to current difficulties.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[substance_use_history] Substance Use History

[Detail current and past patterns of alcohol, tobacco, caffeine, and other substance use. Document frequency, quantity, duration of use, and any periods of heavy use or abstinence. Note any history of substance-related cognitive changes, withdrawal symptoms, or treatment for substance use disorders. Include information about recent use prior to testing sessions.] (Only include if mentioned in a clinical note, transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank).

[previous_assessments_and_interventions] Previous Assessments and Interventions

[Describe any previous neuropsychological, psychological, cognitive, or medical evaluations relevant to the current concerns. Include dates, providers, specific tests administered, key findings, and diagnoses. Document any cognitive rehabilitation, therapy, or other interventions targeting cognitive functioning, including timing, duration, approach, and perceived effectiveness. Note baseline cognitive abilities if known from previous evaluations.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)

[current_coping_strategies] Current Coping Strategies

[Detail strategies the client currently uses to compensate for or manage cognitive difficulties. These might include external aids (calendars, reminders, notes), environmental modifications, reliance on others, cognitive strategies, or lifestyle adaptations. Document which strategies have been most and least helpful. Note the client's level of insight into their difficulties and adaptation to changes in functioning.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[client_s_goals_and_expectations] Client's Goals and Expectations

[Describe the client's understanding of the purpose of the neuropsychological assessment and their personal goals for the evaluation. Document specific questions or concerns they hope to have addressed. Note their expectations regarding diagnosis, prognosis, treatment recommendations, or other outcomes of the assessment. Describe their level of motivation and readiness for assessment and potential interventions. Write this as prose. Do not use bullet points.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[assessment_plan] Assessment Plan

[Detail the specific neuropsychological tests, questionnaires, and procedures planned for the evaluation based on the referral question and information gathered during the interview. Include the domains to be assessed (e.g., attention, memory, executive functioning, language, visuospatial abilities) and rationale for test selection. Note any planned modifications to standard administration procedures to accommodate sensory, motor, language, or other limitations.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[clinical_impressions_and_diagnostic_cons] Clinical Impressions and Diagnostic Considerations

[Provide preliminary diagnostic impressions based on the interview information within the framework of the DSM-5-TR and ICD-11. Discuss possible etiologies for the reported cognitive difficulties, considering neurological, medical, psychiatric, and psychosocial factors. Note differential diagnostic considerations that will need to be evaluated through formal testing. Identify factors that may influence test performance, such as medication effects, sleep problems, pain, anxiety, motivation, or effort. Document any areas requiring particular attention during the assessment process. Write this as prose. Do not use bullet points.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)

[nextsteps] Next Steps

(Create a list using dot points in this section. Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)
[Specify the scheduled dates and times for upcoming neuropsychological testing sessions.]
[Detail any additional records, previous test results, or collateral information to be obtained before completing the assessment.]
[List any recommendations made during the interview, such as medical referrals, immediate safety measures, or interim strategies to manage cognitive difficulties.]
[Specify any preparation instructions given to the client for upcoming testing sessions (e.g., medication timing, bringing glasses/hearing aids, ensuring adequate rest).]
[Note plans for the feedback session, including who will attend and materials to be prepared.]

[summary] Summary

[Provide a concise summary of the key points from the neuropsychological interview in no more than four sentences. Include the primary reason for referral, main presenting concerns, relevant history factors that may contribute to cognitive functioning, and the focus of the planned assessment.]

sections.label: Referral Reason and Purpose of Assessment

title: NSW SIRA (WorkCover) Allied Health Treatment Request (AHTR) Form
description: Allied health clinicians working in the New South Wales workers compensation system in Australia are required to complete and submit an Allied Health
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist allied health clinicians working within the New South Wales compensation system in Australia, which is regulated by the State Insurance Regulatory Authority, also known as SIRA. The template follows the format of the Allied Health Treatment Request form, which clinicians must complete and submit to the insurer after a course of treatment has been completed, and/or before a new course of treatment can commence. Please use a formal and professional tone in your output. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, contextual notes, and psychometric test results. You must strictly adhere to the following format:)

### SIRA Allied Health Treatment Request

**Request number:** [Print the number of the request. It will be one number greater than the most recently submitted Allied Health Treatment Request. If unknown, leave blank.]

**Date of request:** [Print the date in the format DD/MM/YYYY.]

**Date services first commenced:** [Print the date that the clinician provided initial treatment to the client in the format DD/MM/YYYY.]

**Total number of consultations to date:** [Print the total number of sessions / visits / consultations that the clinician has had with the client.]

**Your allied health discipline:** [Print the name of the allied health discipline that the clinician belongs to e.g., psychologist, physiotherapist, counsellor, occupational therapist, etc.]

**Referred by:** [Print the title, name, and organisation of the individual who referred this client to the clinician.]

**Phone number:** [Print the phone number of the individual who referred this client to the clinician.]

### Section 1: Injured person details

**Name:** [Print the client’s first and last name.]

**Date of birth:** [Print the client’s date of birth in the format DD/MM/YYYY.]

**Pre-injury occupation:** [Print the client’s pre-injury occupation.]

**Pre-injury work hours / week (average):** [Print the number of hours that the client worked each week prior to becoming injured.]

**Claim number:** [Print the client’s insurance claim number.]

**Date of injury / accident:** [Print the date of the client’s workplace injury in the format DD/MM/YYYY.]

### Section 2: Your clinical assessment

**Compensable injury illness**

[Describe the client’s workplace injury in one sentence.]

**Current clinical signs and symptoms**

[Describe the current psychological and physical symptoms the client is currently experiencing in relation to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### Risk screening

***Have you applied a risk screening tool in your assessment:***

[If the clinician has applied a risk screening tool in their assessment, print “Yes”. If the clinician has not applied a risk screening tool in their assessment, print “No”.]

***Name of risk screening tool:***

[If the clinician has applied a risk screening tool in their assessment, print the name of the risk screening tool. If more than one risk screening tool has been used, print the name of the tool that is most suitable and relevant to judging an individual's level of risk of suicide, self-harm, or violence perpetration.]

***Date administered:***

[If the clinician has applied a risk screening tool in their assessment, print the date that the assessment listed above was administered in the format DD/MM/YYYY.]

***Score / comment:***

[If the clinician has applied a risk screening tool in their assessment, print the score of that assessment, and provide a brief interpretation of that score in one sentence.]

***Details of any pre-existing conditions directly relevant to the compensable injury:***

[If applicable, describe any pre-existing conditions for the client that are directly relevant to their workplace injury. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

#### Capacity

***Do you have a copy of the position description / work duties (workers compensation and where relevant CTP):***

[If the clinician does have a copy of the position description / work duties, print “Yes”. If the clinician does not have a copy of the position description / work duties, print “No”.]

**Pre-injury capacity**

##### *Work:*

[Describe what the client did before the workplace injury with reference to their occupation. Describe the tasks and duties they performed as part of their occupation, and how many days or hours they worked each week. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

##### *Usual activities:*

[Describe what the client did before the workplace injury with reference to their activities of daily living e.g., driving, transport, leisure, self-grooming, domestic duties, social interaction, etc. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

**Current capacity**

##### *Work:*

[Make a statement about whether or not the client has current capacity to engage in any type of paid work. Describe what the client can do now since the workplace injury occurred, and following any treatment that has taken place. Describe the tasks and duties they can perform now as part of their occupation, and how many days or hours they can work now each week. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

##### *Usual activities:*

[Describe what the client can do now with reference to their activities of daily living e.g., driving, transport, leisure, self-grooming, domestic duties, social interaction, etc. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

#### Standardised Outcome Measures (SOM)

**Initial Score**

* [List the name, date of completion, and scores of initial psychometric testing first undertaken by the client. Print this information for each different measure.]

**Previous Score**

* [List the name, date of completion, and scores of psychometric testing that has been conducted on the most recent previous Allied Health Treatment Request. Print this information for each different measure.]

**Current Score**

* [List the name, date of completion, and scores of psychometric testing that has been conducted most recently. Print this information for each different measure.]

**Interpretation of scores**

[Provide an overall summary interpretation of all psychometric testing completed to date. Provide your response in less than three sentences.]

### Section 3: Barriers to recovery and strategies to address

***Barriers to recovery identified through your screening and assessment:***

[If applicable, describe any factors or barriers that are impeding the client’s recovery e.g., lack of social and family support; transport difficulties; family issues; addictions; issues with the client’s employer; and others. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

***Strategies to address barriers to recovery which may include actions to be taken by you/injured person, strategies agreed with others in treating team, referral to other services, etc:***

[If applicable, describe any recommendations for potential treatment or additional support that may assist the client to deal with any factors or barriers impeding their recovery e.g., mediation; referrals to other specialists or health organisations; support from community organisations; and others. Recommendations may include actions to be taken by the clinician, the client, and/or other members of the client’s treating team. Provide your response in less than three sentences.]

**Would you like any of the following assistance?**

***Direct contact from the insurer***

[If the clinician would like to have direct contact from the insurer, print “Yes”. If the clinician would not like direct contact from the insurer, print “No”.]

***Case conference***

[If the clinician would like to have a case conference, print “Yes”, and describe who the clinician would like to have a case conference with. If the clinician does not wish to have a case conference, print “No”.]

***Collaborative case review with an independent consultant***

[If the clinician would like to have a collaborative case review with an independent consultant, print “Yes”. If the clinician does not wish to have a collaborative case review with an independent consultant, print “No”.]

### Section 4: Treatment plan


***Has the injured person achieved the goals from the last treatment plan?***

[If the client has completely achieved the goals as listed on the last Allied Health Treatment Request, print “Yes”. If the client has not achieved any aspect of the goals as listed on their last Allied Health Treatment Plan, print “No”. If the client has made some achievements in relation to the goals as listed on their last Allied Health Treatment Plan, but not completely achieved their goals, print “Partially”. If this is the first Allied Health Treatment Request for this client, print “N/A”.]

**Injured person goals**

***Work goal***

[Describe the client’s goal in relation to work. If the client was not working at the time of the injury, describe the client’s goal in relation to activity or participation in some other area. The goal should be specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, and timed. The goal should have a specified target time for completion e.g., to be completed by a certain date, to be completed after a certain number of sessions, etc. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

***Activity or participation goal***

[Describe the client’s goal in relation to an activity or participation in some other area that is different from the work goal listed above. The goal should be specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, and timed. The goal should have a specified target time for completion e.g., to be completed by a certain date, to be completed after a certain number of sessions, etc. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

***Injured person’s self-management***

[Describe what techniques, strategies, activities and exercises the client is doing between treatment sessions. If applicable, reference these against any evidence-based therapy being conducted, e.g., cognitive-behaviour therapy, graded exposure, etc. Provide your response in less than three sentences.]

***Your intervention***

[Describe the clinician’s evidence-based treatment approach towards addressing the client’s symptoms and overall presentation e.g., trauma-informed CBT, EMDR, etc. Provide your response in one sentence.]

***Outline the rationale for the services you are requesting***

[Describe the rationale for using this type of intervention to treat the client’s symptoms and overall presentation. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

***How many additional sessions do you anticipate before discharge?***

[Provide the number of additional sessions that the clinician believes will be necessary before treatment can end for this client.]

***Anticipated discharge date***

[Provide the anticipated date that the clinician believes treatment may end for this client. Use the format DD/MM/YYYY. If no date has been provided by the clinician, leave blank.]

***If this date has changed since the last plan, please explain why***

[If applicable, describe why the anticipated date of discharge has changed since the last Allied Health Treatment Request. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

***Did you collaboratively develop this treatment plan with the injured person?***

[If the clinician has collaboratively developed this Allied Health Treatment Plan with the client, print “Yes”. If the clinician has not collaboratively developed this Allied Health Treatment Plan with the client, print “No”.]

***If no, explain why***

[If the clinician has not collaboratively developed this Allied Health Treatment Plan with the client, provide an explanation of why this is the case. Provide your response in less than two sentences.]

### Section 5: Service requested

* [List the type of service, consultation, or equipment being requested by the clinician e.g., psychological treatment, report writing, case conference, communication aid, etc. List the number of sessions, hours, or items being requested by the clinician for each request. List the frequency / timeframe of each request e.g., weekly, fortnightly, as required, etc.]

### Section 6: Your details


**Treating practitioner name**

[Provide the clinician’s title and first and last name.]

**Practice email**

[Provide the contact email address for the clinician’s practice or company.]

**AHPRA number**

[Provide the clinician’s Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency number.]

**Best time/day to contact**

[Provide the best times and the best days for the clinician to be contacted in relation to the Allied Health Treatment Request if required.]

**Practice name**

[Provide the clinician’s practice or company name.]

**SIRA approval number**

[Provide the clinician’s SIRA approval number.]

**Suburb**

[Provide the name of the suburb where the clinician normally practices.]

**State**

[Provide the Australian state where the clinician normally practices.]

**Postcode**

[Provide the clinician’s postcode.]

**Treating practitioner email**

[Provide the clinician’s direct email address.]

**Phone number**

[Provide the clinician’s phone number.]

**Fax**

[Provide the clinician’s fax number.]

title: NZ ACC Completion Report (8544)
description: Once treatment under the ACC (NZ) has concluded, a Completion Report is completed by clinicians in collaboration with the kiritaki (client). It
prompt: (This template is designed to assist mental health clinicians working within New Zealand's ACC to prepare a Completion Report (form ACC8544). This report is completed in collaboration with the kiritaki (client) once treatment under the ACC has been completed. The report must be written in formal, professional clinical language. Base the report only on information that is contained in the transcripts, session notes, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. Refer to the client/kiritaki by their name in the report. You must strictly adhere to the following format:) [s_1_kiritaki_details] 1. Kiritaki Details Kiritaki name: [Print the kiritaki/client's full name. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Date of birth: [Print the kiritaki/client's date of birth. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Claim number: [Print the ACC claim number. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Contact details/safe contact where appropriate: [Print contact details exactly as documented, including any safe contact instructions. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Service being completed: [Print "Short-term Support to Wellbeing" or "Tailored Support to Wellbeing" based on the documented information. If not documented, print "Not documented."] [s_2_current_situation_and_impacts] 2. Current Situation and Impacts (The purpose of this section is to provide an understanding of the current situation and clinical presentation of the kiritaki/client, placing their treatment into the context of their current circumstances. Focus on changes that have occurred since the previous report. If there have been no changes, print "N/A".) **a. Changes to presentation:** [Describe any changes to the kiritaki/client's presentation since the previous report. Include changes in the intensity or frequency of mental injury symptoms, the current emotional, behavioural, and social functioning of the kiritaki/client, any vulnerability factors (e.g., socio-economic factors, disability, mental health factors), and any changes to medication and overall health and wellbeing. Important: Do not report psychometric assessment results in this field — these should be reported in Section 3 alongside the relevant recovery goals.] **b. Changes to life situation:** [Describe any changes to the kiritaki/client's life situation since the previous report. Include information on psychosocial stressors and any impact on engagement or progress in treatment (e.g., worsening chronic illness, bereavement, other loss, legal proceedings, financial stress, unresolved workplace matters). Also note any developing strengths or protective factors. For children and young people, include any changes of school, caregiver, or significant routine.] Have there been any developments of risk to the kiritaki from themselves or others, or from the kiritaki to others, since the previous report? [State "Yes" or "No" based on the documented information. If not documented, print "Not documented."] [If yes, describe the risk, any duty of care actions taken, and how the risk will be managed, based on the documented information.] **c. Changes affecting access to services:** [Describe any changes that have affected the kiritaki/client's access to services since the previous report. Include changes that have impacted previously identified barriers and any additional access barriers the kiritaki/client is currently experiencing.] Changes to non-ACC supports: [List any changes to non-ACC supports involved in the recovery and care of the kiritaki/client since the previous report (e.g., general practitioner, Mental Health and Addiction Services, Oranga Tamariki). Use the format below for each changed or new support.] Name: [Insert if documented.] Role/function: [Insert if documented.]  Community organisation: [Insert if documented.] [Repeat for each additional support if documented.] [s_3_review_of_recovery_goals] 3. Review of Recovery Goals (Complete the information below for all recovery goals identified in the previous report. Note that if the kiritaki/client has the knowledge and skills needed to achieve the goal but does not use them, the barrier getting in the way becomes the updated recovery goal. If further recovery goals are identified, add them below.) [outcome_of_recovery_goal_1] Outcome of recovery goal 1 Goal description: [State the goal exactly as documented in the previous report. If not documented, print "Not documented."] What progress has been made towards achievement of this goal? * Clinical progress: [Describe any progress that has been made towards achievement of this goal. Examples include reduction in symptoms or improvement in the functioning of the kiritaki/client.] * Outcome measures: [Include any psychometric assessment results relevant to this goal. For each, print the name of the measure, date of completion, score, and a brief interpretation. If no psychometric measures are relevant to this goal, print "None administered."] If the expected progress towards this goal has not been made, describe the circumstances contributing to this: [Only complete this field if progress has not been made as expected. Describe only documented reasons. If progress is on track or this is not applicable, print "Not applicable."] Status: [Print one of the following based only on the documented information: "Not achieved" / "Partly achieved" / "Satisfactorily achieved". If not documented, print "Not documented."] [outcome_of_recovery_goal_2] Outcome of recovery goal 2 Goal description: [State the goal exactly as documented in the previous report. If not documented, print "Not documented."] What progress has been made towards achievement of this goal? * Clinical progress: [Describe any progress that has been made towards achievement of this goal. Examples include reduction in symptoms or improvement in the functioning of the kiritaki/client.] * Outcome measures: [Include any psychometric assessment results relevant to this goal. For each, print the name of the measure, date of completion, score, and a brief interpretation. If no psychometric measures are relevant to this goal, print "None administered."] If the expected progress towards this goal has not been made, describe the circumstances contributing to this: [Only complete this field if progress has not been made as expected. Describe only documented reasons. If progress is on track or this is not applicable, print "Not applicable."] Status: [Print one of the following based only on the documented information: "Not achieved" / "Partly achieved" / "Satisfactorily achieved". If not documented, print "Not documented."] [Repeat the above structure for each additional recovery goal documented in the previous report.] [additional_recovery_goal_1] Additional recovery goal 1 (Only complete this section if additional or new goals have been identified since the previous report. Recovery goals must be SMART (Specific, Measurable, Action-oriented, Realistic, and Time-bound), meaningful to the kiritaki/client, relevant to their recovery, and building progress towards the broad treatment outcome. If no new goals have been identified, omit this section and do not print the heading.) Goal description: [State the goal] How will the outcomes of this goal improve kiritaki/client functioning? [Describe what the kiritaki/client will be able to do differently or better as a result of achieving this goal.] How will this goal be achieved? [Specify the services needed and how they will be used to achieve this goal.] How will progress towards this goal be measured? [State the documented measures that will be used to track progress. ] Who will deliver the services to support this goal, and what is the expected timeframe? [Insert the provider and expected timeframe.] [Repeat the above structure for each additional new recovery goal identified.] [s_4_next_steps] 4. Next Steps Self-management plan: [Briefly describe the self-management plan developed with the kiritaki/client to maintain their treatment progress independently. Include any documented coping strategies, self-management skills, warning signs or relapse indicators identified, and any follow-up supports or referrals recommended.] [s_5_other_information] 5. Other Information Date of last face-to-face meeting with the kiritaki that informed this report: [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Other relevant information: [Include any other documented information considered relevant to the kiritaki/client's ongoing wellbeing and recovery. If nothing further to report, print "No further information."] Final outcome measures completed with the kiritaki: [State "Yes" or "No" based on the session notes, contextual notes and transcripts. If not documented, print "Not documented."] [If no, state the documented reason why the final outcome measures were not completed. If not documented or not applicable, print "Not applicable."] [s_6_provider_declaration] 6. Provider Declaration Note to clinician: The following declarations must be completed manually on the official ACC8544 form by checking the relevant boxes: ☐ I have informed the kiritaki/guardian/safe contact/whānau that the information collected for this report will be sent to ACC to update on progress and completion of service. I have kiritaki/guardian/whānau authority for this. ☐ I confirm that the information contained in this report is accurate and that I have followed the standards set out in the Sensitive Claims Service operational guidelines. Lead Service Provider name: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Provider ID: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Supplier name: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Supplier ID: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."] Date: [Print today's date in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

title: NZ ACC Progress Report (8542)
description: A Progress Report is completed by the Lead Service Provider in collaboration with the kiritaki (client) when reporting on progress since the Cover
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist mental health clinicians in preparing a Progress Report (ACC form ACC8542). This report is completed in collaboration with the kiritaki (client) when reporting on progress since the Cover and Wellbeing Plan or Wellbeing Plan. The report must be written in formal, professional clinical language. Base the report only on information that is contained in the transcripts, session notes, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. Refer to the client/kiritaki by their name in the report. You must strictly adhere to the following format:)

[s_1_kiritaki_details] 1. Kiritaki Details

Kiritaki name: [Print the kiritaki/client's full name. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Date of birth: [Print the kiritaki/client's date of birth. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Claim number: [Print the ACC claim number. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Contact details/safe contact where appropriate: [Print contact details exactly as documented, including any safe contact instructions. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

[s_2_current_situation_and_impacts] 2. Current Situation and Impacts

(The purpose of this section is to provide an understanding of the current situation and clinical presentation of the kiritaki/client, placing their treatment into the context of their current circumstances. Focus on changes that have occurred since the previous report. If there have been no changes, print "N/A".)

**a. Changes to presentation:** [Describe any changes to the kiritaki/client's presentation since the previous report. Include changes in the intensity or frequency of mental injury symptoms, the current emotional, behavioural, and social functioning of the kiritaki/client, any vulnerability factors (e.g., socio-economic factors, disability, mental health factors), and any changes to medication and overall health and wellbeing. Important: Do not report psychometric assessment results in this field — these should be reported in Section 3 alongside the relevant recovery goals.]

**b. Changes to life situation:** [Describe any changes to the kiritaki/client's life situation since the previous report. Include information on psychosocial stressors and any impact on engagement or progress in treatment (e.g., worsening chronic illness, bereavement, other loss, legal proceedings, financial stress, unresolved workplace matters). Also note any developing strengths or protective factors. For children and young people, include any changes of school, caregiver, or significant routine.]

Have there been any developments of risk to the kiritaki from themselves or others, or from the kiritaki to others, since the previous report? [State "Yes" or "No" based on the documented information. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
[If yes, describe the risk, any duty of care actions taken, and how the risk will be managed, based on the documented information.]

**c. Changes affecting access to services:** [Describe any changes that have affected the kiritaki/client's access to services since the previous report. Include changes that have impacted previously identified barriers and any additional access barriers the kiritaki/client is currently experiencing.]

Changes to non-ACC supports: [List any changes to non-ACC supports involved in the recovery and care of the kiritaki/client since the previous report (e.g., general practitioner, Mental Health and Addiction Services, Oranga Tamariki). Use the format below for each changed or new support.]
Name: [Insert if documented.]
Role/function: [Insert if documented.] 
Community organisation: [Insert if documented.]
[Repeat for each additional support if documented.]

[s_3_review_of_recovery_goals] 3. Review of Recovery Goals

(Complete the information below for all goals listed in the Wellbeing Plan. Note that if the kiritaki/client has the knowledge and skills needed to achieve the goal but does not use them, the barrier getting in the way becomes the updated recovery goal. If further recovery goals are identified, add them in Section 4.)

[review_of_recovery_goal_1] Review of recovery goal 1
Goal description: [State the goal exactly as documented in the Wellbeing Plan. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
What progress has been made towards achievement of this goal?

* Clinical Progress: [Describe any progress that has been made towards achievement of this goal. Examples include reduction in symptoms or improvement in the functioning of the kiritaki/client.]
* Outcome Measures [Include any psychometric assessment results relevant to this goal. For each, print the name of the measure, date of completion, score, and a brief interpretation. If no psychometric measures are relevant to this goal, print "None administered."]

If the expected progress towards this goal has not been made, describe the circumstances contributing to this: [Only complete this field if progress has not been made as expected. Describe only documented reasons. If progress is on track or this is not applicable, print "Not applicable."]
What are the next steps towards achieving this goal? [Describe the documented next steps.]

[review_of_recovery_goal_2] Review of recovery goal 2
Goal description: [State the goal exactly as documented in the Wellbeing Plan. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
What progress has been made towards achievement of this goal?

* Clinical Progress: [Describe any progress that has been made towards achievement of this goal. Examples include reduction in symptoms or improvement in the functioning of the kiritaki/client.]
* Outcome Measures [Include any psychometric assessment results relevant to this goal. For each, print the name of the measure, date of completion, score, and a brief interpretation. If no psychometric measures are relevant to this goal, print "None administered."]

If the expected progress towards this goal has not been made, describe the circumstances contributing to this: [Only complete this field if progress has not been made as expected. Describe only documented reasons. If progress is on track or this is not applicable, print "Not applicable."]
What are the next steps towards achieving this goal? [Describe the documented next steps.]

[Repeat the above structure for each additional recovery goal documented in the Wellbeing Plan.]

[s_4_addition_of_new_recovery_goals] 4. Addition of New Recovery Goals

(Only complete this section if new recovery goals have been developed with the kiritaki/client since the Wellbeing Plan. Recovery goals must be SMART (Specific, Measurable, Action-oriented, Realistic, and Time-bound), meaningful to the kiritaki/client, relevant to their recovery, and building progress towards the broad treatment outcome. Avoid goals that cannot be achieved within the duration of the requested Tailored Support to Wellbeing package. If no new goals have been identified, leave this section blank.)

[additional_recovery_goal_1] Additional recovery goal 1
Goal description: [State the goal]
How will the outcomes of this goal improve kiritaki/client functioning? [Describe what the kiritaki/client will be able to do differently or better as a result of achieving this goal.]
How will this goal be achieved? [Specify the services needed and how they will be used to achieve this goal.]
How will progress towards this goal be measured? [State the documented measures that will be used to track progress. ]
Who will deliver the services to support this goal, and what is the expected timeframe? [Insert the provider and expected timeframe.]

[Repeat the above structure for each additional new recovery goal identified.]

[s_5_planned_services_and_the_providers_who] 5. Planned Services and the Providers Who Will Deliver These

(Only complete this section if the clinician is requesting additional services or hours beyond those currently approved. List the services required under Tailored Support to Wellbeing Package A or B and any other ACC services to meet the recovery goals listed above. If no additional services are being requested by the clinician, leave this section blank.)

[tailored_support_to_wellbeing] Tailored Support to Wellbeing
Service: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider name / ACC ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider discipline: [Insert if documented.] 
Supplier / Supplier ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Hours: [Insert if documented.]
[Repeat for each additional Tailored Support to Wellbeing service if documented.]
Total hours of Tailored Support to Wellbeing: [Insert total. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

[group_based_therapy] Group-based Therapy
Service: [Insert if documented. If none, print "None."] 
Provider name / ACC ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider discipline: [Insert if documented.] 
Supplier / Supplier ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Hours: [Insert if documented.]
Expected start and end dates of Group-based Therapy: [Insert if documented. If not applicable, print "Not applicable."]

[other_acc_services] Other ACC Services
Service: [Insert if documented. If none, print "None."] 
Provider name / ACC ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider discipline: [Insert if documented.] 
Supplier / Supplier ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Hours: [Insert if documented.]
Total expected package duration of all services listed above: [Insert if documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
If other ACC services have been listed above, please describe how these services will support the recovery of the kiritaki/client: [Describe the documented rationale for any other ACC services listed. If no other ACC services are listed, leave this blank.]

[s_6_other_information] 6. Other Information

Date of last face-to-face meeting with the kiritaki that informed this report: [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Date of disengagement by the kiritaki (if applicable): [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not applicable or not documented, print "Not applicable."]
Other relevant information: [Include any other documented information considered relevant to assist in the recovery of the kiritaki/client. If nothing further to report, print "No further information."]

[s_7_provider_declaration] 7. Provider Declaration

Note to clinician: The following declarations must be completed manually on the official ACC8542 form by checking the relevant boxes:
☐ I have informed the kiritaki/guardian/safe contact/whānau that the information collected for this report will be sent to ACC to support decisions on treatment and rehabilitation needs. I have kiritaki/guardian/whānau authority for this.
☐ I confirm that the information contained in this report is accurate and that I have followed the standards set out in the Sensitive Claims Service operational guidelines.
Lead Service Provider name: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Provider ID: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Supplier name: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Supplier ID: [Print exactly as documented. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Date: [Print today's date in DD/MM/YYYY format.]
