title: NZ ACC Specialist Cover Assessment - Adult (8536)
description: Within New Zealand's ACC Sensitive Claims Service, mental health practitioners may be engaged to complete a Specialist Cover Assessment (ACC8536) to
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist mental health clinicians working within New Zealand's ACC Sensitive Claims Service to prepare a Specialist Cover Assessment report (ACC form ACC8536). The purpose of this report is to support ACC's cover determination for mental injury arising from a Schedule 3 event (sexual abuse or assault). The report must be written in formal, professional clinical language suitable for a report. Clearly distinguish throughout between reported history, observed information, psychometric findings, and clinical opinion. Do not speculate beyond the documented evidence. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, session notes, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. Refer to the client/kiritaki by their name in the report. You must strictly adhere to the following format:)

[part_a_kiritaki_information] Part A: Kiritaki Information

[s_1_kiritaki_details] 1. Kiritaki Details

Kiritaki name: [Print the kiritaki/client's full name. If not documented, print "Not documented."] 
Date of birth: [Print the kiritaki/client's date of birth. If not documented, print "Not documented."] 
Claim number: [Print the ACC claim number. If not documented, print "Not documented."] 
Contact details/safe contact where appropriate: [Print contact details. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

[part_b_specialist_cover_assessment] Part B: Specialist Cover Assessment

[s_2_sources_of_information] 2. Sources of Information

1. Dates and duration of consultations: [List each session with the client conducted as part of this assessment, including the date and duration of each.]
2. Sources of information collected, received, and considered (include dates and authors): [Using bullet points, list all sources of information reviewed in preparing this report. This may include session notes, clinical records, psychometric assessment results, collateral information, referral documentation, prior ACC Sensitive Claims Service reports, and any other relevant documents. Include dates and authors for each source where documented.]

[s_3_about_the_schedule_3_events] 3. About the Schedule 3 Events

[For each Schedule 3 event, provide a clear, concise and specific account of what occurred. Do not use broad or vague terms such as "sexual abuse" or "inappropriate touch" — describe the specific nature of the event using clear clinical language. For each event include: the specific acts that occurred; the date or date range of the event(s); the frequency of the event(s); and the perpetrator's relationship to the kiritaki/client if known. Refer to third parties by their relationship to the kiritaki/client rather than by name. If sufficient detail has been provided in available Sensitive Claims Service reports already reviewed, and no additional information is required, state that clearly and adopt that information here.]

[s_4_background_kiritaki_information] 4. Background Kiritaki Information

a. Background summary: [Provide a concise summary of the kiritaki/client's relevant background. Include the following where documented: relevant medical history (illnesses, operations, hospitalisations); cultural and spiritual background; family/whānau and personal history; developmental history; education and employment history; alcohol and other drug history; and forensic history.]

b. Summary of the current circumstances and presenting difficulties of the kiritaki.
Current circumstances: [Describe the kiritaki/client's current living situation, relational circumstances, occupational status, social circumstances, financial situation, and any other practical circumstances relevant to this assessment. Provide your response in less than 5 sentences]
Presenting difficulties: [Describe the kiritaki/client's current emotional, cognitive, behavioural, relational, and functional difficulties in less than 5 sentences.]

c. Summary of the mental health history of the kiritaki and any treatment delivered for historical and current presenting difficulties.
Mental health history: [Summarise the kiritaki/client's past and current mental health history, including previous diagnoses, episodes of difficulty, and relevant clinical contacts. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]
Treatment delivered: [Summarise psychological and other treatments received by the kiritaki/client, both historically and currently.  Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

d. If the kiritaki has received treatment from another health provider for this condition, provide a contact name and either email address or phone number for each provider.
Other treating health providers: [If the kiritaki/client has received treatment from another health provider for this condition, list each provider below. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note, otherwise print "Not documented.")] Contact name: [Insert if documented.] 
Contact email or phone: \[Insert if documented\.\] \[Repeat for each additional provider if documented\.\]

e. Current medications and dosages, including the names of prescribers: [List all current medications, dosages, and the name of each prescriber as documented. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note, otherwise print "Not documented.")]

[s_5_strengths_and_vulnerabilities] 5. Strengths and Vulnerabilities

a. Personality assessment: [Provide a careful, evidence-based description of personality patterns relevant to the kiritaki/client's strength and vulnerability presentation and formulation, using only information supported by the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical notes. Note that personality traits do not need to reach the clinical threshold for a diagnosis to be relevant here. Where a comprehensive personality assessment including psychometrics has been conducted, summarise the findings. Provide your response in fewer than 6 sentences.]

b. Kiritaki strengths and protective factors: [Describe factors that may support the kiritaki/client's recovery, which may include but are not limited to: cultural or spiritual identity; significant relationships; family/whānau connectedness; employment; insight; motivation; and community support. Only include factors that are documented. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

c. Areas of vulnerability: [Describe aspects of vulnerability that may affect the kiritaki/client's ability or capacity to engage in or participate effectively in treatment. Vulnerabilities may include disability, socio-economic factors, age, housing arrangements, social supports, mental health factors, safety concerns, or instability. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

d. Are there any areas of risk? [State "Yes" or "No" based on the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
[If yes, describe each identified risk below, including: risk to self; risk posed to others; and risk of harm to the kiritaki/client from others. Describe any duty of care actions that have been taken as relevant to the assessment. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note, otherwise print "Not documented.")]

e. Are any other agencies currently involved in supporting the kiritaki? [State "Yes" or "No" based on the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
[If yes, list the agencies currently involved in supporting the kiritaki/client, for example Community Mental Health and Addiction Services. based on the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

[s_6_psychometrics] 6. Psychometrics

Results of psychometric testing, if relevant, and symptom validity measures:
[For each psychometric measure administered, print the following on a separate line using the format below. If no psychometric assessments have been administered or documented, print "No psychometric assessments are documented."
Name of measure: [Print name of the measure.] 
Date administered: [Print date administered in DD/MM/YYYY format.] 
Respondents: [Insert who completed the measure, e.g., "Kiritaki (self-report)", "Clinician-rated".] 
Rating: [Insert score, range, percentile, or clinical classification exactly as documented.]
Repeat for each measure administered.]
Comment on results: [Provide a concise, evidence-based interpretation of the psychometric findings as a whole, drawing only on the documented results above. Note any clinically significant findings, areas of consistency or discrepancy across measures, and the relevance of the results to the diagnostic formulation. Provide your response in fewer than 6 sentences. If no psychometric results are documented, print "Not documented."]

[s_7_clinical_symptoms_or_diagnosis] 7. Clinical Symptoms or Diagnosis

1\. Diagnosis: \[For each documented diagnosis\, state the full diagnosis\, the diagnostic system used\, the relevant diagnostic code\, and the severity level where applicable \(e\.g\.\, DSM\-5\-TR Major Depressive Disorder\, Moderate 296\.32\)\. Explain how the diagnostic criteria are met with reference to the kiritaki/client's documented symptom presentation\. All diagnoses should be identified\, including those not being proposed as mental injuries caused by a Schedule 3 event\. If no formal diagnosis has been documented\, complete the Significant Difficulties section below instead or in addition\.\]

2\. Significant difficulties: \[Describe any emotional\, cognitive\, behavioural\, or social difficulties of clinical significance that are documented but may not meet the full threshold for a specific DSM\-5\-TR or ICD\-11 diagnosis\. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences\.\]

Formulation and summary: [Provide a clear, evidence-based clinical formulation explaining how the kiritaki/client has developed their presenting difficulties. The formulation should draw together the kiritaki/client's background, the Schedule 3 events, relevant vulnerabilities and protective factors, and the current symptom presentation to explain the development and maintenance of the difficulties proposed as mental injury. Address any other relevant difficulties not proposed as mental injury. Provide your response in fewer than 6 sentences.]

[s_8_opinion_on_mental_injury] 8. Opinion on Mental Injury

a. Causal relationship - Schedule 3 events and proposed mental injuries: [Describe the clinical evidence relevant to the question of whether a causal relationship exists between the Schedule 3 events described in Section 3 and the mental injuries proposed above. Present this as clinical opinion, clearly attributing reasoning to the evidence (from the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical notes) rather than stating causal conclusions as fact. Do not speculate beyond the documented evidence. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

b. Causal relationship - other life factors and proposed mental injuries: [Identify and describe the clinical evidence regarding the contribution of other life factors (i.e., factors other than the Schedule 3 events) to the proposed mental injuries. Only include factors that are documented in the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical notes. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences. If no other contributing factors are documented, print "Not documented."]

*Date mental injury suffered* 
[For each proposed mental injury, state the diagnosis or proposed mental injury and the date or time period when it is likely to have reached diagnostic threshold. Use the format below. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical notes, otherwise print "Not documented.")]
Proposed mental injury: [Insert diagnosis or proposed mental injury.] 
Date: [Insert date or time period, e.g., "approximately [month/year]" or "by [date]".]
[Repeat for each proposed mental injury.]
Clinical rationale for dates above: [Summarise the clinical reasoning and evidence base for each date stated above, concisely. This must be grounded in the documented history, symptom progression, and available clinical information.]

[s_9_treatment] 9. Treatment

[Outline recommendations for treatment for the kiritaki/client and for service providers to consider. Structure recommendations as follows, where applicable:
For proposed mental injuries: recommend evidence-based therapeutic modalities, additional supports, and relevant services available under the ACC Sensitive Claims Service.
For other non-injury difficulties: recommend appropriate assessment pathways and non-ACC services where relevant.]

[s_10_prognosis] 10. Prognosis

[Provide a measured, evidence-based prognosis for the proposed mental injury or injuries. Include the likely trajectory of recovery with appropriate treatment. Note any potential barriers to treatment and recovery, including psychological, practical, relational, or systemic factors. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

[part_c_function_assessment] Part C: Function Assessment

Note: Only include Part C if ACC has given explicit prior approval for a Function Assessment to be completed as part of this Specialist Cover Assessment. If no such approval is documented in the referral or available materials, omit Part C entirely.

[s_11_current_and_ongoing_functional_effect] 11. Current and Ongoing Functional Effects

a. Symptoms of the proposed or covered mental injuries: [Describe the specific symptoms of the proposed or covered mental injuries as documented. Provide your answer in fewer than 3 sentences]

b. Effects of these symptoms and their treatment on the current functioning of the kiritaki: [Describe the effects of these symptoms and their treatment on the kiritaki/client's current day-to-day functioning. Provide specific documented examples of impact on activities of daily living (e.g., self-care, domestic tasks, community participation, transport). Provide your answer in fewer than 4 sentences]

c. Effects that may impact on the work activity of the kiritaki not already outlined: [Provide specific documented examples of how the symptoms and their treatment affect the kiritaki/client's capacity for work activity that have not already been addressed above. Provide your answer in fewer than 3 sentences.]

d. Safety concerns as a barrier to work: [Identify any potential safety concerns for the kiritaki/client or others arising from the functional effects described above that may constitute a barrier to work. Provide specific examples and clinical rationale. Provide your answer in fewer than 3 sentences. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

e. Comorbid conditions affecting functioning: [Describe any comorbid conditions that affect the kiritaki/client's current functioning and may impact on activities of daily living or work activity. Provide your answer in fewer than 3 sentences. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

f. Material contribution of mental injuries to functional impact: [If the impact on functioning is multifactorial, state whether the symptoms of the proposed or covered mental injuries are materially contributing to this impact.]

g. Impact on specific work types in the referral: [If specific work types were identified in the referral for consideration, summarise any relevant comments about the impact of the proposed or covered mental injuries and their treatment on those specific work types. If not referenced in the referral or not documented, print "Not documented."]

h. Recommendations to support return to work: [Summarise the clinician’s recommendations to reduce the adverse effects of the proposed or covered injuries or other relevant psychiatric conditions that may support the kiritaki/client to return to work. This may include, for example, workplace support, modified work hours or patterns, or avoidance of specific workplace exposures that may function as triggers.]

[s_12_effects_on_past_functioning] 12. Effects on Past Functioning

[Summarise the evidence of functional effects of the covered mental injuries, or their treatment, that were likely a barrier to work in the past. For each period identified, explain why the functional effects would have impacted on work activity and state the dates when these occurred. Provide your answer in fewer than 5 sentences. If no such evidence is documented, print "Not documented."]

[part_d_other_information_and_declaration] Part D: Other Information and Declarations

[s_13_other_information] 13. Other Information

Date of last face-to-face meeting with the kiritaki that informed this report: [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Date of disengagement by the kiritaki (if applicable): [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not applicable or not documented, print "Not applicable."]
Other relevant information: [Include any other documented information considered relevant to assist ACC in determining cover or to support the recovery of the kiritaki/client. If nothing further to report, print "No further information."]
Attached documents: [List any documents attached to this report, such as clinical reports or psychometric results. If none, print "No documents attached."]
Other providers who contributed to the assessment: Contact name: \[Insert if documented\.\] Contact email: \[Insert if documented\.\] \[Repeat for each additional provider if documented\.\]

[s_14_provider_declaration] 14. Provider Declaration

Note to clinician: The following declarations must be completed manually on the official ACC8536 form by checking the relevant boxes. Please confirm each of the following with your kiritaki before submission:
☐ I have explained to the kiritaki that ACC will send a copy of this report to their Lead Service Provider (if relevant). 
☐ The kiritaki would like ACC to send them a copy of this report. 
☐ I have explained to the kiritaki that they can participate in a feedback session before this report is submitted to ACC. 
☐ I have informed the kiritaki that the information collected for this report will be sent to ACC to support cover decisions and treatment and rehabilitation needs. I have kiritaki authority for this. 
☐ I confirm that the information contained in this report is accurate and that I have followed the standards set out in the Sensitive Claims Service operational guidelines.

The kiritaki: 
☐ Participated in the feedback session. 
☐ Did not participate in the feedback session.
(If the kiritaki did not participate in the feedback session, state the reason below.) Reason for non-participation: [Insert if documented. If not applicable, print "Not applicable."]

Assessment Provider name and profession: [Print exactly as documented.] 
Provider ID: [Print clinician’s name] 
Supplier name: [Print exactly as documented.] 
Supplier ID: [Print exactly as documented.] 
Date: [Print today's date in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

sections.label: Part D: Other Information and Declarations

title: NZ ACC Wellbeing Plan (8541)
description: A Wellbeing Plan (ACC8541) is completed by clinicians in collaboration with the kiritaki (client) when planning their recovery from a covered mental
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist mental health clinicians working within New Zealand's ACC Sensitive Claims Service to prepare a Wellbeing Plan (ACC form ACC8541). This report is completed in collaboration with the kiritaki (client) when planning recovery from a covered mental injury. The report must be written in formal, professional clinical language. Base the report only on information that is contained in the transcripts, session notes, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. Refer to the client/kiritaki by their name in the report. You must strictly adhere to the following format:)

[s_1_kiritaki_details] 1. Kiritaki Details

Kiritaki name: [Print the kiritaki/client's full name. If not documented, print "Not documented."] 
Date of birth: [Print the kiritaki/client's date of birth. If not documented, print "Not documented."] 
Claim number: [Print the ACC claim number. If not documented, print "Not documented."] 
Contact details/safe contact where appropriate: [Print contact details. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

[s_2_current_situation_and_impacts] 2. Current Situation and Impacts

(The purpose of this section is to provide an understanding of the current situation and clinical presentation of the kiritaki/client, placing their treatment into the context of their current circumstances. Focus on changes that have occurred since the previous report. If there have been no changes, print "N/A".)

**a. Changes to presentation:** [Describe any changes to the kiritaki/client's presentation since the previous report. Include changes in the intensity or frequency of mental injury symptoms, the current emotional, behavioural, and social functioning of the kiritaki/client, any vulnerability factors (e.g., socio-economic factors, disability, mental health factors), and any changes to medication and overall health and wellbeing. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

**b. Changes to life situation:** [Describe any changes to the kiritaki/client's life situation since the previous report. Include information on psychosocial stressors and any impact on engagement or progress in treatment (e.g., worsening chronic illness, bereavement, other loss). Also note any developing strengths or protective factors. For children and young people, include any changes of school, caregiver, or significant routine. Provide your response in fewer than 4 sentences.]

Have there been any developments of risk to the kiritaki from themselves or others, or from the kiritaki to others since the previous report? [State "Yes" or "No" based on the documented information. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
[If yes, describe the risk, any duty of care actions taken, and how the risk will be managed.]

**c. Changes affecting access to services:** [Describe any changes that have affected the kiritaki/client's access to services since the previous report. Include changes that have impacted previously identified barriers and any additional access barriers the kiritaki/client is currently experiencing. Provide your response in fewer than 3 sentences.]

Changes to non-ACC supports: [List any changes to non-ACC supports involved in the recovery and care of the kiritaki/client since the previous report (e.g., general practitioner, Mental Health and Addiction Services, Oranga Tamariki). Use the format below for each changed or new support. If no changes are documented, print "N/A".]
Name: [Insert if documented.] 
Role/function: [Insert if documented.] 
Community organisation: [Insert if documented.]
[Repeat for each additional support if documented.]

[s_3_treatment] 3. Treatment

**a. Tailored treatment needs:** [Describe what treatment is needed to address the kiritaki/client's symptoms and functional difficulties. Include: what symptoms are currently impacting the kiritaki/client's functioning and quality of life; the supports needed to address these symptoms and improve functioning and quality of life (e.g., GP input for medication, community group engagement, alternative housing); and the cultural and spiritual needs identified in the Early Supports Plan and how these will be met within the treatment plan. Provide your response in fewer than 5 sentences.]

**b. Broad treatment outcomes:** [Describe what the kiritaki/client would like to be able to do differently or better after successful mental injury support and treatment, and how they will know this has been achieved. Provide your response in fewer than 4 sentences.]

**c. Other non-ACC community supports involved in the treatment pathway:** [List any other non-ACC supports in the community that will be involved in the kiritaki/client's treatment pathway and describe the role each will take (e.g., Community Mental Health Service key worker carrying out home visits every two weeks). Use the format below for each support. If none are documented, print "Not documented."]
Name: [Insert if documented.] 
Role/function: [Insert if documented.] 
Community organisation: [Insert if documented.]
[Repeat for each additional support if documented.]

[s_4_treatment_barriers] 4. Treatment Barriers

Treatment barrier 1: [Describe the potential and actual barriers to access, delivery, and care for the kiritaki/client. Provide your response in fewer than 3 sentences]
Plans to address treatment barrier 1: [Describe the documented plan to address the treatment barrier above. Note that addressing treatment barriers may occur prior to, or alongside, targeting recovery goals depending on the nature of the barrier. Examples include lifting depressed mood before commencing trauma processing; addressing safety in the home before commencing trauma work. Note any referrals to specialist teams or community supports that have been identified. Provide your response in fewer than 4 sentences]
[Repeat above structure for each treatment barrier identified.]

[s_5_recovery_goals] 5. Recovery Goals

(Recovery goals should be SMART (Specific, Measurable, Action-oriented, Realistic, and Time-bound), meaningful to the kiritaki/client, relevant to their recovery, and building progress towards the broad treatment outcome. Avoid goals that cannot be achieved within the duration of the requested Tailored Support to Wellbeing package. Use the structure below:)
[recovery_goal_1] Recovery goal 1
Goal description: [State the goal]
How will the outcomes of this goal improve kiritaki/client functioning? [Describe what the kiritaki/client will be able to do differently or better as a result of achieving this goal. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
How will this goal be achieved? [Specify the services needed and how they will be used to achieve this goal. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
How will progress towards this goal be measured? [State the documented measures that will be used to track progress. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
Who will deliver the services to support this goal, and what is the expected timeframe? [Insert the provider and expected timeframe. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]

[recovery_goal_2] Recovery goal 2
Goal description: [State the goal, if documented]
How will the outcomes of this goal improve kiritaki/client functioning? [Describe what the kiritaki/client will be able to do differently or better as a result of achieving this goal.]
How will this goal be achieved? [Specify the services needed and how they will be used to achieve this goal. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
How will progress towards this goal be measured? [State the documented measures that will be used to track progress. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
Who will deliver the services to support this goal, and what is the expected timeframe? [Insert the provider and expected timeframe. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]

[recovery_goal_3] Recovery goal 3
Goal description: [State the goal, if documented]
How will the outcomes of this goal improve kiritaki/client functioning? [Describe what the kiritaki/client will be able to do differently or better as a result of achieving this goal.]
How will this goal be achieved? [Specify the services needed and how they will be used to achieve this goal. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
How will progress towards this goal be measured? [State the documented measures that will be used to track progress. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
Who will deliver the services to support this goal, and what is the expected timeframe? [Insert the provider and expected timeframe. Provide your response in fewer than 2 sentences.]
[Repeat above structure if further recovery goals are identified.]

[s_6_planned_services_and_the_providers_who] 6. Planned Services and the Providers Who Will Deliver These

(List the services required under Tailored Support to Wellbeing Package A or B and any other ACC services to meet the recovery goals listed above. Use the format below, organised under the three sub-categories. If a sub-category has no services, print "None" under that heading.)

[tailored_support_to_wellbeing] Tailored Support to Wellbeing
Service: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider name / ACC ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider discipline: [Insert if documented.] 
Supplier / Supplier ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Hours: [Insert if documented.]
[Repeat for each additional Tailored Support to Wellbeing service if documented.]
Total hours of Tailored Support to Wellbeing: [Insert total. If not documented, print "Not documented."]

[group_based_therapy] Group-based Therapy
Service: [Insert if documented. If none, print "None."] 
Provider name / ACC ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider discipline: [Insert if documented.] 
Supplier / Supplier ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Hours: [Insert if documented.]
Expected start and end dates of Group-based Therapy: [Insert if documented. If not applicable, print "Not applicable."]

[other_acc_services] Other ACC Services
Service: [Insert if documented. If none, print "None."] 
Provider name / ACC ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Provider discipline: [Insert if documented.] 
Supplier / Supplier ID: [Insert if documented.] 
Hours: [Insert if documented.]

Total expected package duration of all services listed above: [Insert combined total hours of Tailored Support to Wellbeing, Group-based Therapy, and Other ACC Services, if applicable.]

If other ACC services have been listed above, please describe how these services will support the recovery of the kiritaki/client: [Describe the documented rationale for any other ACC services listed. If no other ACC services are listed, leave this blank.]

[s_7_other_information] 7. Other Information

Date of last face-to-face meeting with the kiritaki that informed this report: [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Proposed Progress Report submission date: [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not documented, print "Not documented."]
Date of disengagement by the kiritaki (if applicable): [Print the date in DD/MM/YYYY format. If not applicable or not documented, print "Not applicable."]
Other relevant information: [Include any other documented information considered relevant to assist in the recovery of the kiritaki/client. Provide your response in fewer than 3 sentences. If nothing further to report, print "No further information."]

[s_8_provider_declaration] 8. Provider Declaration

Note to clinician: The following declarations must be completed manually on the official ACC8541 form by checking the relevant boxes:
