☐ I confirm that the information contained in this report is accurate and that I have followed the standards set out in the Sensitive Claims Service operational guidelines.

Lead Service Provider name: [Print exactly as documented.]
Provider ID: [Print exactly as documented.]
Supplier name: [Print exactly as documented.]
Supplier ID: [Print exactly as documented.]
Date: [Print today's date in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

sections.label: 6. Planned Services and the Providers Who Will Deliver These

title: Occupational Therapy (OT) Functional Capacity Assessment Interview
description: A detailed summary of a session(s) between an occupational therapist and a client where the aim is to produce a functional capacity assessment. This
prompt:
(The template below will summarise an assessment being conducted by an occupational therapist who is completing a functional capacity assessment on a client. As such, you must pay particular attention to issues that are of interest to and within the broad clinical scope of occupational therapists. Such issues will include, but not be limited to, helping clients participate in meaningful activities that support their health and wellbeing, including: functional independence with activities of daily living; physical health and mobility; mental health and wellbeing; cognitive functioning; paediatrics and developmental delays; vocational and educational participation; environmental modifications; rehabilitation and recovery support; aged care and palliative support; and advocacy and health promotion. After analysing the session notes, contextual notes, and transcript from one or more sessions between a clinician and a client, you will generate a detailed summary according to the headings below. In your output, you will use language and terminology that is consistent with evidence-based occupational therapy practice, and consistent with the language of the Australian National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS). Although the assessment will focus on a client’s deficits and difficulties, where possible, use affirming and strengths-based language. Note that there may be a parent, caregiver, or other type of support person in the interview with the client being assessed, and you should note any relevant comments they make as well. Be highly descriptive in your output. Use as many paragraphs as needed in each section to summarise information, as the sentences you print may be used for copying and pasting into formal reports. You must strictly adhere to the following layout:)



[functional_capacity_assessment] Functional Capacity Assessment

Client Name: \[Print the client’s first and last name.]

Date of Birth: \[Print the client’s date of birth in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

Date of Assessment: \[Print today’s date in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

Time of Assessment: \[Print the starting time of this session in HH\:MM am/pm format.]

Location of Assessment: \[Describe the location where the assessment took place e.g., in the client’s home, at the clinician’s office, etc.]

Name of Clinician: \[Print the title and name of the clinician, followed by their profession (e.g., occupational therapist).]

Other People Present: \[List the names of any other people who were in attendance during the assessment, other than the clinician and the client. State what the relationship of any other person in attendance is to the individual being assessed (e.g., parent, caregiver, other family member, support worker, case manager, etc.).]

[referral_source_reason_for_referral] Referral Source / Reason for Referral

\[Describe who referred the client for assessment. Describe the reason and purpose for the assessment.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[presentation_during_assessment] Presentation During Assessment

\[If applicable, provide a paragraph summary of the client’s presentation and participation during the assessment. Information may include: a brief mental status examination; client’s level of cooperation; client’s level of enthusiasm; client’s level of understanding; client’s cognitive functioning; client’s communication abilities; etc.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[diagnoses_and_medical_history] Diagnoses and Medical History

\[Describe the client’s diagnoses and medical history. Briefly summarise how the diagnoses and medical history impact on the client’s functional capacity and their everyday life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[medications] Current Medications

\[Describe the client’s current medications, including drug names, doses, frequency of doses, and what condition or symptom each medication is treating. Describe any other relevant information about the client’s medications, such as reported side effects, adherence, effectiveness, etc.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[history] Background

\[Describe the client’s background. This may include information such as: developmental history; education / employment history; social history; family history; history of trauma; medical history; drug and alcohol history; etc. Provide a summary of how the client’s diagnoses and difficulties have negatively affected their life in the past.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[client_goals] Client Goals

\[Describe the client’s goals, including the client’s NDIS goals if applicable.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[family_social_and_formal_support] Family, Social, and Formal Support

\[Describe the client’s family, friends, social relationships, and social support. Describe the nature of these relationships (e.g., identify who the client is close to, who provides the client with support, etc.). Describe who lives with the client. Describe all aspects of socialisation for the client, such as involvement with groups and clubs, supported employment, etc. Describe who provides formal support to the client, such as support workers, therapy providers, disability support organisations, etc. If the client attends a day program, how often do they attend this, what are the types of activities they do at the day program, and which organisation and individuals oversee the day program. Describe the client’s self-evaluation of the quality and quantity of their family, social, and formal supports.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[home_environment] Home Environment

\[Describe the client’s current home environment and living arrangements. Describe the advantages and challenges for the client in their current home environment. Describe the impact of the client’s home environment on their disability. Provide detailed information on any aspect of the physical environment discussed by the client and the clinician (e.g., the bathroom is difficult to manoeuvre within, there are three steps up to the front door, etc.). (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[domains_of_functional_capacity_including] Domains of Functional Capacity (Including ADLs)

**a. Communication:** \[Describe the client’s ability to understand and express verbal and non-verbal communication. Describe the client’s use of assistive communication tools or devices. Describe the client’s participation in conversations, instructions, and social cues. Describe the client’s abilities in any other communication-related activities of daily living, including how these negatively impact on the client’s quality of life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

**b. Social Interaction:** \[Describe the client’s ability to form and maintain friendships. Describe the client’s behaviours in structured and unstructured environments. Describe any sensory integration issues that affect the client’s social interactions or behaviours in social settings (e.g., sensory triggers contributing to dysregulation in social settings, etc.). Describe the client’s emotional regulation and interpersonal skills. Describe if and how the client’s emotional or psychological state negatively impacts their social functioning. Describe the negative impact of any other significant factors on the client’s engagement in social interaction or community activities (e.g., transport issues, physical limitations, etc.). Describe the client’s abilities in any other social interaction-related activities of daily living, including how these negatively impact on the client’s quality of life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

**c. Learning:** \[Describe the client’s cognitive, attention, memory, expression, and comprehension abilities. Describe the client’s executive functioning (e.g., planning, processing, organising, problem-solving, etc.). Describe the client’s capacity to acquire new skills and adapt to change. Describe any sensory integration issues that affect the client’s learning e.g., visual/auditory processing difficulties affecting focus or classroom engagement, etc. Describe the client’s abilities in any other learning-related activities of daily living, including how these negatively impact on the client’s quality of life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

**d. Mobility:** \[Describe the client’s ability to move around safely at home and in the community. Detail specific areas of physical functioning e.g., upper limb function, lower limb function, etc. Detail the client’s abilities in specific areas of daily life related to mobility e.g., chair transfers, toilet transfers, bed transfers, using stairs, pressure area needs, etc. Describe the client’s use of mobility aids, if applicable. Describe the client’s endurance and safety considerations, both physical and psychological. Describe any sensory integration issues that negatively affect the client’s mobility e.g., noises, crowds, bright lights, etc. Describe the client’s abilities in any other mobility-related activities of daily living, including how these negatively impact on the client’s quality of life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

**e. Self-Care:** \[Describe the client’s personal hygiene and grooming. Describe any sensory integration issues that negatively affect the client’s self-care (e.g., tactile sensitivities impacting grooming or hygiene routines, etc.). Describe the client’s meal preparation skills and eating habits. Describe the client’s medication management. Describe the client’s sleep hygiene and daily routines. Describe the client’s abilities in any other self-care-related activities of daily living, including how these negatively impact on the client’s quality of life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

**f. Self-Management:** \[Describe the client’s ability to make decisions and problem-solve. Discuss the client’s ability to manage finances, appointments, and correspondence with key stakeholders and social acquaintances. Describe the client’s emotional or psychological state (e.g., low mood, high anxiety, agitated, aggressive, etc.) Describe the client’s awareness of safety and risks. Describe the client’s ability to cope with change, stress, or emotional distress. Describe the client’s abilities in any other self-management-related activities of daily living, including how these negatively impact on the client’s quality of life.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[assistive_technology_and_environmental_m] Assistive Technology and Environmental Modifications

\[Describe the client’s assistive equipment and technology in current use. Describe any clinician recommendations for new aids or modifications.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[outcome_measures_and_assessment_tools_us] Outcome Measures and Assessment Tools Used

\[Describe the name and detailed descriptions of results of any outcome measures or assessment tools used, including psychometric measures (e.g., Vineland-3, WHODAS 2.0, COPM, MOHO tools, etc.). Describe how each assessment is related to the client’s disability or difficulties, and provide detailed descriptions of how the results of each assessment elaborate on the level and extent of the client’s disability and difficulties e.g., include descriptors used by the assessment authors if applicable, etc. Remember that sections of your output may be copied and pasted into formal reports, so this section needs to be clearly laid out, especially if several assessments have been used.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[client_strengths_skills_and_hobbies] Client Strengths, Skills, and Hobbies

\[Describe the client’s strengths, skills, and interests. Describe the client’s hobbies and recreational pastimes. Describe anything that the client is good at. Describe any positive or attractive personality traits of the client. Describe any other unique, positive aspect of the client’s presentation and life generally.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[summary_of_findings] Summary of Findings

\[Describe key barriers to participation in any aspect of life for the client. Describe the client’s overall capacity across domains (e.g., independent, needs prompting, requires full support, etc.). Provide a summary interpretation of functional capacity.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[risks_and_safety_concerns] Risks and Safety Concerns

\[Describe any risk factors, such as self-harm or suicidal ideation, physical health risks, social risks, and any other risk related to the client’s functional capacity based on the information that has been provided in this assessment. Describe any protective factors that mitigate the risk. Describe any action plans already in place for known risks.]

[nextsteps] Recommendations

* \[Describe any clinician recommendations for the client. Describe recommended supports required to enhance the client’s functional capacity. Describe services or therapies recommended to the client. Describe any recommendation for the client that falls under the following categories which align with NDIS funding: assistive technology or home modifications; hours of support with specific providers, including the number of recommended hours, who will provide the support, and where the support will take place; home environment or accommodation recommendations; support coordination or plan management recommendations; transport recommendations; assistance with social and community participation recommendations; improved living arrangements recommendations; increased social and community participation recommendations; improved relationships recommendations; improved health and wellbeing recommendations; improved learning recommendations; improved daily living skills recommendations; and disability employment services recommendations. Describe if any further assessments are needed or recommended. Provide the clinician’s rationale for all recommendations, and, where applicable, take the NDIS Reasonable and Necessary Criteria into account when providing a rationale.]
* \[Provide details of any future appointment times between the clinician and the client.]

sections.label: Outcome Measures and Assessment Tools Used

title: Occupational Therapy (OT) SOAP Session / Progress Note
description: A detailed summary of a session between an occupational therapist and a client. This template follows the traditional SOAP layout: Subjective,
prompt:
(The template below is designed to summarise a session between an occupational therapist and a client. As such, you must pay particular attention to issues that are of interest to and within the broad clinical scope of occupational therapists. Such issues will include, but not be limited to, helping clients participate in meaningful activities that support their health and wellbeing, including: functional independence with activities of daily living; physical health and mobility; mental health and wellbeing; cognitive functioning; paediatrics and developmental delays; vocational and educational participation; environmental modifications; rehabilitation and recovery support; aged care and palliative support; and advocacy and health promotion. After analysing the transcript and any contextual notes from a session between an occupational therapist and a client, you will generate a detailed summary of that session according to the headings below. Note that there may be a parent, caregiver, or other type of support person in the session with the client, and you should note any relevant comments they make as well. You will strictly adhere to the following layout:)

[occupational_therapy_soap_progress_note] Occupational Therapy SOAP Progress Note

[subjective] Subjective

* \[Describe the client’s self-reported experiences, concerns, goals, or observations shared during the session. This may include: Function-related challenges (e.g., “increased difficulty with dressing due to shoulder pain”); meaningful goals or priorities (e.g., “would like to return to driving”); any emotional or psychological experiences (e.g., “feeling frustrated by dependence on others”); and contextual changes (e.g., “new wheelchair received last week”, or “transitioning back to school/work”, etc.). Describe the duration/timing/location/quality/severity/context of any client self-reported experiences, concerns, goals, or observations shared during the session.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe any self-reported things that worsen or alleviate the concerns, issues or symptoms for the client, including self-treatment attempts and their effectiveness.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe how the concerns, issues, or symptoms have changed or evolved over time for the client.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe any client-reported past occurrences of similar concerns, issues, or symptoms, including when they occurred, how they were managed, and the outcomes.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe the client’s self-reported impact of the concerns, issues, or symptoms on their daily activities. Describe how the concerns, issues, or symptoms affect the client's daily life, work, and activities.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe any relevant client-reported history related to concerns, issues, or symptoms. This may include: contributing factors such as past psychiatric history, past medical history, past investigations, and past treatments; social history; family history; and any other relevant history.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[objective] Objective

* \[Describe the occupational therapist’s observations of clinically relevant behaviours, performance in tasks, and measurable data during the session. This may include: session focus (e.g., ADL assessment, upper limb rehab, sensory processing strategies, environmental assessment, vocational support, etc.); observations e.g., participation level (such as engaged, hesitant, fatigued, etc.), motor performance (such as fine motor dexterity, mobility, balance, etc.), cognitive functioning (such as attention, planning, sequencing, etc.), sensory response (such as over-responsivity to noise, calm with appropriate input, etc.), emotional state (such as cooperative, anxious, fearful, etc.); tools / assessments used e.g., AMPS, COPM, sensory profile, observation checklist etc., and including tools measuring performance outcomes.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe the mental state of the client using the customary elements and features of a Mental State Examination.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe any occupational therapist investigations with results.] (You must only include completed investigations and the results of these investigations if they have been explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank. All planned or ordered investigations must not be included under the Objective section; instead all planned or ordered investigations must be included under the Plan section below.)
* \[Provide a summary of the results of any psychometric assessments, including assessments administered through the NovoPsych platform.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[assessment] Assessment

* \[Describe the clinical interpretation of how the client is functioning in relation to their occupational goals. This may include: client-demonstrated summary of strengths and barriers; progress noted in (e.g., task initiation, safety awareness, fine motor coordination, etc.); barriers observed included (e.g., fatigue, difficulty sequencing steps, limited range of motion, etc.); current functioning in context / environment is consistent with (e.g., previous performance, expected recovery trajectory, developmental norms, etc.); statement about the appropriateness and effectiveness of interventions (e.g., the intervention remains appropriate and aligned with client-centred goals, etc.); statement about the clinical reasoning or justification for intervention approach.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)
* \[Describe any change in diagnosis or prognosis, including questions or issues related to these things e.g., differential diagnosis formulation, etc.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

[nextsteps] Plan

* \[Describe the next steps, goals and recommendations for the client. This may include: the plan for the next session (e.g., continue working on kitchen task sequencing with visual supports, introduce modified cutlery for mealtime independence, etc.); ongoing focus areas (e.g., increase safe transfers, develop routines for school readiness, build tolerance for sensory input, etc.); assessments planned; changes in treatment; recommendations/referrals (e.g., physiotherapy input, home modification provider, educational psychologist, etc.); goal review/update (e.g., short-term goal met today and will now progress to more complex task, continue current goal with revised strategy, etc.); and time, date and location of the next session.] (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank.)

title: Peer Supervision
description: When two or more clinicians of roughly equal experience engage in a peer supervision meeting, this template provides a detailed summary of the cases
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used when two or more clinicians conduct a peer supervision meeting in a mental health setting. A peer supervision meeting is a collaborative and non-hierarchical process among clinicians of roughly equal experience and status who provide mutual support, consultation, and engage in reflective discussion. The document generated by this template will provide a detailed summary of that meeting. Review the transcript and identify which speaker corresponds to who. When documenting statements, concerns or observations, clearly identify who made each point. Organise your summary using the following headings and instructions, but exclude any headings or sections that are not explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note:)

Date: [Print the date of the peer supervision session in the format: day number, full month name, year (e.g., 10 September 2025).]
Time: [Print the starting time of the peer supervision meeting in the format HH:MM am/pm.]
Attendees: [Print the names of all attendees of the peer supervision meeting, if known.]

[case_discussions] Case Discussions

(For each case discussed, create a separate section with the heading "Case [number]" and include the following three subsections:)

##### **Case 1:**

* **Clinical Case Details:** [Provide details of the clinician presenting the case, including the clinician’s name. Provide details of the client being featured in the case presentation, including presenting problems, age, background, life circumstances, how long the client has been in treatment for, diagnosis, treatment plan, and interventions. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Formulation:** [Provide details of the presenting clinician’s understanding of the client's issues from psychological and theoretical perspectives, and of any refinement of the formulation. What theoretical framework is the presenting clinician viewing client issues through? (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Other Attendees’ Input and Recommendations:** [Provide a detailed account of input provided by other attendees (i.e., the non-presenting clinicians) for each case, including reflections, challenges, learning points, suggestions, strategies, or alternative perspectives. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[Repeat the above structure for Case 2, Case 3, etc. as needed.]

[other_discussion_points] Other Discussion Points

1. [Provide a detailed account of all other issues raised by meeting attendees that may not relate to a particular case discussion. Some examples of other issues might include: private practice management issues; administration issues; mental health funding issues; professional development opportunities; management issues; employment issues; clinician self-care including the clinician's own stress, burnout, or personal challenges; workplace wellbeing; application of therapeutic approaches; professional obstacles and setbacks; ethical and legal issues such as ethical boundaries, risk management, cultural and diversity competence, and clinical documentation; and psychometric assessment issues. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[summary] Summary

[Provide a summary of the peer supervision meeting in less than four sentences.]

[action_points_and_follow_up] Action Points and Follow-Up

* [Describe any key takeaways and decisions made. Identify action items, assigned responsibilities, and deadlines for follow-up. List the day, date, and time of the next peer supervision meeting. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: Play Therapy
description: This template is for working with children using a play therapy framework. It provides the therapist with a detailed yet concise summary of a play
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by therapists who use a play therapy framework when working with children in a mental health / behavioural setting. The therapist may take one of the following play therapy approaches, Non-Directive Play Therapy, Directive Play Therapy, Cognitive-Behavioural Play Therapy. Using the provided transcript from the session, you will generate a detailed, yet concise summary of the session, including key interactions, therapeutic interventions, and observations using evidence-based language consistent with professional play therapy practice. Use professional, objective, descriptive language. Avoid making assumptions about the child's intent; focus on verbalised behaviours and interactions. Include direct quotes from the child, when relevant, to highlight key insights. Ensure the note reflects the therapeutic process and clinical observations in a manner appropriate for documentation by a psychologist, psychotherapist, or counsellor. Organise the summary under the following headings, and use bullet points beneath each of those headings.)

[presenting_concerns_and_goals] Presenting Concerns and Goals

* [Summarise the overarching therapeutic goals for the child client. Note any specific concerns or challenges addressed in this session.]

[summary_of_session_activities] Summary of Session Activities

* [Describe the play materials or techniques used (e.g., sand tray, puppets, art supplies). Summarise the child client's engagement and notable themes or narratives that emerged during play. Include examples of symbolic or metaphorical expressions if relevant.]

[therapist_interventions_and_techniques] Therapist Interventions and Techniques

* [Document specific therapeutic approaches (e.g., child-centred play therapy, directive play techniques). Highlight reflective statements, open-ended questions, or specific guidance provided by the therapist.]
* [Note any instances of empathic understanding, unconditional positive regard, respecting of the child’s lead.]
* [Interactions with Therapist: Describe how the client interacted with the therapist, including any notable moments of rapport or resistance.]

[observations_of_the_child_s_behaviour_an] Observations of the Child's Behaviour and Emotions

* [Note the child client's affect or emotional expression, body language that the therapist verbalised, and verbalisations during the session. Record evidence of emotional processing, problem-solving, or self-expression.]
* [Play Themes and Patterns: Detail the themes or patterns that emerged during play, such as specific types of play, recurring storylines, or symbolic play such as the nature of the relationships between toys.]

[clinical_impressions_and_analysis] Clinical Impressions and Analysis

* [Evaluate the session's alignment with therapeutic goals. Describe any progress, shifts in the child's play, or new themes that emerged.]
* [Provide a brief analysis of the session, linking observations and client responses to the therapeutic goals. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise exclude section.)]

[plan_for_future_sessions] Plan for Future Sessions

* [What was proposed for activities to occur in the next steps or adjustments to the therapeutic approach for next session. Note any changes to focus areas or techniques for subsequent sessions. If mentioned in session, list details of when the next session is scheduled for, including time and date.]

sections.label: Observations of the Child's Behaviour and Emotions

title: Positive Psychology
description: For use by a clinician using a positive psychology framework, this template provides a detailed summary of a session with a client.
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by a mental health clinician working within a positive psychology framework. Following analysis of a transcript of a therapy session between a clinician and a client, you will generate a detailed summary of that session. Use language that is consistent with the practice of positive psychology. The note should emphasise the client’s strengths, progress, and positive resources, while also acknowledging challenges in a constructive and solution-focused manner. Avoid pathologizing language and instead focus on capabilities, adaptive coping, and opportunities for growth. The template will follow the following format:)

[START TEMPLATE.]

Session Overview
[Provide a brief summary of the session’s focus, including the client’s main concerns and goals. Describe key themes discussed during the session, framed within a positive psychology lens (e.g., strengths, resilience, values, meaningful experiences). Write this as pose. Do not use bullet points.]

Strengths-Based Observations
[Identify and highlight the client’s personal strengths that emerged in the session. Note instances where the client demonstrated resilience, optimism, growth, or positive coping strategies. Recognise the client’s values, personal accomplishments, or character strengths that contribute to their well-being. Write this as pose. Do not use bullet points.]

Positive Emotional Experiences & Engagement
[Describe moments where the client expressed gratitude, joy, curiosity, or a sense of meaning. Describe any discussions around engagement, flow states, or fulfilling activities that enhance the client’s well-being. Describe any positive shifts in mood, motivation, or outlook observed during the session. Describe any instances of the client flourishing. Write this as pose. Do not use bullet points.]

Solution-Focused Progress & Goals
[Describe progress made toward therapeutic goals, using a growth-oriented and empowering narrative. Describe any positive changes, reframing of difficulties, or newly discovered resources that support well-being. Describe any instances or acknowledging of the client’s commitment to growth and well-being. Write this as pose. Do not use bullet points.]

Barriers to Growth
[Describe any barriers, obstacles, impediments, or complications to the client achieving their goals or making progress towards being happier. Write this as pose. Do not use bullet points.]

Gratitude & Takeaways
[Provide a summary of the key takeaways from the session, emphasising positive insights and reflections.  Note any expressions of gratitude from the client or clinician, reinforcing positive reinforcement. Describe the rapport or therapeutic alliance between the clinician and client. Write this as pose. Do not use bullet points.]

Next Steps
[Outline collaborative next steps, goals, or interventions that align with the client’s strengths and aspirations. List the time, day, and date of any next appointment between the clinician and the client.]

[END TEMPLATE.]

title: Professional Development
description: This template can be used to convert the content of a professional development session, such as a workshop or webinar, into comprehensive notes. TIP:
prompt:
(The template below is designed to help clinicians document the content of professional development sessions, such as workshops or webinars. A clinician should be able to look at your detailed document and treat it as a comprehensive collection of study notes. The template will use the following format:)



**Title of Professional Development:** \[Print the title of the session.]

**Date:** \[Print the date of the professional development session in DD/MM/YYYY format.]

**Time:** \[Print the time of the professional development session in 24-hour HH\:MM format.]

**Presenter(s):** \[Print the names of the presenters.]

[content] Content

* \[Provide detailed, structured, and comprehensive notes on the content of the session. Use full sentences. These detailed notes should appear in the exact order that they were presented in the session. If a point is related to something that was presented earlier, make a note of this. Your detailed notes should include: key points, explanations, and examples provided by the presenter; identification of the main topics covered; discussion of any models, frameworks, or case studies mentioned; relevant background information or clarifications provided by the presenter; reflections on the overall flow and emphasis areas of the session; and any implicit themes or underlying messages pertinent to mental health practice. Be sure to include input from other attendees, including reflections, challenges, learning points, suggestions, strategies, or alternative perspectives.]

[action_points_and_follow_up] Action Points and Follow-Up

* \[Describe any key takeaways and decisions made. Identify action items, assigned responsibilities, and deadlines for follow-up. List any instructions or challenges that the presenter has given to attendees. Describe any observations on potential applications in professional practice.]

[summary] Summary

\[Provide a concise summary of no more than six sentences on the content of the session.]

title: Psychiatric / Mental Health Nurse Progress Note
description: Summarises an interaction between a psychiatric nurse and a patient, with sections specifically targeted to the requirements of psychiatric nurses.
prompt:
[Provide a summary of an interaction between a psychiatric nurse and a mental health patient. If there is no content for a particular section heading, do not print that section heading. Organise the summary under the following section headings:]
 
Patient: [Print the patient’s first and last name.]
 
DOB: [Print the patient’s date of birth in Day Month, Year format.]
 
Consultation date: [Print the date of the consultation in Day Month, Year format.]
 
[presentation] Presentation / Current Status
 
[In one paragraph of less than five sentences, describe the patient’s presenting issues, reasons for today’s consultation, and chief complaints, including symptoms, requests, etc. Describe the impact of the client's presenting concerns and chief complaints on their daily activities. Explain how the symptoms affect the client's daily life, work, relationships, and activities. Highlight any relevant physiological or lifestyle observations that may be impacting the patient’s mental health e.g., sleep, appetite, relationship changes, etc. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]
 
[risk] Risk Assessment & Management
 
[Describe the patient’s risk profile in one paragraph of less than five sentences, taking the following into account: Suicidal ideation; homicidal ideation; self-harm; violence and aggression; addictive behaviours; risk-taking and impulsivity; and any other issue that could pose a significant risk of harm to any individual. Describe any risk management plan. Describe any factors that mitigate the level of risk. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]
 
[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)
 
[Describe in one paragraph of less than five sentences the patient’s presentation using the following standard MSE domains (only include each domain if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank): Appearance (the patient’s general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire); behaviour (the patient’s interaction with their surrounds, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport); speech (the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the patient’s speech; including if the client communicates in any other non-verbal way, such as with gestures, noises, or eye contact); mood and affect (the patient’s reported mood and emotional state using their own words if possible; including the range and appropriateness of the patient’s emotional response during the appointment, and note any discrepancies with the patient’s stated mood; including any observations about emotional expression made by the psychiatric nurse; including any behaviours that provide insight into mood and affect, such as anger outbursts, withdrawal, and laughing; including any other factors that may be providing insight into the patient’s mood or affect, such as expressions of illness, or other interactions with the psychiatric nurse; including the response of the patient to the use of any visual aids regarding mood or affect, such as mood scales); thought process (the patient’s coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts); thought content (the content of the patient’s thoughts, and note the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any); perception (hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the patient, if any); cognition (the patient’s orientation to time / place / person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension); and insight and judgment (the patient's understanding of their mental health and their decision-making capacity, noting any lack of awareness or denial).]
 
[medical_investigations] Medical Investigations
 
[In a paragraph of less than five sentences, describe the results of any medical investigations or readings taken by the psychiatric nurse, including blood pressure, respiratory rate, weight, temperature, etc. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]
 
[interventions] Interventions
 
[In a paragraph of less than five sentences, describe what the psychiatric nurse did during the consultation. Describe any psychological interventions used e.g., supportive counselling, CBT, psychoeducation, etc. Describe actions related to medication administration, monitoring, or coordination. Describe the current medication dosage, and document if there were any changes to medication. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]
 
[patient_response] Patient Response
 
[In a paragraph of less than five sentences, describe the patient’s response to medication and any other intervention used by the psychiatric nurse. Describe the level of engagement, rapport, and/or therapeutic alliance in the consultation. Describe the patient’s overall progress, noting any barriers to reaching treatment goals. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]
 
[current_diagnosis] Current Diagnosis
 

* [List all the patient’s current psychiatric diagnoses, including the accompanying diagnostic codes from the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, 5th Edition, Text Revision, also known as the DSM-5-TR. Describe any differential diagnoses or other diagnostic-specific information mentioned by the psychiatric nurse. Only list diagnoses that are mentioned by the psychiatric nurse in the transcript, or are listed in the contextual notes or clinical note – DO NOT MAKE UP YOUR OWN DIAGNOSES.]

 
[plan_follow_up] Plan / Follow-Up
 
[In one paragraph of less than five sentences, describe the plans for follow-up. Describe any tasks or activities assigned to the patient. Mention any tasks that the psychiatric nurse will do e.g., follow up with key stakeholders, refer the patient to a health professional or other service, etc. Mention the date and time of the next appointment between the psychiatric nurse and the patient. Mention any plans related to the next appointment. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

title: Psychiatric Clinical Interview and Assessment
description: For use by a psychiatrist seeing a patient for the first time, this template gives a detailed summary of a clinical interview and assessment,
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by psychiatrists conducting a clinical interview and assessment with a patient being seen for the first time. Using the provided transcript, you will generate a detailed summary of the appointment. Ensure the summary is concise, adheres to evidence-based practice, and professionally worded according to psychiatric terminology, such as using the term "patient" instead of "client". The content of the note should include clear, clinically relevant observations and recommendations. Avoid including direct quotes from the transcript unless they are pivotal to the clinical assessment process. Organise the summary under the following headings.)

[identifying_information] Identifying Information

* \[Detail patient's name or initials, age, gender, and date of consultation.(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)

[reason_for_consultation] Reason for Consultation

\[Detailed statement of the presenting problem or purpose of the appointment. Include information such as type and nature of symptoms, length of time with symptoms, external stressors that are relevant to the symptoms, interpersonal or relationship factors that are relevant to the symptoms, and any other information that is relevant to the presenting problem or purpose of the appointment. Include information related to the impact on functioning for the patient (e.g., occupational, family, social, academic).]

[history] Personal and Developmental History

\[Describe as much detail as possible related to the patient’s personal and developmental history. Include information such as early development, family history and structure, education and employment history, occupation, current living arrangements, and social support.]

\[Describe any history of trauma, including family or intergenerational trauma, or significant adversity or any type of traumatic event.]

\[Describe any family history of any illness or medical condition, including psychiatric illness and neurodevelopmental conditions. Include any information about family suicides or family addiction behaviours (e.g., alcoholism, other drug abuse).(only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank)]

[current_key_lifestyle_indicators] Current Key Lifestyle Indicators

* \[Describe the patient’s current sleep pattern and sleep hygiene.]
* \[Describe the patient’s current diet, including binge behaviours, caffeine intake, and consumption of junk food.]
* \[Describe the patient’s current level of physical exercise or general activity levels.]
* \[Describe the patient’s level of social functioning, include information about quality and quantity of social and family relationships, the patient’s efforts at connecting socially with others, and the extent of any social isolation.]

[medical_status_and_medical_history] Medical Status and Medical History

* \[List previous non-psychiatric diagnoses and medical conditions, including information about when the diagnosis was made and by whom. Provide information given by the patient that describes responses to treatment, such as effective outcomes, side effects, and adverse reactions. If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[psychiatric_illness_history] Psychiatric Illness History

* \[List previous psychiatric diagnoses and conditions, including information about when the diagnosis was made and by whom. Provide information given by the patient that describes responses to treatment, such as effective outcomes, side effects, and adverse reactions. Describe any previous psychological interventions, including with therapists, online programs, or group programs. Describe any past hospitalisations for mental health reasons, including locations, names of key medical personnel involved, and dates of these hospitalisations. If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[medications] Medication History

* \[List all medications that the patient is currently taking, including psychotropic and non-psychotropic. Include information about doses and frequencies. Describe any medication history that details information such as response to any past medication, side effects, and adverse reactions. Assess adherence to previous medication regimens. If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[drug_and_alcohol_use_history] Drug and Alcohol Use History

\[Describe the patient’s current level of alcohol consumption, cigarette or vape use, and if they consume any other recreational or illicit substance (e.g., cannabis, methamphetamine, MDMA, cocaine). Describe any past or current misuse of prescription medications (e.g., opioids, benzodiazepines, stimulants). If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[other_addiction_history] Other Addiction History

\[Describe any other potentially problematic addictive behaviours mentioned by the patient (e.g., gambling, video gaming, Internet, social media, food, pornography). Include this information even if the patient does not view the behaviour as problematic or as an addiction. If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[legal_and_forensic_history] Legal and Forensic History

\[Describe any previous history mentioned by the patient that involves illegal activity, interactions with the justice system, imprisonment, interactions with the family law system, or domestic violence behaviour. If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)

\[Document findings across the following domains:]

Appearance: \[Describe the patient’s general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Appearance:" and leave blank.]

Behaviour: \[Describe the patient’s interaction with their surrounds, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Behaviour:" and leave blank.]

Speech: \[Describe the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the patient’s speech. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Speech:" and leave blank.]

Mood and Affect: \[Describe the patient’s reported mood and emotional state using their own words if possible. Describe the range and appropriateness of the patient’s emotional response during the appointment, and note any discrepancies with the patient’s stated mood. Note any observations about emotional expression made by the psychiatrist. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Mood and Affect:" and leave blank.]

Thought Process: \[Describe the patient’s coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Thought Process:" and leave blank.]

Thought Content: \[Describe the content of the patient’s thoughts, and note the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Thought Content:" and leave blank.]

Perception: \[Describe hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the patient, if any. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Perception:" and leave blank.]

Cognition: \[Describe the patient’s orientation to time / place / person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Cognition:" and leave blank.]

Insight and Judgment: \[Describe the patient's understanding of their mental health condition and their decision-making capacity, noting any lack of awareness or denial. If this is not stated in the transcript, print "Insight and Judgment:" and leave blank.]

[strengths_coping_skills_recreational_int] Strengths, Coping Skills, Recreational Interests, and Hobbies

* \[List the patient’s strengths, positive coping skills, recreational interests, hobbies, and other protective factors. Only list things that are accepted as helpful and useful in successfully managing mental health. Do not list anything that is considered harmful to mental health (e.g., consuming alcohol, other addictive behaviour). If not explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, leave blank.]

[scales] Psychometric Assessment

* \[Summarise any psychometric testing results, including scores and interpretation. Detail any plans specifically mentioned by the psychiatrist to conduct any future psychometric testing. If there is no mention of psychometric assessment in the transcript, do not type the subheading title, and do leave this section blank.]

[formulation] Formulation

\[Provide a detailed bio-psycho-social psychiatric formulation, including predisposing, precipitating, perpetuating and protective factors. If applicable, also include a psychodynamic formulation of the patient's presentation. Include evidence from the transcript to enhance the details of your formulation.]

[diagnosis] Diagnosis

\[Describe the psychiatrist's diagnostic impressions and observations, based on DSM-5-TR and ICD-11 criteria. Include the diagnostic code of any such diagnoses. Describe if the psychiatrist has stated that these diagnoses are new or confirmation of diagnoses previously given. Detail any comorbidities or differential diagnosis considerations provided by the psychiatrist.]

[risk] Risk

\[Make a risk assessment in a statement (e.g., suicidal or homicidal ideation, self-harm or harm to others, and risk of relapse, if any). Include any information related to past suicide attempts or past self-harming behaviour. Include any information about psychosocial stressors contributing to the level of risk. Describe any protective factors that mitigate the risk.]

[treatment_plan_discussed_with_patient] Treatment Plan Discussed With Patient

* \[Specify any evidence-based recommendations for medication changes made by the psychiatrist, and specify if any existing medications should be continued or discontinued. Provide the psychiatrist's rationale for these recommendations. If the psychiatrist has not made any recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify any suggested non-pharmacological interventions made by the psychiatrist, such as psychotherapy or lifestyle modifications. Provide the psychiatrist's rationale for these recommendations. If the psychiatrist has not made any recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Specify if any referrals to other specialists or services have been or will be made by the psychiatrist (e.g., lab tests). Provide the psychiatrist's rationale for these recommendations. If the psychiatrist has not made any recommendations, leave blank.]
* \[Detail what the plan is for follow-up and monitoring, as detailed by the psychiatrist (e.g., next appointment details). If the psychiatrist has not made any plans for follow-up or monitoring, leave blank.]
* \[Detail if any Safety Plan has been made by the psychiatrist in the event that the patient poses a risk to safety, including steps to take in a crisis. If the psychiatrist has not made any Safety Plan, leave blank.]
* \[Summarise any education provided by the psychiatrist to the patient about their diagnosis and treatment options. Document the patient’s understanding and agreement with the plan. If the psychiatrist has not provided any education to the patient about their diagnosis and treatment options, leave blank.]

[summary] Summary

\[Provide a brief paragraph summary of the appointment in no more than four sentences.]

[other_practitioners] Other Practitioners

* \[Detail names, locations and organisations and contact details of other practitioners involved in the patient's care, including doctors, case managers, lawyers, or other contacts. Use as many bullet points as needed to capture practitioner details (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

sections.label: Strengths, Coping Skills, Recreational Interests, and Hobbies

[medications] Current Medications

* \[List current psychiatric and non\-psychiatric medications\, including doses and frequencies\. Assess adherence to the prescribed regimen\. Note any reported side effects or concerns about medications\. Include evidence\-based reasons and considerations for possible medication adjustments\, if applicable\.\]\(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\. [risk] Risk

Describe any protective factors that mitigate the risk.]

* [Specify any suggested non-pharmacological interventions, such as psychotherapy or lifestyle modifications. Describe the rationale for these recommendations.]
* [Specify if any referrals to other specialists or services have been made (e.g., lab tests). Describe the rationale for these recommendations.]
* [Detail what the plan is for follow-up and monitoring (e.g., next appointment details).]
* [Detail if any Safety Plan has been made in the event that the patient poses a risk to safety, including steps to take in a crisis.]
* [Summarise any education provided to the patient about their diagnosis and treatment options. Document the patient’s understanding and agreement with the plan.]

[summary] Summary

[Provide a brief paragraph summary of the session in no more than four sentences.]

title: Psychiatric Medication Review
description: For use by a psychiatrist seeing a previously known patient for a medication review appointment, this template provides focused information on the
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by psychiatrists conducting a medication review of a previously known patient. Using the provided transcript, you will generate a detailed summary of the appointment. Ensure the summary is concise, adheres to evidence-based practice, and professionally worded according to accepted psychiatric terminology, such as using the term "patient" instead of "client". The content of the note should include clear, clinically relevant observations and recommendations. Avoid including direct quotes from the transcript unless they are pivotal to the therapeutic process. Organise the note under the following headings.)

[identifying_information] Identifying Information

* [Detail the patient's name or initials, age, gender, and date of today's consultation.]

[diagnosis_and_clinical_concerns] Diagnosis and Clinical Concerns

* [List all known diagnoses and key clinical concerns.]

[reason_for_consultation] Reason for Consultation

Medication review. [Include any further brief statement that gives context to the reason for the medication review (e.g., requires new prescription, ineffectiveness of current medication regime, unacceptable side effects of current medication, scheduled follow-up appointment). Include any other information on the patient’s progress since their last appointment, including improvements, challenges, and observations from the patient or others.]

[mse] Mental Status Examination (MSE)

[Provide a short paragraph using language consistent with accepted psychiatric and medical terms of no more than four sentences that summarises the patient’s presentation using the following standard MSE domains (only include each domain if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note, otherwise leave blank): Appearance (the patient’s general physical appearance, hygiene, grooming, and attire); Behaviour (the patient’s interaction with their surrounds, activity level, psychomotor activity, eye contact, and rapport); Speech (the rate, volume, tone, coherence, and articulation of the patient’s speech; including if the client communicates in any other non-verbal way, such as with gestures, noises, or eye contact); Mood and Affect (the patient’s reported mood and emotional state using their own words if possible; including the range and appropriateness of the patient’s emotional response during the appointment, and note any discrepancies with the patient’s stated mood; including any observations about emotional expression made by the clinician; including any behaviours that provide insight into mood and affect, such as anger outbursts, withdrawal, and laughing; including any other factors that may be providing insight into the patient’s mood or affect, such as expressions of illness, or other interactions with the clinician; including the response of the patient to the use of any visual aids regarding mood or affect, such as mood scales); Thought Process (the patient’s coherence, organisation, and flow of thoughts); Thought Content (the content of the patient’s thoughts, and note the presence of delusions, distortions, obsessions, or intrusive thoughts, if any); Perception (hallucinations or other perceptual disturbances of the patient, if any); Cognition (the patient’s orientation to time / place / person, attention, memory, concentration, and comprehension; [medications] Current Medications

* \[List current psychiatric and non\-psychiatric medications\, including doses and frequencies\.\]\(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\. If the medication name is unclear or cannot be confidently identified from the transcript\, print \[Medication Name\] rather than inferring or guessing the name\.\)

[notes_on_current_medications] Notes on Current Medications

* \[Assess adherence to the prescribed medication regime\, including any challenges \(e\.g\.\, forgetfulness\, cost\, or stigma\)\. Note any patient reported side effects or concerns about medications \(e\.g\.\, severity\, onset\, impact on functioning\)\. Detail the patient’s assessment of medication effectiveness\, including any changes to symptoms since taking the medication\. Detail the patient’s concerns or issues about taking the current medications\.\]\(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\.\)

Include any feedback from the psychiatrist on the patient’s self\-report of the effectiveness of the current medication\.\]\(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note\, otherwise leave blank\.\)

[plan_including_changes_to_medication] Plan (Including Changes to Medication)

[risk] Risk
