Only include if explicitly mentioned in the added files, transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print sub-heading.)] **Recommendations** * [This section should list specific, actionable recommendations based exclusively on what the school counsellor / psychologist has stated or what is contained in supporting documentation. Organise recommendations into logical categories using appropriate subheadings. All recommendations must be explicitly mentioned by the school counsellor / psychologist—do not generate recommendations independently.] [Consider organising recommendations under categories such as: \- Academic Supports and Curriculum Adjustments \- Behavioural Supports and Interventions \- Social\-Emotional Support \- Classroom Environment and Teaching Strategies \- Resource Requirements \(e\.g\.\, teacher aide hours\, specialist support\, assistive technology\) \- Funding and Resource Applications \(if applicable\) \- Professional Development for Staff \- Collaboration and Consultation \(with external providers\, specialists\, or services\) \- Home\-School Communication and Parental Support \- Monitoring and Review \- Referrals for Further Assessment or Services\] [Within each category, list specific recommendations clearly and concisely. Each recommendation should specify: \- What action needs to be taken \- Who is responsible for implementing the recommendation \(e\.g\.\, classroom teacher\, school leadership\, learning support team\, external provider\) \- When the action should be implemented or reviewed \(where specified\) \- Expected outcomes or goals \(where specified\)\] [Only include categories and recommendations that have been explicitly stated by the school counsellor / psychologist in added files, transcripts, contextual notes, clinical notes, or supporting documentation. Do not print sub-headings of blank categories.]
**Funding Justification** [If this report is intended to support a funding application or request for additional resources, this section should clearly articulate: \- The specific funding or resources being requested \(e\.g\.\, Individual Disability Allocation level\, additional teacher aide hours\, specialist equipment\); \- Evidence supporting the need for this level of funding\, including assessment results\, functional impacts\, and intensity of support required; \- How the requested funding aligns with relevant funding frameworks \(e\.g\.\, NCCD categories and levels of adjustment\, state\-specific disability funding criteria\); \- Expected outcomes if funding is approved and how resources will be utilised to support the student's educational access and participation\.\] (Only include the above section if this report has been stated to support a funding application. Only include information explicitly provided by the school counsellor or contained in supporting documentation.]
**Summary and Conclusion** [In two concise paragraphs, provide: A brief summary of the student's current situation and primary support needs; the key recommendations and their expected impact on the student's educational outcomes; and any critical next steps or timeframes for implementation. (Only include if sufficient information has been provided to create a meaningful summary, otherwise leave blank.] [Print the following:] This report has been prepared to support [student's first name]'s educational needs and should be considered alongside input from teachers, parents/caregivers, and other professionals involved in [his/her/their] care. The recommendations outlined are based on current available information and should be reviewed regularly as [student's first name]'s needs and circumstances evolve. For any questions regarding this report or to discuss implementation of recommendations, please feel free to contact me. Yours sincerely, [School counsellor's / psychologist's first and last name] [Professional title]

title: Schools - Case Conference/Parent Meeting
description: For mental health professionals working in schools or other educational settings to document meetings, case conferences and other communications with
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by school psychologists, counsellors, and other mental health clinicians working in educational settings to document meetings with teachers, learning support staff, parents/carers, and other stakeholders about a student. The meeting and transcript may involve multiple speakers. Review the transcript and identify which speaker corresponds to who. When documenting statements, concerns or observations, clearly identify who made each point. Using the provided transcript, you will generate a detailed, yet concise summary of the meeting. Organise the summary under the following headings.)
Time & Date: [The time and date of the meeting] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) Student: [Name and year level/grade of the student being discussed] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) Location: [Location of meeting] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [meeting_attendees] Meeting Attendees * [List all attendees present. Include their name, role/position, relationship to student - e.g., classroom teacher, learning support officer, parent, school counsellor] [apologies] Apologies * [List of people who were not able to attend the meeting.] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank.) [meeting_purpose] Meeting Purpose * [Summarise the reason for the meeting, specific concerns being addressed, or goals of the discussion] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [student_overview_and_current_concerns] Student Overview and Current Concerns * [Detailed description of the student's current academic, social, emotional, or behavioural presentation. For each concern raised, document who raised it (e.g., "Teacher reported frequent absences," "Parent expressed concern about sleep difficulties"). Include any recent incidents, changes, or developments affecting the student. Document any strengths, skills, positive resources, or support networks identified.] [discussion_and_key_issues] Discussion And Key Issues * [Detail the key discussion points and perspectives shared by different stakeholders. Include relevant observations, insights, or updates. Document any identified barriers or concerns related to the student's progress or care, and any differences in viewpoints between stakeholders.] * Teacher perspectives: [Classroom observations, academic performance, behaviour management strategies, and teacher concerns] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) * Parent/Carer perspectives: [Family perspectives, home observations, concerns, and any relevant family circumstances] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) * Learning Support perspectives: [Observations from learning support staff, intervention effectiveness, and additional support needs] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) * Student voice: [Student's own perspective if participating, or their views as reported by others] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [assessment_and_intervention_review] Assessment and Intervention Review * [Review of any assessments, evaluations, or formal testing completed] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) * [Effectiveness of current interventions, accommodations, or support strategies] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) * [Gaps in current support or areas needing additional intervention] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [agreed_strategies_and_action_plan] Agreed Strategies and Action Plan * [Record details of any plans or strategies agreed to, including classroom strategies and accommodations. Document specific actions to be taken, by whom, and by when. Include any referrals to be made, resources or support to be provided, behaviour management approaches, communication protocols between home and school, and monitoring arrangements. Note how progress will be monitored and who will be responsible for reporting back.] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [risk_assessment_and_safety_planning] Risk Assessment and Safety Planning * [Document any safety concerns identified at school or home and any risk management strategies agreed upon] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [additional_notes] Additional Notes * [Any supplementary comments, observations, or relevant context that emerged during the meeting. Include any resources, handouts, or materials shared, and budget or funding considerations discussed.] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [next_meeting] Next Meeting * [Document the date and time of next meeting] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) * [If discussed, note the specific focus or agenda for next meeting] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank) [meeting_summary] Meeting Summary [Provide a brief paragraph summarising the key concerns discussed, main decisions made, and next steps planned for supporting the student] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank)
(Review the entire note to confirm that statements are consistently attributed to the correct person throughout the note. Correct any inconsistencies to maintain clear and accurate attribution.)

title: Schools - Observations
description: For mental health professionals to dictate observations of students made in schools and other educational settings. This template is designed to
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by school psychologists, counsellors, and other mental health clinicians working in educational settings to document observations of students in various school environments. Using the provided transcript or observation notes, you will generate a detailed, objective summary of the student's behaviour, interactions, and functioning. Organise the summary under the following headings with heading 3 formatting.)

[observation_details] Observation Details

Date: [Date of observation] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
Time: [Start and end time of observation] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
Duration: [Total observation time] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
Location: [Specific location(s) where observation took place - classroom, playground, library, etc.] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
Observer: [Name and role of observer] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[observation_context] Observation Context

* Activity/Lesson: [Description of the activity, lesson, or situation being observed] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Setting Description: [Physical environment, number of students present, adult supervision, noise level, structure of activity] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Purpose of Observation: [Specific behaviours, skills, or concerns being observed] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[academic_behaviour_and_engagement] Academic Behaviour and Engagement

* Attention and Focus: [Student's ability to attend to tasks, maintain concentration, and resist distractions] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Task Completion: [Student's approach to completing work, persistence, and quality of output] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Following Instructions: [Student's ability to understand and follow teacher directions] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Participation: [Level of participation in class discussions, activities, and group work] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[social_behaviour_and_interactions] Social Behaviour and Interactions

* Peer Relationships: [Quality of interactions with classmates, ability to make friends, social inclusion/exclusion] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Communication Skills: [Verbal and non-verbal communication, conversation skills, listening abilities] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Conflict Resolution: [How student handles disagreements, ability to problem-solve social situations] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Group Dynamics: [Student's role in group activities, leadership, cooperation, sharing] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[emotional_and_behavioural_observations] Emotional and Behavioural Observations

* Emotional Expression: [Range and appropriateness of emotional responses, mood stability, emotional regulation] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Behavioural Patterns: [Specific behaviours observed, frequency, duration, and triggers] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Coping Strategies: [How student manages stress, frustration, or challenging situations] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Response to Authority: [Student's interaction with teachers and other school staff] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[physical_and_developmental_observations] Physical and Developmental Observations

* Motor Skills: [Fine and gross motor abilities, coordination, physical activity participation] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Sensory Processing: [Responses to sensory stimuli, seeking/avoiding behaviours, sensory preferences] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Self-Care: [Independence in personal care, organisation of belongings, self-advocacy] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[environmental_factors] Environmental Factors

* Classroom Dynamics: [Teacher management style, classroom structure, peer group composition] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Accommodations in Place: [Any modifications, supports, or accommodations being used] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* Antecedents and Triggers: [Environmental factors, events, or situations that preceded specific behaviours] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[strengths_and_positive_observations] Strengths and Positive Observations

* [Document specific strengths, talents, or positive behaviours observed. Note situations where the student performed well or showed particular skills. Identify environmental factors that support positive behaviour]

[areas_of_concern] Areas of Concern

* [Document specific concerning behaviours, patterns, or difficulties observed. Note situations where the student struggled or required additional support. Identify potential barriers to learning or social functioning]

[nextsteps] Recommendations

* [Suggest specific interventions, accommodations, or strategies based on observations]
* [Recommend further assessment or evaluation if needed]
* [Propose environmental modifications or support strategies] (use as many bullet points as needed; only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[next_steps] Next Steps

* [Plan for additional observations or monitoring]
* [Communication with parents, teachers, or other staff]
* [Referrals or consultations recommended] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[observation_summary] Observation Summary

* [Provide a summary of key findings of the observation session in 3-4 sentences]

title: Schools - Therapy Session
description: Documents therapy sessions for mental health professionals working with students in schools and other educational settings.
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by school psychologists, counsellors, and other mental health clinicians working in educational settings to document therapy, counselling or support sessions with students. Using the provided transcript, you will generate a detailed, yet concise summary of the session. Organise the summary under the following headings, and use bullet points beneath each of those headings.)

[presentation] Current Presentation

* [Describe student's current presentation, primary concerns, or reason for this session, including how long the issue(s) have been occurring. Include any concerns raised by teachers, parents/carers, or other school staff if mentioned.]
* [Describe the current impact on functioning, including academic performance, classroom engagement, peer relationships, participation in school-based activities, and attendance patterns. Note any impacts on engagement with existing supports or school programs.]

(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)

[history] Background

* Academic History: [Details of any academic difficulties, learning supports, or educational accommodations.]
* Behavioural History: [Details of any behavioural incidents, disciplinary actions, or classroom management strategies.]
* Family/Home Factors: [Details of any family circumstances, home environment factors, or parent/carer concerns affecting the student.]
* Previous Support: [Details of any previous counselling, therapy, or school-based interventions.]

(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)

[mse] Mental Status Examination

* Behaviour: [Observe the student's activity level, interaction with their surroundings, and any unique or notable behaviors (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Speech: [If the pace of speech was fast or slow for the speaker who is the student, note the pace of speech for the student, where a “normal pace of speech” is between 130 and 200 words per minute, “fast pace of speech” is defined as 201 words per minute or over, and “slow pace of speech” is defined as less than 129 words per minute. Only include a note on the pace of speech if it is fast or slow, if it is normal, do not include it in the note]
* Mood: [Record the student's self-described emotional state, using their own words if possible (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Affect: [Describe the range and appropriateness of the student's emotional response during the examination, noting any discrepancies with the stated mood (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Thoughts: [Assess the student's thought process and thought content, noting any distortions, delusions, or preoccupations (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Perceptions: [Note any reported hallucinations or sensory misinterpretations, specifying type and impact on the student (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Cognition: [Describe the student's memory, orientation to time/place/person, concentration, and comprehension (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Insight: [Describe the student's understanding of their own condition and symptoms, noting any lack of awareness or denial (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Judgment: [Describe the student's decision-making ability and understanding of the consequences of their actions (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[content] Session Content

* [Describe details of discussions during the session, including key themes, concerns raised by the student, and topics explored] (use as many bullet points as needed to capture all details discussed)
* [Document any school-related issues discussed: academic stress, peer relationships, teacher interactions, bullying, or classroom difficulties] (use as many bullet points as needed to capture all details discussed)
* [Note any family or home-related matters that impact school functioning]

[goals] Goals

* [Describe any therapeutic goals discussed, including academic, social, emotional, or behavioural objectives] (use as many bullet points as needed; only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section)

[interventions] Interventions

* [Provide detail of the specific interventions, techniques, or strategies used during the session, including any safety planning or risk management] (use as many bullet points as needed; only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* [Note any coping strategies, skills, or techniques taught to the student] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* [Describe any ideas, concepts or frameworks that were introduced, or the content of any handouts or use of whiteboard)(use as many bullet points as needed to capture all the details discussed) (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* [Engagement: Note the student's willingness to participate, openness to discussion, and rapport with the clinician] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[progress_and_challenges] Progress and Challenges

* [Describe progress made towards therapeutic or academic goals] (use as many bullet points as needed; only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* [Document any setbacks, obstacles, or challenges to progress] (use as many bullet points as needed; only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)
* [Note any changes in school functioning, academic performance, or peer relationships] (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank)

[risk] Risk Assessment and Management

* Suicidal Ideation: [describe any history of suicidal ideation, attempts, plans in detail] (if current suicidal thoughts or behaviours are present, highlight text in red)
* Homicidal Ideation: [Describe any homicidal ideation] (if past or current homicidal thoughts or behaviours are mentioned, highlight text in red)
* Self-harm: [Detail any history of self-harm] (if current self-harm is present, highlight text in red)
* Violence & Aggression: [Describe any recent or past incidents of violence or aggression]
* Addictive Behaviours: [Detail any recent or past addictive behaviours, e.g., illicit drugs, gambling]
* Risk-taking/Impulsivity: [Describe any risk-taking behaviours or impulsivity]
* Management Plan: [Describe strategy or steps taken to manage suicidal ideation / homicidal ideation / self-harm / violence & aggression / substance use / risk-taking behaviour (if applicable)]

(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)

[collaboration_and_communication] Collaboration and Communication

* [Note any communication with parents/carers, teachers, or other school staff discussed or planned]
* [Document any referrals made or recommended, as well as any case conferences or student support team meetings planned.]
* [Record any classroom accommodations, adjustments, or strategies suggested for teachers]

(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)

[nextsteps] Next Steps

* Next Session: [Date, time, and focus of next session]
* Between-Session Tasks: [Any activities, homework, or strategies for the student to practice]
* Follow-up Actions: [Any communication, referrals, or collaboration planned with other school staff or parents]

(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise exclude section.)

[summary] Session Summary

* [Summarise the key points from the session in 2-3 sentences]

title: Sex Therapy
description: Designed for clinicians providing sex therapy using approaches such as Sensate Focus, Cognitive Behavioural Sex Therapy, Mindfulness-Based Sex
prompt:
(Provide a progress note for a clinician conducting sex therapy, which may include approaches such as Sensate Focus, Cognitive Behavioural Sex Therapy, Mindfulness-Based Sex Therapy, the Masters and Johnson approach, or other evidence-based interventions. The session and transcript include a therapist and a couple, but may be an individual client. When documenting statements, concerns, observations, or interventions, clearly identify who made each point. Keep documentation concise and focused on clinically relevant information. Avoid repetition. Use the transcript, notes and contextual notes to summarise the session, using appropriate sex therapy terminology and interventions. Organise the summary according to the subheadings below.)
(Start the output at Session Content, do not print anything else prior) [content] Session Content * [Presenting concerns: Briefly summarise the primary sexual and other clinically relevant concerns discussed, noting any changes since the previous session. Include any differences in how the partners describe the issue or different perspectives. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] * [Review of homework/sexual exercises: Briefly document progress with previously assigned exercises (e.g., sensate focus, communication tasks, mindfulness practices), focusing on key outcomes, barriers, and client responses. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] * [Sexual experiences and communication: Document discussions of sexual experiences, physical sensations, intimate communication, and sexual functioning that occurred between sessions or during previous exercises. Note any significant revelations or concerns about sexual response, desire, pleasure, or intimacy. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] * [Key topics discussed: Summarise the main sexual health issues, patterns, cycles or dynamics explored during the session and their relevance to treatment goals. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] [sexual_health_assessment_and_dynamics] Sexual Health Assessment and Dynamics [Document sexual functioning, desire patterns, communication about sexuality, relationship dynamics affecting intimacy, and any physical or psychological factors discussed. Note relevant medical history, medications, or health conditions mentioned. Include relational dynamics around sexuality and intimacy.] [therapeutic_alliance_and_engagement] Therapeutic Alliance and Engagement [Briefly note engagement level and comfort discussing sexual topics. Document any resistance, shame, or collaborative moments observed during the session (use 1-2 sentences or bullet points)] [interventions] Interventions [Provide specific detail about all therapeutic interventions used and the therapeutic rationale. This may include sex therapy techniques such as sensate focus assignments, cognitive restructuring for sexual beliefs, mindfulness exercises, communication training, psychoeducation about sexual response, exposure exercises, as well as general therapeutic interventions such as CBT techniques, psychodynamic exploration, or systemic interventions. For each intervention, identify the specific technique, specify the therapeutic approach, detail what was targeted (e.g., performance anxiety, desire discrepancy, communication barriers), and summarise how clients responded, including any notable shifts in understanding or comfort level.] (Note: provide sufficient detail for another therapist to continue treatment. Use as many bullet points as needed for separate interventions) [progress] Progress [Provide a concise summary of progress toward sexual health goals. Focus on clinically significant changes in sexual functioning, communication, intimacy, or relationship satisfaction. Note areas where progress is evident and areas that remain challenging.] [homework] Homework [Describe exercises, communication tasks, or other assignments given to clients, including rationale and expected outcomes. Note any concerns or feedback from clients about the assignments. (Only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise exclude section)] [risk] Risk Assessment [Document any safety concerns, risk factors, or other clinical risks identified. Note any risk management strategies implemented or planned. (Only include if mentioned in the transcript otherwise exclude section)] [nextsteps] Next Steps [Outline the focus for the next session and any specific goals or exercises to continue. Provide the time, day and date of any future session agreed upon.] [summary] Summary [Provide a brief recap of key points from the session in no more than three sentences, summarising the sexual concerns addressed, interventions used, client responses, and treatment direction.]
(Review the entire note to confirm that statements are consistently attributed to the correct individual throughout the note. Correct any inconsistencies to maintain clear and accurate attribution.)

title: SMART Goals for Clients
description: Document SMART goals and provide them to clients. This template focuses on helping the clinician and client record SMART goals i.e., goals that are
prompt:
(The template below will summarise a section of a session where a clinician and client have discussed the client’s goals. Output will focus only on those aspects of the session that deal with setting and/or achieving goals. Your output will not cover any other aspect of the session that is not relevant to the client’s goals. A discussion about goals between a clinician and a client may be a highly structured approach or a less structured, more casual approach. Goal-setting sessions may be the first session between a mental health clinician and a client or a follow-up session where a client may modify their goals, set new goals, or review progress against goals. You will decide what type of conversation is being had about goals in this session: is it goal-setting, goal-modifying, goal-tracking, or a combination of these. Your analysis of the goals discussed must follow the SMART approach towards setting goals: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This document is designed to record client goals and will be provided to the client. Therefore, you must provide your output in the first person, referring to the client by name, and using terms such as "you" or "your" rather than referring to them as "the client" or "the patient" in the third person. Write in an encouraging yet concrete manner. Organise your summary using the following format:) [smart_goals_print_client_s_first_and_las] SMART GOALS - [Print client’s first and last name] [Print the session date in Day Month, Year format] Dear [client first name], Thank you for discussing your goals with me. This document uses the SMART Goal framework to clearly define what you hope to achieve. 🥇 **GOAL 1:** [Goal Title] [Paraphrase the goal as articulated by the client and clinician during the session. Capture the essence of what the client wants to achieve, including any context about why this goal matters to them.] 🧠 SMART Summary: | SMART | Status | Description | | ----- | ------ | ----------- | | Specific
🎯 | [Insert 🟢 if the goal meets criterion, insert 🔴 if the goal does not meet criterion, or insert 🟠 if the goal only partially meets criterion.] | [Define the specific element of the goal, as described in session. If the goal is only partially specific, state why it is only partially specific and encourage the client to be even more specific. If the goal is not specific at all, print “Please describe the goal in more specific terms.” For goals that are partially met or not met at all, provide a suggestion in the form of a question about how the client can make their goal more specific.] | | Measurable
📏 | [Insert 🟢 if the goal meets criterion, insert 🔴 if the goal does not meet criterion, or insert 🟠 if the goal only partially meets criterion.] | [Define the measurable element of the goal, as described in session. If the goal is only partially measurable, state why it is only partially measurable and encourage the client to be even more measurable. If the goal is not measurable at all, print "Please describe the goal in more measurable terms." For goals that are partially met or not met at all, provide a suggestion in the form of a question about how the client can make their goal more measurable.] | | Achievable
🏆 | [Insert 🟢 if the goal meets criterion, insert 🔴 if the goal does not meet criterion, or insert 🟠 if the goal only partially meets criterion.] | [Define the achievable element of the goal, as described in session. If the goal is only partially achievable, state why it is only partially achievable and encourage the client to be even more achievable. If the goal is not achievable at all, print "Please describe the goal in more achievable terms." For goals that are partially met or not met at all, provide a suggestion in the form of a question about how the client can make their goal more achievable.] | | Relevant
💡 | [Insert 🟢 if the goal meets criterion, insert 🔴 if the goal does not meet criterion, or insert 🟠 if the goal only partially meets criterion.] | [Define the relevant element of the goal, as described in session. If the goal is only partially relevant, state why it is only partially relevant and encourage the client to be even more relevant. If the goal is not relevant at all, print "Please describe the goal in more relevant terms." For goals that are partially met or not met at all, provide a suggestion in the form of a question about how the client can make their goal more relevant.] | | Time-Bound
⌚ | [Insert 🟢 if the goal meets criterion, insert 🔴 if the goal does not meet criterion, or insert 🟠 if the goal only partially meets criterion.] | [Define the time-bound element of the goal, as described in session. If the goal is only partially time-bound, state why it is only partially time-bound and encourage the client to be even more time-bound. If the goal is not time-bound at all, print "Please describe the goal in more time-bound terms." For goals that are partially met or not met at all, provide a suggestion in the form of a question about how the client can make their goal more time-bound.] | 📢 Action Steps: [List concrete actions or steps that can be taken to move towards the goal. If no concrete steps were discussed, write “Please list your next steps towards this goal”.] 🧱 Anticipated Barriers And Obstacles: [Describe barriers that might get in the way of achieving the goal. This may include external obstacles in the world, or internal obstacles such as habits or lack of motivation. If not explicitly discussed in the transcript, leave blank and do not print sub-heading.] ✨ Strategies To Overcome Obstacles: [Describe ways of overcoming barriers or obstacles to achieving the goal. What skills, strategies, mindsets, habits were discussed. Describe in detail. If not explicitly discussed in the transcript, leave blank and do not print heading.] 📖 Progress Review [Summarise what the client and clinician discussed regarding current progress on this goal. Include specific examples, achievements, challenges, setbacks, or barriers mentioned. Write in an affirming and congratulatory way if the client is on track with their goal. If the client is not on track, be realistic and consider reframing the failure in an encouraging and compassionate way. Describe the current status of the client’s progress with achieving this goal by using terms such as “On track” / “Partially achieved” / “Achieved” / “Modified” / “Discontinued” / “Experiencing barriers”. Note if and how the goal was adjusted, refined, or replaced with a new goal. If there is no explicit mention of the client’s current progress with this goal, leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.] 🥇 **GOAL 2:** [Goal Title/Brief Description] [Repeat the same structure as Goal 1 as many times as necessary for each additional goal that was discussed.] ***

sections.label: SMART GOALS - [Print client’s first and last name]

title: SOAP - Subjective Objective Assessment Plan
description: Assessment note using the following titles: Subjective information, Past History, Objective information,  Assessment Plan
prompt:
\[Create a session progress note based on the Subjective Objective Assessment Plan, or SOAP, framework. The progress note will summarise a session between a mental health clinician and a client. Use the following template outline:]

[subjective] Subjective

* \[Describe the reasons for the client's visit. Describe the client's presenting concerns and chief complaints, including symptoms, requests, etc. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe the duration, timing, location, quality, severity, context of the client's presenting concerns and chief complaints (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe anything that worsens or alleviates the symptoms, including self-treatment attempts and their effectiveness (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe how the the client's symptoms have changed, progressed, or evolved over time (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe any past occurrences of similar symptoms, including when they occurred, how they were managed, and the outcomes (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe the impact of the client's presenting concerns and chief complaints on their daily activities. Explain how the symptoms affect the client's daily life, work, relationships, and activities (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe any other symptoms (focal and systemic) that accompany the reasons for the client's visit and their chief complaints (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[past_history] Past History

* \[Describe contributing factors including past psychiatric and relevant medical history, investigations, treatments, and other factors relevant to the reasons for the client's visit and chief complaints (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe social history that may be relevant to the reasons for the client's visit and chief complaints (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe family history that may be relevant to the reasons for the client's visit and chief complaints (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe any other information that may be relevant to the reasons for the client's visit and chief complaints (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[objective] Objective

[mse] Mental State Examination

* Appearance: \[Describe the client's clothing, hygiene, and any notable physical characteristics (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Behaviour: \[Describe the client's activity level, interaction with their surroundings, and any unique or notable behaviours (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Speech: \[Note the rate, volume, clarity, and coherence of the client's speech (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Mood: \[Record the client's self-described emotional state, using their own words if possible (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Affect: \[Describe the range and appropriateness of the client's emotional response during the examination, noting any discrepancies with the stated mood (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Thoughts: \[Assess the client's thought process and content, noting any distortions, delusions, or preoccupations (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Perceptions: \[Note any reported hallucinations or sensory misinterpretations, specifying type and impact on the client (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Cognition: \[Describe the client's memory, orientation to time/place/person, concentration, and comprehension (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Insight: \[Describe the client's understanding of their own condition and symptoms, noting any lack of awareness or denial (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* Judgment: \[Describe the client's decision-making ability and understanding of the consequences of their actions (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[investigations_with_results] Investigations With Results

* \[Describe any investigations with results discussed by the clinician and the client. You must only include completed investigations and the results of these investigations that have been explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise you must leave Investigations With Results blank. All planned or ordered investigations must not be included under Objective; instead all planned or ordered investigations must be included under Plan.]

[psychometric_assessments] Psychometric Assessments

* \[Describe any psychometric assessments discussed by the clinician and the client. You must only include completed psychometric assessments that have been explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise you must leave Psychometric Assessments blank. All planned or ordered psychometric assessments must not be included under Objective; instead all planned or ordered investigations must be included under Plan.]

[assessment] Assessment

* \[Describe the client's likely diagnosis and any observations made by the clinician in regard to the diagnosis (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe the differential diagnosis provided by the clinician (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[nextsteps] Plan

* \[Describe all the planned assessments (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe all the planned treatment (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Describe all other relevant actions such as counselling, referrals, etc. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

(Never come up with your own patient details, assessment, plan, interventions, evaluation, and plan for continuing care - use only the transcript or contextual notes as a reference for the information included in your note.)

title: Social Worker Session Note
description: Social workers work with a wide variety of clients in different settings. Some social workers prefer using a particular type of case note format,
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used to summarise a session between a social worker and a client. If there is no content for a particular section heading, do not print that section heading. Organise your summary using the following format:)

Date: [Print the date in Day Month, Year format.]

Client: [Print the client’s first and last name.]

Other attendees: [Print the names of any other attendees, and explain the relationship of these attendees to the client.]

[client_situational_summary] Client Situational Summary

[Provide a summary of the client’s living situation or immediate context if relevant to the session. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[presenting_issues_session_focus] Presenting Issues / Session Focus

[Describe the client’s presenting issues, and specifically focus on what has led them to seek having a session today. Summarise what was discussed in session. Describe any immediate concerns or crises mentioned by the client. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[psychosocial_observations] Psychosocial Observations

[Describe the client’s affect, engagement level, and overall presentation. Describe any notable changes from any previous sessions mentioned by the social worker or the client. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[services_provided_interventions] Services Provided / Interventions

[Describe what the social worker did during the session e.g., counselling, case management, referrals, advocacy, psychoeducation, etc. Describe if there were any specific evidence-based psychological therapy modalities used e.g., CBT, ACT, DBT, etc. Describe any resources or referrals provided to the client by the social worker, or any other aspect of the social worker’s case management activities for the client. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[progress_toward_goals] Progress Toward Goals

[Provide a brief update on treatment or service goals. Describe what is working, and what barriers exist. Describe any development of or changes to any service plan. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[risk] Risk Assessment & Management Plan

[Describe any safety concerns related to the client e.g., self-harm, housing instability, basic needs, exploitation, financial hardship, etc. Describe strategies or steps taken to manage suicidal ideation, homicidal ideation, self-harm, violence and aggression, substance use, risk-taking behaviour, or any other risk. Describe any protective factors that mitigate the risk. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note.) If no issues related to risk were mentioned during the session, print: “No concerns noted.”]

[strengths_and_protective_factors] Strengths & Protective Factors

[Summarise the client’s existing strengths, coping strategies, and supports that contribute to progress or resilience. Include examples where the client demonstrated initiative, insight, or engagement e.g., accessing helpful resources, made a useful lifestyle change, etc. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[follow_up_and_next_steps] Follow-Up & Next Steps

[Describe any action items, tasks, or activities for the client to do e.g., homework tasks, make appointments with health professionals, etc. Describe any action items, tasks, or activities for the social worker to do e.g., follow-up or coordination with any key stakeholders, services, or organisations, etc. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

Next Session: [Mention date and time of next session. Mention any plans about next session. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: Solution-Focused Therapy (SFT) Session Note
description: For therapists working within a Solution-Focused Therapy (also known as Solution-Focused Brief Therapy or SFBT) framework, this template summarises a
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by therapists working within a Solution-Focused Therapy, or SFT, framework with clients who present with a variety of concerns. Using the transcript from the session, you will generate a detailed summary of the session from a SFT perspective. Ensure the summary is concise and uses solution-focused language, focusing on client strengths, resources, exceptions, and preferred futures rather than problems and deficits. The language should reflect the collaborative, respectful, and curious stance central to SFT. Organise the summary under the following headings:)

[reason_for_session_and_context] Reason for Session and Context

[Provide a brief statement of what brought the client to therapy or the reason for the current session. This should be minimal detail - just enough context to understand the situation. Frame this in the client's own words where possible, focusing on what they want to be different rather than extensive problem description. For example: "Client attended seeking help with low mood and wanting to feel more connected to family" rather than detailed symptom lists. Include any relevant updates since the last session. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[client_s_best_hopes_and_preferred_future] Client's Best Hopes and Preferred Future

[Summarise what the client identified as their best hopes for therapy or what they want to be different in their life. Include specific, behavioural descriptions of their preferred future - what they will be doing differently, how others will notice, what the first small signs of change will look like. If the miracle question was used, integrate the client's response here, capturing their description of life after the miracle. Use the client's own language wherever possible. Note any goals or outcomes the client hopes to achieve. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[progress_and_change] Progress and Change

[Describe any positive differences, improvements, or changes that occurred between sessions or before the first session. Note what the client has been doing differently that contributed to these changes. Document what is already working in the client's life. Include the client's own explanation of how these changes happened. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[exceptions_to_the_problem] Exceptions to the Problem

[Identify and describe times when the problem is less severe, absent, or better managed. Detail what the client was doing differently during these exception times, what was happening in their life, who was present, and what actions or thoughts contributed to the exception. Include how the client explained these exceptions and what they learned from examining them. Document any past solutions or successful coping strategies the client has used. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[progress_and_scaling] Progress and Scaling

[Record all scaling questions used and the client's responses. For each scale used (such as progress toward goal, confidence, coping, motivation), note: the client's current rating, what specifically places them at that number rather than lower, what would be a "good enough" number, and what would represent one point higher on the scale. If VIPs (Very Important Persons) perspectives were explored through scaling, include those ratings and what keeps those numbers from being lower. Document the client's assessment of their current position on their journey toward their preferred future. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[client_strengths_resources_and_abilities] Client Strengths, Resources, and Abilities

[Document the client's strengths, skills, resources, and abilities that emerged during the session. Include coping strategies, personal qualities, support systems, past successes, or any resources available to the client. Describe how the client has managed to cope with or survive their difficulties. Note VIPs (Very Important Persons) in the client's life and what these people appreciate about the client. Reference specific examples from the client's life that demonstrate their competence and resourcefulness. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[compliments_and_affirmations] Compliments and Affirmations

[Document direct and indirect compliments given to the client during the session. Include validation of the client's efforts, acknowledgment of their strengths, and recognition of positive changes or progress. Note any questions like "How did you manage to do that?" that invited the client to recognise their own competence. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[interventions_and_techniques_used] Interventions and Techniques Used

[Detail the specific Solution-Focused Therapy techniques employed during the session, such as: solution-building conversation, exception-finding questions, scaling questions, coping questions, relationship questions (e.g., "What would your partner notice?"), the miracle question, presuppositional questions, or compliments. Describe the client's engagement with these interventions and any notable responses. Keep this section concise - focus on listing the techniques rather than describing content already captured in other sections. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[client_engagement_and_insights] Client Engagement and Insights

[Describe the client's level of engagement with the solution-focused approach and the quality of the therapeutic relationship. Note any shifts in the client's perspective from problem-focused to solution-focused thinking. Capture moments when the client showed increased hopefulness, recognition of their own abilities, or new awareness of what is already working. Include any "aha moments" or important themes that emerged. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[between_session_tasks_and_next_steps] Between-Session Tasks and Next Steps

[Outline any experiments, tasks, or suggestions offered to the client for the period between sessions. Detail what the client agreed to try, notice, or do more of. Include invitations to observe exceptions, continue what is working, or experiment with small changes. Note the client's confidence in completing these tasks and the collaborative nature of developing them. If discussed, note plans for the next session and include the day, date, and time. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank and do not print the sub-heading.)]

[summary] Summary

[Provide a brief recap in no more than four sentences that highlights: the client's preferred future or best hopes, key exceptions or strengths identified, the client's current position on their progress (using scaling if relevant), and any between-session tasks or next steps agreed upon.]

sections.label: Client's Best Hopes and Preferred Future

title: Summary of Conversation
description: This template can be used to summarise work meetings or professional conversations.
prompt:
(The context for this meeting may be a work meeting, or other general or professional conversation.)

[summary_of_conversation] Summary of Conversation

* Date of Meeting: \[Print the date of the meeting in DD/MM/YYYY format.]
* Time of Meeting: \[Print the time of the meeting in 24-hour HH/MM format.]
* Participants: \[Print the names of participants in the meeting.]

[key_points] Key Points

* \[List all of the reasons for the meeting, and list all of the main points discussed in the meeting, using as many dot points as needed to capture all aspects of the conversation.]

[detailed_notes] Detailed Notes

\[Describe in detail, the key aspects of the meeting including the below points.]

* \[Key information, discussion points, projects, concepts, ideas (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Key events or circumstances (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Key decisions or agreements made in the meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Tasks to be completed, including timelines (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Goals (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Intentions (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Progress towards achieving goals/objectives (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Setbacks and obstacles (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* \[Any other key aspects of the conversation.]

[nextsteps] Next Steps

* Next Meeting: \[Mention date and time of next meeting, mention any plans about next meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]
* To Be Completed Before Next Meeting: \[Mention any tasks or activities assigned, and print any other event to be undertaken prior to the next meeting (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, otherwise leave blank).]

title: Supervision
description: Summarises a supervision session, where an experienced clinician (supervisor) is providing guidance and support to a less experienced clinician
prompt:
(Provide a detailed summary of a supervision session between an experienced clinician, referred to as the supervisor, and a less experienced clinician, referred to as the supervisee. In this supervision session, the supervisor is providing guidance and support to the supervisee. The supervision session takes place in the context of a mental health setting. Include key discussion points such as: case conceptualisations; therapeutic techniques; the supervisee’s professional development; ethical and legal considerations; personal factors such as burnout, emotional responses, and others; professional boundaries; cultural competence; and reflective practice. Follow these next instructions carefully:)

(Use the following steps:
1\. Read and Analyse the Transcript: Carefully review the given transcript to identify key discussion points\, themes\, and outcomes from the session\.
2\. Identify Key Elements:

* Identify which speaker is the supervisor and which speaker is the supervisee.
* Identify the main topics discussed during the session.
* Identify important contributions from both the supervisor and the supervisee.
* Identify any specific client cases or clinical issues mentioned.
* Identify goals set for future supervision or client interventions.
* Reflect on the supervisee’s progress, challenges, and areas for development.

3\. Summarise Key Points: Condense the main elements into coherent\, concise notes\.
4\. Review and Revise: Ensure accuracy and clarity of the summary\.\)

(Organise your summary using the following headings and instructions, but exclude any headings or sections that are not explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note:)

Date: [Print the date of the supervision session in the format: day number, full month name, year (e.g., 10 September 2025).]

Time: [Print the starting time of the supervision meeting in the format HH:MM am/pm.]

Attendees: [Print the names of all attendees of the meeting, if known. Note which attendee is the supervisor and which attendee is the supervisee.]

[case_discussions] Case Discussions

(For each case discussed, create a separate section with the heading "Case [number]" and include the following three subsections:)

##### **Case 1:**

* **Clinical Case Details**: [Provide details of the client being featured in the case presentation, including presenting problems, age, background, life circumstances, how long the client has been in treatment for, diagnosis, treatment plan, and interventions. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Formulation**: [Provide details of the supervisee's understanding of the client's issues from psychological and theoretical perspectives, and of any refinement of the formulation. What theoretical framework is the supervisee viewing client issues through? (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Supervisor’s Input and Recommendations**: [Provide a detailed account of input provided by the supervisor for each case, including reflections, challenges, learning points, suggestions, strategies, or alternative perspectives. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[Repeat the above structure for Case 2, Case 3, etc. as needed.]

[intervention_and_therapy_techniques] Intervention and Therapy Techniques

* **Therapeutic Approaches**: [Review the application of therapeutic models such as CBT, DBT, ACT, Schema Therapy, and others. Be sure to include all presented cases in your analysis. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Skill Development**: [Identify areas for the supervisee’s growth in clinical techniques as described by the supervisor. For example, improving empathy, active listening, managing resistance, specific therapy approach, etc. Provide a detailed account of input provided by the supervisor, including reflections, challenges, learning points, suggestions, strategies, or alternative perspectives. Be sure to include all presented cases in your analysis. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Supervisor Past Client Review**: [Note if the supervisor reflected on any specific therapy sessions from the supervisor’s personal clinical experience, including client progress, challenges, key themes, etc. If the supervisor mentioned a specific interaction with any client to illustrate a point, provide a quote. Be sure to include all presented cases in your analysis. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[material_introduced] Material Introduced

* [Describe in detail any ideas, education, concepts, or therapeutic modalities that the supervisor introduced to the supervisee, or any handouts or other materials the supervisor provided or undertook to provide. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[supervisee_s_professional_development] Supervisee’s Professional Development

* **Learning Goals**: [Describe any professional goals that were revisited, set, or discussed, such as developing expertise in specific disorders, therapies, or populations. Be sure to include all presented cases in your analysis. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to learning goals. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Feedback**: [Provide details about any constructive feedback that was provided by the supervisor to the supervisee e.g., on clinical skills, communication, documentation, etc. Be sure to include all presented cases in your analysis. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Reflection on Growth**: [Evaluate the supervisee’s development as a clinician or therapist. Identify strengths and areas for improvement. Be sure to include all presented cases in your analysis. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to reflections on growth. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[progress_obstacles_and_setbacks] Progress, Obstacles, and Setbacks

* **Progress**: [Describe in detail the progress achieved by the supervisee towards each professional or personal goal/objective. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to progress. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Obstacles and Setbacks**: [Describe in detail the setbacks and obstacles to progress for each goal/objective for the supervisee. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to obstacles and setbacks. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[personal_factors_and_self_care] Personal Factors and Self-Care

* **Personal Impact**: [Where relevant and applicable, explore how personal issues may be influencing the clinical work of the supervisee, as described by the supervisee themselves, or described by the supervisor. For example, stress, burnout, etc. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to personal impact. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Emotional Reactions**: [Explore any countertransference, emotional responses to clients, and how these reactions are managed by the supervisee. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to emotional reactions. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Self-Care Strategies**: [Explore all the ways that the supervisee is maintaining appropriate self-care practices to avoid burnout and ensure sustainable practice. Note any specific comments or advice given by the supervisor in relation to self-care strategies. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[ethical_and_legal_issues] Ethical and Legal Issues

* **Risk Management**: [Review any legal concerns associated with any case given by the supervisee e.g., duty of care, mandatory reporting, and risk management when dealing with suicidal or self-harming clients. Describe any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to risk management. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Documentation**: [Note any issues related to clinical documentation practices and record-keeping to meet legal and ethical standards that are relevant to any case presented by the supervisee. Describe any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to documentation. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Ethical Considerations**: [Describe any ethical dilemmas or issues related to confidentiality, boundaries, dual relationships, etc. related to any case presented by the supervisee. Describe any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to ethical considerations. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[professional_role_and_relationship_manag] Professional Role and Relationship Management

* **Therapeutic Boundaries**: [Summarise any issues related to boundary management in therapy, particularly with challenging clients or in unique circumstances, as described by the supervisee. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to therapeutic boundaries. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Professional Roles**: [Summarise any issues related to relationships with colleagues and inter-professional dynamics, particularly in team-based settings, as described by the supervisee. Note any stresses or frustrations being experienced by the supervisee as a result of these professional roles issues. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to professional roles. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[cultural_and_diversity_competence] Cultural and Diversity Competence

* **Cultural Sensitivity**: [Summarise any cultural, racial, gender, and socioeconomic factors that have impacted the therapeutic relationship or client presentations, as described by the supervisee. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to cultural sensitivity. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[reflective_practice] Reflective Practice

* **Supervisee’s Experiences**: [Summarise the self-reflective insights provided by the supervisee. Note the extent to which the supervisee was self-reflective, and how aware the supervisee was of any personal biases, values, or experiences that may have influenced their clinical work. Outline any comments and advice provided by the supervisor in relation to the supervisee’s experiences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Process of Supervision**: [Provide notes on the supervision process itself, including the supervisee’s attitude with feedback provided by the supervisor, the relationship with the supervisor, and how supervision is helping to enhance the supervisee’s practice. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[summary] Session Summary

[Summarise this supervision session in a single paragraph which balances the supervisee’s progress, and the input provided by the supervisor. You may look at such things as current work, strengths and areas of growth, assessment and management, interventions, progress towards goals/objectives, obstacles, and any other relevant aspect of the session.]

[nextsteps] Next Steps

* **Next Session**: [Outline the date and time of the next supervision session. Mention any other plans about the next session or any future session or meeting. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]
* **Assigned Homework**: [Describe any tasks or activities assigned to the supervisee. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

(Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

[summary] Session Summary

You may look at such things as current work, strengths and areas of growth, assessment and management, interventions, progress towards goals/objectives, obstacles, and any other aspect of the session that is particularly relevant to the supervisee.]

* **Assigned Homework**: [Describe any tasks or activities assigned to the supervisee. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)]

title: Supervision (Group)

prompt:
(Provide a detailed summary of a group supervision session between an experienced clinician, referred to as the supervisor, and two (or more) less experienced clinicians, referred to as the supervisees. In this group session, the supervisor is providing guidance and support to the supervisees, and the supervisees may also provide guidance and support to each other. Identify which speaker is the supervisor and which speakers are the supervisees. The group supervision session takes place in the context of a mental health setting. IMPORTANT: Group supervision is generally structured around supporting each supervisee to enhance their clinical skills, ensure ethical and effective practice, and grow in their professional and personal development. Organise your summary using the following headings and instructions, but exclude any headings and sections that are not explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note:)

Date: [Print the date of the group supervision session in the format: day number, full month name, year (e.g. 10 September 2025).]

Time: [Print the starting time of the group supervision meeting in the format HH:MM am/pm.]

Attendees: [Print the names of all attendees of the meeting, if known. Note which attendee is the supervisor. You do not have to identify who is Speaker A, Speaker B, etc.]

[case_discussions] Case Discussions

Provide details of the client being featured in the case presentation, including presenting problems, age, background, life circumstances, how long the client has been in treatment for, diagnosis, treatment plan, and interventions. [material_introduced] Material Introduced
