title: Therapeutic Letter for Client
description: This template creates a warm, affirming summary of a therapy session to give to a client. It can be used after each session to reinforce insights,
prompt:
(Use the template below to generate a therapeutic letter from a clinician to their client. Tailor the letter to reflect key content from the session/s, reinforce insights, validate progress, offer encouragement, note key aspects of therapy, and comment on homework or next steps. Write the letter in the clinician's voice, directly addressing the client by name, and use aspects of the transcript to mirror the clinician's style. Use a gentle, warm, affirming and professional tone that is hopeful. Write in a style that is engaging to the client, considering their presentation, age and other factors. Structure the letter in paragraphs and full sentences. Do not print out any title at the top of the output.)

Dear \[Client’s Name],

I wanted to take a moment to reflect on the work we’ve done together and acknowledge the progress you have made on your journey.

\[Name the difficulty and offer words of support and encouragement.]

\[Validate the client’s feelings and efforts in their journey.]

\[Highlight the client’s resilience and strengths (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note).]

\[Reflect on therapeutic insights, breakthroughs or progress, emotional or behavioural shifts (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note).]

\[Summarise important themes, coping strategies, or goals discussed in therapy (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note).]

Key Session Takeaways

* \[Include reminders of strategies, resources, or tools that the client found helpful (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note).]

\[Provide actionable steps or affirmations to guide future challenges (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual note).]

Sincerely,

\[Clinician’s Name]

(Below is an example of the format and style of the letter.)

**\[EXAMPLE START.]**

Dear \[Client’s Name],

I wanted to take a moment to reflect on the work we’ve done together and acknowledge your courage and dedication throughout this journey. You’ve faced \[specific challenge] with honesty and determination, and it’s been inspiring to see how you’ve \[specific progress or strength].

Remember the tools we’ve discussed—like \[specific strategy]—and how they’ve helped you navigate \[specific situation]. You’ve shown incredible resilience, and I believe in your ability to continue moving forward, even when things feel tough.

The key takeaways that I’d like you to remember from today’s session are:
\[List as many key points as needed, in a warm, affirming tone. Include any homework tasks]

Please keep this letter as a reminder of how far you’ve come and as a source of encouragement when you need it most.

Sincerely,

\[Clinician Name]

**\[EXAMPLE END.]**

title: Therapy Progress Note - Brief
description: This template is for ongoing psychological therapy or counselling, and aims to be a concise summary of a therapy session.
prompt:
(The following template is to be used following a therapy session between a clinician and a client. It will provide a concise summary of that session. Please use the following layout:)

[review_since_last_session] Review since last session

* [Describe the client’s current presentation, how things have been since last appointment, etc. Use as many dot points as needed (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Severity/Impact:** [Describe the current impact of the client's difficulties and symptoms on their functioning e.g., impact on work/school/activities of daily life, etc. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]

[history] Past Medical & Psychiatric History

* **Current Medications:** [List current medications, including details such as dose and frequency (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Recent Substance Use:** [Provide details of any recent substance use that may be impacting the client's mental health, including type, frequency, and duration (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]

[content] Session Content

* [Describe any goals that were discussed, including if any new objectives, desires or things to work on were discussed, or if existing goals were reviewed. When available, describe SMART goals and quote specific aims. Use as many bullet points as needed to capture key points discussed (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* [Describe any setbacks or obstacles to progress for therapy goals and objectives. Use as many bullet points as needed to capture key details discussed (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* [Describe details of other relevant discussions with client during the session, including life circumstances, milestones, relationships, interactions, or events important to the client's wellbeing that have either occurred or are upcoming. Use as many bullet points as needed to capture key details discussed (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* [List the client's key relationships and interactions (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]

[interventions] Interventions

* [Describe details of specific therapeutic techniques and interventions used or to be used. For example, if any techniques from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Cognitive-Behavioural therapy, psychodynamic psychotherapy, interpersonal therapy, Schema Therapy, Dialectical Behaviour Therapy are used, specify the skills introduced or the therapeutic themes of the session. Use as many bullet points as needed to capture the key details discussed (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* [Describe any ideas, concepts or frameworks that were introduced, or the content of any handouts, or use of whiteboard, or the use of any other clinical resources. Use as many bullet points as needed to capture all the details discussed (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]

[risk] Risk Assessment and Management

* **Suicidal Ideation:** [Describe in detail any history of suicidal ideation, attempts, and plans (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Homicidal Ideation:** [Describe any homicidal ideation (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank).]
* **Self-harm:** [Detail any history of self-harm (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Violence & Aggression:** [Describe any recent or past incidents of violence or aggression (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Addictive Behaviours:** [Detail any recent or past addictive behaviours, e.g., illicit drugs, gambling, etc. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Risk-taking/Impulsivity:** [Describe any risk-taking behaviours or impulsivity (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Management Plan:** [If applicable, describe any strategies or steps taken to manage suicidal ideation, homicidal ideation, self-harm, violence and aggression, substance use, or risk-taking behaviour (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]

[nextsteps] Next Steps

* **Next Session:** [Print the date and time of the next session, describe any plans about the next session (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]
* **Assigned Homework:** [Describe any tasks or activities assigned to the client (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, or clinical note, otherwise exclude section).]

title: Trauma-Focused CBT
description: For clinicians working with victims of trauma, this template provides a summary of a therapy session conducted using a trauma-focused CBT framework.
prompt:
(The template below is designed to be used by clinicians working with victims of trauma. The clinicians are using a trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy framework. After analysing the transcript from a session between a clinician and a client, you will generate a detailed summary of the session from a trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy perspective. Include detailed information that is considered appropriate and necessary in the context of the evidence-based approach of trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy. Use terms and language that are consistent with the evidence-based approach of trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy. Organise the summary using the following layout:)



[presentation] Presenting Problem(s)

\[Provide a brief summary of trauma-related symptoms and issues discussed in session. Describe what aspect of the trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy treatment phase the client is currently in (e.g., Psychoeducation, Relaxation, Affect Modulation, Cognitive Processing, Trauma Narrative, Cognitive Restructuring, In Vivo Exposure, Enhancing Safety).]

\[Summarise other concerns or difficulties discussed in the session. Highlight any relevant updates to symptoms, behaviours, or situations since the previous session. Detail the client’s current emotional state, and indicate the type and results of any assessments used by the clinician in session, e.g. Subjective Units of Distress, or SUDS; Depression Anxiety Stress Scale, or DASS; and others. Detail any relevant life events or current circumstances contributing to the client’s presentation.]

[content] Session Content

\[Provide a concise, detailed summary of the session. Include information related to the client’s experience of trauma, how their daily functioning is impacted by the trauma, and the client’s efforts at managing their emotional state in response to the trauma. Include any review of homework and the client's progress with tasks from the previous session, including barriers and successes. Include key topics discussed, core issues explored and how they relate to the client’s presentation, and treatment goals.]

[cbt_formulation] CBT Formulation

\[Below is a five-part model: Situation, Thoughts, Emotions, Physical Sensations, Behaviours. For each event, situation, or circumstance discussed in session with the client, use this CBT model to describe the cycle of thoughts impacting on emotions, physical sensations, and behaviours. Repeat the headings for each event or circumstance.]

**Situation:** \[Describe the triggering event, situation, or circumstances that started a cycle.]

**Thoughts:** \[Describe the negative or unhelpful thoughts that occurred in response to the event, situation, or circumstances.]

**Emotions:** \[Describe the emotions or feelings that are generated by the negative or unhelpful thoughts.]

**Physical Sensations:** \[Describe the physiological reactions that occurred in response to these thoughts and emotions e.g., heart palpitations, stomach upset, tension, fatigue, etc. (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

**Behaviours:** \[Describe the behaviours, actions, or reactions that result from these thoughts, emotions, and physical sensations. Note any feedback loops that cause further difficult situations.]

[unhelpful_thinking_styles] Unhelpful Thinking Styles

* \[Describe any thoughts the client had that are associated with cognitive distortions. If helpful, use direct quotes from the transcript. Note if the clinician identified or challenged these thoughts in the transcript or not. Use as many dot points as you need to detail thoughts or quotes and their associated unhelpful thinking style.]

[core_beliefs] Core Beliefs

* \[Describe any core beliefs, schemas or foundational beliefs, and any associated experiences, that were explored in the transcript. Use as many dot points as you need to detail core beliefs. If helpful, use direct quotes from the transcript (only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank).]

[interventions_used] Interventions Used

\[Describe the specific trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy therapeutic interventions used by the clinician in the session (e.g., psychoeducation, cognitive restructuring, affect regulation, exposure, processing the trauma narrative, and any other trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy intervention). Summarise how these interventions were received by the client and any feedback from the client, including level of understanding of the interventions shown by the client. Detail any notable shifts in thinking or behaviour expressed by the client.]

[key_insights_and_client_responses] Key Insights and Client Responses

\[Describe the client’s emotional and behavioural responses; including the client’s affect, engagement, level of distress, or resistance during the session.]

\[Describe any cognitive shifts observed in the client; including changes in perspective, increased insight, increased understanding of their difficulties or progress toward therapy goals, or cognitive reframing.]

\[Describe the client’s ability to use learned coping skills or techniques outside of therapy.]

\[Highlight any other important themes that emerged during the session.]

[therapeutic_relationship] Therapeutic Relationship

\[Briefly comment on the therapeutic alliance or dynamics observed in the session. Note any areas where rapport was strengthened or challenges arose.]

[homework_or_action_plan] Homework or Action Plan

\[Describe homework or tasks assigned to the client, including rationale and expected outcomes. Note any feedback or concerns from the client about the homework.]

[risk] Risk Assessment

\[Summarise any risk factors, such as self-harm or suicidal ideation. Identify protective factors that mitigate the risk, and describe any action plans if risk is present.]

[trauma_informed_summary] Trauma-Informed Summary

\[Provide a summary in no more than six sentences on the client’s trauma symptoms (e.g., changes in re-experiencing, avoidance, hyperarousal, or negative mood/cognition), the client’s functional impairment (e.g., any changes in sleep, appetite, concentration, or social/occupational functioning), and the client’s overall progress (e.g., movement toward treatment goals, improvements in distress tolerance, or areas needing further work).]

[nextsteps] Next Steps

\[Outline the preliminary agenda or goals for the next session, based on today's session. Outline any activities or tasks that will be completed by either the clinician or the client before the next session. Provide the time, day and date of any future session agreed upon between the clinician and the client.]

title: Treatment Summary Report - Third Party Request
description: A concise report providing an overview of a client/patient's treatment, intended for use with ongoing or past clients/patients, where a report has
prompt:
(Please write a concise, objective report providing an overview of a client/patient’s treatment. The report is to be provided to a third party who has requested information about a client/patient’s treatment. The report should not include any inferences, interpretations, or subjective assessments. Only provide details about treatment sessions, interventions used, progress made, and any documented statements or actions from the client. Ensure that all information presented is directly observable or stated by the client, with no speculative or interpretive language. Use the name of the client throughout the report. Use the below headings as a structure. Do not print out any title at the top of the output.)
Re: [Client’s Full Name]; DOB [Client’s Date of Birth.] Dear [Recipient name (using appropriate title if applicable).] (If the recipient’s name is not stated, just write "Recipient Name".), [author_details] Author Details I am [Record the name and qualifications of the clinician, and the purpose or reason for their involvement with the client.] I am writing in response to your request for information regarding [Client’s Full Name]’s treatment. [information_sources] Information Sources [List all sources of information used to compile this report, including: clinical records, session notes, assessment results, and dates of treatment. Specify the time period covered by this report.] [purpose_of_treatment_report] Purpose of Treatment Report This report provides a summary of [Detail the reason for the request of the treatment report and who the report has been requested by. (Only include if mentioned in the client’s notes, transcripts, and contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] This report is prepared from the perspective of a treating clinician and not as a forensic expert [Make it clear that the author is the treating clinician, not a forensic expert. Outline the subsequent scope and limitations of this report - for example, that the clinician can only provide information relevant to the client’s treatment and has not conducted any investigations outside the scope of treatment.] [presentation] Presentation [Provide a concise statement regarding the reason the client sought or was referred for treatment, as documented in the initial assessment. Include the date treatment commenced, the presenting concerns, and the referral source if applicable. (Only include information that is explicitly mentioned in the client’s notes, transcripts, and contextual notes.)] [Using as many paragraphs as needed, provide a concise summary of the client’s background, initial presenting issues, and initial goals for treatment. Using the client’s session notes, transcripts, and contextual notes, include information such as: the client’s initial presenting issues; other key client information such as circumstances, background, diagnoses and medical history, occupation, current living arrangements, and social support; trauma history; relevant people involved in the client’s life; and the client’s goals for treatment. If applicable, describe how any of this information significantly changed over the course of treatment. State the source of any information provided, for example by saying “the client reported that”. (Only include information that is explicitly mentioned in the client’s notes, transcripts, and contextual notes.)] [psychometric_assessments] Psychometric Assessments The following psychometric assessments have been completed: [Using bullet points, list the name of each psychometric assessment. For each assessment, you must include: 1) Assessment name, 2) One sentence description explaining what the tool measures 3) Results summary, and 4) Quantitative results (scores, percentiles, clinical ranges). If the same assessment has been administered on more than one occasion, only list the most recent and include any change in symptoms over time. (Only include information that is explicitly in the client’s psychometric assessments results, transcript(s), notes(s) or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank. If no psychometric assessments were administered, do not include the Psychometric Assessment section.)] [treatment] Treatment [Using as many paragraphs as needed, provide a concise, objective summary of the treatment provided by the clinician. Using the client’s session notes, transcripts, and contextual notes, include information such as: date of first and last session; frequency of sessions; total number of sessions completed to date; treatment approaches used (e.g., CBT, ACT, schema therapy, psychodynamic psychotherapy); types of interventions used; types of homework or other activities completed as part of treatment; and the client’s engagement in treatment. Only include information that is explicitly mentioned in the client’s notes, transcripts, and contextual notes.] [Provide an objective summary of the client's progress toward treatment goals, as documented in session notes, contextual notes and transcripts. Include quantitative measures or psychometric assessment results to support this, where available (e.g., reductions in symptom measures, functional improvements). Note any significant events or changes during the course of treatment that impacted progress (e.g., medication changes, life events, treatment interruptions). Document any periods of stability and/or symptom exacerbation, as recorded in session notes. (Only include information that is explicitly in the client’s psychometric assessments results, transcript(s), notes(s) or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] [Describe the client's most recent clinical presentation, as documented in the latest session notes. Include information about current symptoms, functional status, and any ongoing concerns. Document the client's current treatment status (e.g., active treatment, maintenance phase, discharged). If treatment has concluded, provide the date of termination and the reason (e.g., goals achieved, client decision, referral elsewhere). (Only include information that is explicitly in the client’s psychometric assessments results, transcript(s), notes(s) or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] [If treatment is ongoing, summarise the current treatment plan as documented in the most recent treatment plan update. Include current goals, planned interventions, and anticipated duration. If treatment has concluded, summarise any aftercare recommendations or referrals that were provided.] (Only include if applicable and explicitly documented in the transcripts, notes or contextual notes, otherwise leave blank.)] [nextsteps] Recommendations [Only offer recommendations related to treatment, such as ongoing therapy or referrals to other professionals, such as engaging an independent psychologist for assessment and opinion if a request falls beyond the scope of the clinician.] If you require additional information or have any questions about the contents of this report, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, [Clinician Name] [Clinician Profession] [Practice Name]

title: WorkCover Queensland Comprehensive Treatment Report
description: Mental health practitioners working in the Queensland workers' compensation system may be requested by the insurer to provide a Comprehensive
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist mental health practitioners working within the Queensland workers' compensation system, also known as WorkCover Queensland, in preparing a Comprehensive Treatment Report. This report is used when the insurer requires detailed information about a worker's work-related psychological injury or condition, typically in complex cases. The report must be written in clear, plain English that can be understood by decision-makers who may not have a medical or allied health background. Avoid complex clinical jargon where possible, and explain technical terms in simple language. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, session notes, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. You must strictly adhere to the following format:) [comprehensive_treatment_report] Comprehensive Treatment Report **Client:** [Print the client's first and last name. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Date of Birth:** [Print the client's date of birth in the format DD/MM/YYYY. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Claim Number:** [Print the client's WorkCover Queensland claim number. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Date of Injury:** [Print the date of the client's workplace injury in the format DD/MM/YYYY. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Diagnosis:** [Print the client's DSM-5 diagnosis including the diagnostic code and severity level if applicable (e.g., Major Depressive Disorder, Moderate 296.32). If the client has more than one diagnosis related to their workplace injury, list all relevant diagnoses. If unknown, print "Unknown."] [history_of_work_related_injury] History of Work-Related Injury [Describe the history and mechanism of the accepted work-related injury. Explain what happened at work that led to the psychological injury. Include when the injury occurred or developed, and describe the specific workplace events, circumstances, or conditions that contributed to the injury. Explain how the injury has developed or progressed over time. Write this in plain language that a non-clinician can understand. Provide your response in less than five sentences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] [summary_of_treatment_provided] Summary of Treatment Provided [Provide a summary of all psychological treatment and rehabilitation that the clinician has provided to the client to date. Include the therapeutic approach used (e.g., cognitive behaviour therapy, trauma-focused therapy), when treatment commenced, the total number of sessions provided, and the frequency of sessions (e.g., weekly, fortnightly). Describe the main focus and goals of the treatment as it relates to the client's work-related psychological injury. Provide your response in less than five sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] [progress_summary] Progress Summary [Describe the client's progress in treatment to date. Explain how the client's symptoms and functioning have changed since treatment began. Focus on observable, measurable improvements in plain language. Describe improvements in the client's ability to manage their symptoms, engage in daily activities, maintain relationships, or function at home, in the community, and at work. Provide your response in less than five sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] [scales] Outcome Measures [List up to three psychometric assessments used to measure outcomes and to demonstrate progress to date. For each assessment administered, provide the name of the assessment, the initial administration date and scores, the most recent administration date and scores, and a brief interpretation of the results in plain language. Use an ordered list to display this information, just like the following example:
1. **Depression Anxiety Stress Scale (DASS-42)**; Initial administration date: 15/03/2025; Initial scores: Depression 10 (80th percentile) (Mild), Anxiety 10 (89th percentile) (Mild), Stress 16 (83rd percentile) (Moderate), Total Distress 36 (83rd percentile) (Mild); Most recent administration date: 10/01/2026; Most recent scores: Depression 5 (50th percentile) (Normal), Anxiety 4 (51st percentile) (Normal), Stress 7 (38th percentile) (Normal), Total Distress 16 (45th percentile) (Normal); The change in DASS-21 scores indicates that the client's psychological functioning has improved ... etc. 2. **PTSD Checklist for DSM-5 (PCL-5)**; Initial administration date: 05/06/2025; Initial scores: Re-Experiencing 4 (Normal), Avoidance 5 (Severe), Negative Alterations 9 (Mild), Hyper-Arousal 10 (Moderate), Total 28 (76th percentile) (Mild); Most recent administration date: 10/01/2026; Most recent scores: Re-Experiencing 3 (Normal), Avoidance 2 (Normal), Negative Alterations 6 (Normal), Hyper-Arousal 5 (Normal), Total 16 (48th percentile) (Normal); The change in PCL-5 scores indicates that the client's trauma symptoms have stabilised ... etc. 3. **Trauma Recovery Measure (TRM)**; Initial administration date: 19/08/2025; Initial scores: Validation 21 (65th percentile) (Middle), Liberation 13 (83rd percentile) (Middle), Positive Self 22 (62nd percentile) (Middle), Total 56 (75th percentile) (Middle); Most recent administration date: 10/01/2026; Most recent scores: Validation 26 (87th percentile) (Late), Liberation 14 (90th percentile) (Late), Positive Self 25 (80th percentile) (Middle), Total 65 (89th percentile) (Late); The change in TRM scores indicates that the client has moved from the middle to the late stages of trauma recovery, indicating improvement in functioning ... etc.] [work_status_and_improved_function] Work Status and Improved Function [Describe the client's current work status and how their functional capacity has improved. Explain whether the client has returned to work (full duties, modified duties, or graduated return), is off work, or has capacity to return to work. Describe improvements in the client's functional capacity at home and/or work (e.g., communication, self-care, social engagement, administrative responsibilities). Provide your response in less than five sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] [self_management] Self-Management [Describe how treatment is assisting the client to self-manage their injury. Explain what strategies, techniques, or skills the client is now using independently to manage their symptoms (e.g., relaxation techniques, thought challenging, activity scheduling). Describe the client's level of understanding of their condition and their engagement with self-management strategies. Provide your response in less than five sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] [direction_of_future_treatment] Direction of Future Treatment **Planned Interventions:** [Describe the planned psychological interventions for future treatment. Specify the therapeutic approach that will be used and explain what this will involve in plain language. Describe the focus of future treatment and how it will address the client's ongoing needs related to their work-related psychological injury. Provide your response in less than three sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Number and Frequency of Treatment Sessions:** [Specify the recommended number of treatment sessions and the frequency of sessions (e.g., 8 sessions, fortnightly; 12 sessions, weekly). If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Expected Duration of Rehabilitation Plan:** [Provide the expected timeframe for the rehabilitation plan (e.g., 3 months, 6 months). Include an anticipated end date for treatment if discussed. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Functional Goals:** [Outline the functional goals for future treatment using the SMART framework (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Timed). List goals that focus on what the client will be able to do. Goals should relate to functioning in daily life, work capacity, or other meaningful areas. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Return to Work Alignment:** [Describe how the above functional goals will assist the client in returning to work (where appropriate). Explain how achieving these goals will support the client's capacity to perform work tasks, manage workplace stress, or successfully reintegrate into the workplace. Provide your response in less than three sentences. If return to work is not relevant or if unknown, print "Unknown."] **Anticipated Self-Management:** [Describe when the clinician anticipates the client will be able to self-manage their condition without ongoing professional support. Provide a specific timeframe if discussed (e.g., "within 4-6 months", "by June 2026"). If unknown, print "Unknown."] [other_contributing_factors] Other Contributing Factors [In the clinician's opinion, describe any other factors contributing to the current psychological injury beyond the accepted work-related injury. These may include pre-existing mental health conditions, non-work stressors (e.g., family issues, financial stress, physical health problems), personality factors, or other life circumstances. Explain how these factors interact with or impact the work-related psychological injury. Write in plain language. Provide your response in less than five sentences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] [barriers_to_rehabilitation_progress_and_] Barriers to Rehabilitation Progress and Return to Work **Identified Barriers:** [Identify any barriers to the client's rehabilitation progress and/or return to work. These barriers may include psychological factors (e.g., ongoing symptoms, fear of returning to work), practical factors (e.g., lack of suitable duties, unresolved workplace issues), yellow flags (psychosocial risk factors), ergonomic issues, work-related issues (e.g., conflict with supervisor, workplace culture), or other relevant factors. Provide your response in less than five sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Suggestions to Overcome Barriers:** [For each barrier identified above, provide specific, practical suggestions to overcome these barriers. Frame recommendations in a solution-focused way that identifies actions that could be taken by the client, the clinician, the employer, WorkCover Queensland, or other parties. These may include continued psychological treatment, workplace modifications, mediation, referrals to other services, or other strategies. Provide your response in less than five sentences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] If you require any further information, please do not hesitate to contact me. Yours sincerely, [Clinician first and last name.]

sections.label: Barriers to Rehabilitation Progress and Return to Work

title: WorkCover Queensland Provider Management Plan (PMP) – Form 032
description: Mental health practitioners working in the Queensland workers' compensation system, also known as WorkSafe, are required to complete and submit a
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist mental health practitioners working within the Queensland workers' compensation system, which is regulated by WorkCover Queensland, also known as WorkSafe Queensland. The template follows the format of the Provider Management Plan form (Q-COMP 032 v5), which clinicians must complete and submit to the insurer after the initial approved or pre-approved consultations have been provided, and before additional treatment can commence. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. You must strictly adhere to the following format:) [workcover_queensland_provider_management] WorkCover Queensland Provider Management Plan #### [worker_s_details] Worker's details Name: [Print the client's first and last name. If unknown, print "Unknown."] DOB: [Print the client's date of birth in the format DD/MM/YYYY. If unknown, print "Unknown."] DOI: [Print the date of the client's workplace injury in the format DD/MM/YYYY. If unknown, print "Unknown."] Diagnosis: [Print the client's DSM-5 diagnosis including the diagnostic code. If multiple diagnoses are relevant to the compensable injury, list them all. If unknown, print "Unknown."] Claim number: [Print the client's WorkCover Queensland claim number. If unknown, print "Unknown."] Referring doctor: [Print the title, first name and last name of the doctor who referred this client to the clinician. If unknown, print "Unknown."] Worker's occupation: [Print the client's occupation or job role at the time of injury. If unknown, print "Unknown."] This is provider management plan number: [Print the number of this provider management plan. It will be one number greater than the most recently submitted Provider Management Plan for this client. If this is the first Provider Management Plan for this client, print "1". If unknown, print "Unknown."] Date of initial consultation for present injury: [Print the date that the clinician provided initial treatment to the client for this workplace injury in the format DD/MM/YYYY. If unknown, print "Unknown."] Total consultations for this injury approved to date: [Print the total number of consultations that have been approved by the insurer for this client to date, including all pre-approved sessions and any sessions approved in previous Provider Management Plans. If unknown, print "Unknown."] No. consultations required in this plan: [Print the number of additional consultations that the clinician is requesting approval for in this Provider Management Plan. If unknown, print "Unknown."] [nextsteps] Treatment plan [Provide a clear description of the treatment approach being used with this client. Include the evidence-based therapeutic modality or framework being employed (e.g., cognitive behaviour therapy, acceptance and commitment therapy, EMDR, motivational interviewing, trauma-informed therapy, etc.). Describe the specific focus of treatment as it relates to the client's compensable psychological injury. Outline the treatment goals that have been collaboratively developed with the client, ensuring they are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and timed (SMART goals). Describe how the treatment addresses the client's current symptoms and functional limitations related to their workplace injury. Include information about the frequency and duration of sessions being requested (e.g., weekly sessions for 6 weeks, fortnightly sessions for 8 weeks). Provide the clinical rationale for the requested number of sessions, referencing the client's progress to date, current symptom severity, complexity of presentation, and anticipated trajectory of recovery. Describe any therapeutic homework, between-session tasks, or self-management strategies the client is engaging in. Include information about return to work planning and how treatment supports the client's capacity to return to work or maintain employment. Provide your response in paragraph form using less than six sentences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the transcript, contextual notes, added files, or clinical note, otherwise leave blank.)] [type_and_number_of_consultations_being_p] Type and number of consultations being provided TOC Item No.: [If unknown, print "Unknown."] No. of services or costs: [If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Outcome measures used to assess and monitor worker's progress throughout treatment period** Outcome measures:
* [List the names of up to three standardised outcome measures that have been administered to the client.]
Measures at initial assessment:
* [For each of the outcome measures listed above, print the scores obtained at the initial assessment of these outcome measures, along with the date the measures were administered in the format DD/MM/YYYY.] Current measures at beginning of plan:
* [For each of the outcome measures listed above, print the most recent score obtained, along with the date the measures were administered in the format DD/MM/YYYY.] Anticipated outcomes at end of current plan:
* [For each of the outcome measures listed above, describe the anticipated or expected score at the completion of the treatment period being requested in this Provider Management Plan. Base this on the client's rate of progress, treatment goals, and clinical improvement trajectory discussed in transcripts or documents. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Identified barriers to return to work and recommended strategies to overcome barriers** Barriers:
* [List any factors or barriers that are impeding the client's recovery and return to work. These may include psychological factors (e.g., persistent symptoms, fear of returning to work, avoidance behaviours), practical factors (e.g., workplace issues, lack of suitable duties, transport difficulties), social factors (e.g., lack of family support, financial stress), or other relevant factors mentioned in the transcripts or documents. If no barriers have been identified, print "Unknown."] Recommended strategies:
* [For each barrier identified above, list the strategies that have been discussed or recommended to address or overcome these barriers. These may include therapeutic interventions, workplace modifications, referrals to other services, communication with the employer, graduated return to work planning, or other strategies mentioned in the transcripts or documents. Provide your response using dot points that correspond to each barrier listed. If no strategies have been discussed, print "Unknown."] Other: [Describe any other relevant clinical information for this client that has not been detailed elsewhere in this Provider Management Plan and that may assist the insurer in understanding the client's progress, treatment needs, or return to work planning. This may include significant changes in the client's circumstances, relevant psychosocial factors, the client's engagement and motivation in treatment, response to interventions, or other pertinent information mentioned in the transcripts or documents. If there is no additional information to report, print "Unknown."]

sections.label: Type and number of consultations being provided

title: WorkCover Queensland Psychological Treatment Progress Report
description: Mental health practitioners working in the Queensland workers' compensation system may be requested by the insurer to provide a Psychological
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist mental health practitioners working within the Queensland workers' compensation system in preparing a Psychological Treatment Progress Report. This report type is used to communicate relevant information about a worker's work-related psychological injury or condition to WorkCover Queensland, also known as WorkSafe Queensland. The report must be written in clear, plain English that can be understood by decision-makers who may not have a medical or allied health background. Avoid complex clinical jargon where possible, and explain technical terms in simple language. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, session notes, contextual notes, added files, and psychometric test results. You must strictly adhere to the following format:) #### **Psychological Treatment Progress Report** **Client:** [Print the client's first and last name. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Date of Birth:** [Print the client's date of birth in the format DD/MM/YYYY. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Claim Number:** [Print the client's WorkCover Queensland claim number. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Diagnosis:** [Print the client's DSM-5 diagnosis including the diagnostic code and severity level if applicable (e.g., Major Depressive Disorder, Moderate 296.32). If the client has more than one diagnosis related to their workplace injury, list all relevant diagnoses. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Impact of Diagnosis on Functional Capacity** [Describe how the client’s current psychological condition affects their functional capacity at home and/or work (e.g., communication, self-care, social engagement, administrative responsibilities, etc.). Provide your response in less than three sentences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] **Return to Work Capacity** [Outline if the clinician believes the client now has capacity to commence a gradual return to work at their substantive workplace or an alternative location. If known, provide a clear yes or no answer, followed by specific recommendations in relation to their return to work. If yes, describe what type of work capacity they have (e.g., modified duties, reduced hours, specific restrictions). If no, explain what needs to change before they would have this capacity. If known, describe the clinician’s recommended approach to return to work, including any graduated plan, timeframes, workplace modifications needed, or suitable duties. Focus on what the client CAN do. Provide your response in less than five sentences. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] **Barriers to Return to Work** [Outline the barriers (psychological, practical, workplace-related, or other factors) that would need to be overcome to support the client's return to work. Describe the clinician’s recommendations for overcoming these barriers? Frame recommendations in a solution-focused way that identifies actions that could be taken by the client, the clinician, the employer, or other parties. Provide your response in less than six sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Treatment Provided** [Briefly describe the psychological therapy or intervention being delivered. Name the therapeutic approach in plain language (e.g., cognitive behaviour therapy, trauma-focused therapy) and explain in simple terms what this involves. Include how many sessions have been provided to date and the frequency. Describe the main focus of treatment as it relates to the client's work-related psychological injury. Provide your response in less than four sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] #### Outcomes Achieved **Measurable Outcomes:**
[List up to three psychometric assessments used to measure outcomes and to demonstrate progress to date. For each assessment administered, provide the name of the assessment, the initial administration date and scores, the most recent administration date and scores, and a brief interpretation of the results in plain language. Use an ordered list to display this information, just like the following example:
1. **Depression Anxiety Stress Scale (DASS-21)**; Initial administration date: 15/03/2025; Initial scores: Depression 10 (80th percentile) (Mild), Anxiety 10 (89th percentile) (Mild), Stress 16 (83rd percentile) (Moderate), Total Distress 36 (83rd percentile) (Mild); Most recent administration date: 10/01/2026; Most recent scores: Depression 5 (50th percentile) (Normal), Anxiety 4 (51st percentile) (Normal), Stress 7 (38th percentile) (Normal), Total Distress 16 (45th percentile) (Normal); The change in DASS-21 scores indicates that the client's psychological functioning has improved ... etc. 2. **PTSD Checklist for DSM-5 (PCL-5)**; Initial administration date: 05/06/2025; Initial scores: Re-Experiencing 4 (Normal), Avoidance 5 (Severe), Negative Alterations 9 (Mild), Hyper-Arousal 10 (Moderate), Total 28 (76th percentile) (Mild); Most recent administration date: 10/01/2026; Most recent scores: Re-Experiencing 3 (Normal), Avoidance 2 (Normal), Negative Alterations 6 (Normal), Hyper-Arousal 5 (Normal), Total 16 (48th percentile) (Normal); The change in PCL-5 scores indicates that the client's trauma symptoms have stabilised ... etc. 3. **Trauma Recovery Measure (TRM)**; Initial administration date: 19/08/2025; Initial scores: Validation 21 (65th percentile) (Middle), Liberation 13 (83rd percentile) (Middle), Positive Self 22 (62nd percentile) (Middle), Total 56 (75th percentile) (Middle); Most recent administration date: 10/01/2026; Most recent scores: Validation 26 (87th percentile) (Late), Liberation 14 (90th percentile) (Late), Positive Self 25 (80th percentile) (Middle), Total 65 (89th percentile) (Late); The change in TRM scores indicates that the client has moved from the middle to the late stages of trauma recovery, indicating improvement in functioning ... etc.] **Return to Work Support:** [Outline how the provided treatment supports the client's return to work (RTW) goals. Describe how the psychological interventions have specifically helped the client move closer to being able to return to work. This may include building coping strategies, addressing workplace fears, improving confidence, or working on specific workplace-related difficulties. Provide your response in less than three sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown."] #### Progress Toward Goals **Current Functional Goals:** [Outline the worker's current functional goals using the SMART framework (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Timed). Describe goals that have been collaboratively developed with the client and focus on what the client can do or aims to be able to do. Goals should relate to functioning in daily life, work capacity, or other meaningful areas. If you cannot locate any goals in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, print "Unknown."] **Progress and Support:** [Outline how psychological treatment has supported progress toward these goals. For each goal, describe what progress has been made and what the client is now able to do. Explain what specific treatment strategies or interventions have helped the client make this progress. Provide your response referring to each goal mentioned above. If unknown, print "Unknown."] **Self-Management:** [Describe a timeframe for when the clinician anticipates the worker will be able to self-manage their condition. Describe the client's current ability to manage their psychological condition independently. Explain what strategies or skills the client is using to manage symptoms. Provide your response in less than three sentences. If unknown, print "Unknown.")] #### Recommendations **Self-Management:** [As described by the clinician, provide clear, evidence-based recommendations to support the worker's ongoing self-management of the condition. Specify whether the clinician believes that ongoing psychological treatment is recommended. If yes, state the type of treatment, recommended frequency (e.g., fortnightly), anticipated number of sessions, and timeframe. Explain the clinician’s rationale for ongoing treatment in plain language. If no further treatment is recommended, state this clearly and explain why. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] **Return to Work:** [As described by the clinician, provide specific, practical recommendations to support the worker’s return to work. Include proposed treatments, workplace modifications, graduated return to work plans, suitable duties, or other strategies. Provide timeframes where applicable. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise print "Unknown.")] **Other Recommendations:** [If applicable, provide any other evidence-based recommendations provided by the clinician not already mentioned that would support the client's recovery. This may include referrals to other services, lifestyle strategies, or personal relationships. (Only include if explicitly mentioned in the session notes, contextual notes, added files, or transcripts, otherwise leave blank.)] If you require any further information, please do not hesitate to contact me. Yours sincerely, [Clinician first and last name.]

title: WorkSafe Victoria – Psychology Treatment Planning Form (PS604)
description: Psychologists working in the workers compensation system in Victoria (WorkSafe / WorkCover), Australia, are required to complete a Psychology
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist psychologists working within the WorkSafe Victoria workers compensation system in Australia. It follows the format of the Psychology Treatment Planning Form (PS604) which psychologists must complete close to the commencement of treatment with a client. Psychologists then must submit this form to the insurance company that is managing the client’s case. Please use a formal and professional tone in your output. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, contextual notes, and psychometric test results. You must strictly adhere to the following format:)

### Psychology Treatment Planning Form (PS604)

#### Details

Worker’s Name: \[Client’s First and Last Name.]
Claim Number: \[Client’s Claim Number.]
Occupation / Job Title: \[Client’s Occupation or Job Title.]
General Practitioner’s Name: \[GP’s First and Last Name.]
Referrer’s Name: \[Referrer’s First and Last Name (if different from the General Practitioner).]
Date of Referral: \[Date of Referral.]
Date of First Session: \[Date of First Session between the psychologist and client.]

#### 1 Your DSM diagnosis (or provisional diagnosis) with respect to the claimable injury, using the latest published version of the DSM.

\[Describe the psychologist’s and / or General Practitioner’s diagnosis according to criteria set out in the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders – 5th Edition – Text Revision, also known as the DSM-5-TR. Print out the relevant diagnostic codes from the DSM-5-TR for any diagnosis given. Include the level of severity of any diagnosis, and any clinically significant co-morbidities.]

#### 2 With respect to the claimable injury, describe the psychological and physical symptoms the worker is currently presenting.

\[Describe the psychological and physical symptoms the client is currently experiencing in relation to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

#### 3 Describe the impact of these symptoms on the following areas:

#### 3.1 Home life

\[Describe the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on their home life, including any issues with family, ability to perform domestic duties, or any other aspect of living at home. If applicable, refer to the client’s ability with these things prior to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 3.2 Social engagement

\[Describe the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on their level of social engagement. If applicable, refer to the client’s level of social engagement prior to their workplace injury. For example, is the client able to socialise effectively, is the client engaging in social isolation, and how does the client’s current level of social engagement compare to their level of social engagement prior to the workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 3.3 Emotional wellbeing / quality of life

\[Describe the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on their emotional wellbeing and quality of life. Describe how the workplace injury has impacted on the client’s mental health. If applicable, refer to the client’s wellbeing and quality of life prior to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 3.4 Readiness to return to work or progress with the support of occupational rehabilitation services

\[Describe the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on the client’s readiness to return to work or on the client’s progress with the support of occupational rehabilitation services. Describe if the client is ready, or will be ready in the near future, to return to work or engage in occupational rehabilitation services. Describe the overall impact on the client’s mental health and how this may prevent the client from currently participating in employment or any occupational rehabilitation services. Make an explicit statement about the client's current capacity to engage in employment or occupational rehabilitation services. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 3.5 Other

\[Describe the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on any other aspect of their lives not captured by 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, or 3.4 above (e.g., ability to drive, ability to engage in community activities, or ability to perform any other duty or function. If applicable, refer to the client’s ability with these things prior to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 4 Details of any other psychological or physical conditions

\[If applicable, describe any other psychological or physical conditions or diagnoses that are not directly related to the workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Considerations for treatment and additional support

\[If applicable, describe any suggestions or recommendations for other treatment, additional support, or other referrals that may address these other psychological or physical conditions or diagnoses that are not directly related to the workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 5 Details of any factors that could enhance recovery (personal strengths or situational factors)

\[Describe any factors that could enhance the client’s recovery (e.g., personal strengths and characteristics, situational factors, family support, etc.). Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 6 Details of any factors that may impede recovery

\[If applicable, describe any factors that may impede the client’s recovery (e.g., lack of social and family support, transport difficulties, family issues, addictions, issues with the client’s employer, etc.). Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Considerations for treatment and additional support

\[If applicable, describe any potential treatment or additional support that may assist the client to deal with any factors impeding their recovery (e.g., mediation, referrals to other specialists or health organisations, support from community organisations, etc.). Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 7 Goals and treatment

#### 7.1 Home life

\[If applicable, describe the client’s goals for improving their home life and domestic situation. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Treatment modality / interventions / strategies

\[If applicable, describe the evidence-based treatment modalities, interventions, and strategies to help the client reach their goals for improving home life. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 7.2 Social engagement

\[If applicable, describe the client’s goals for improving their social engagement. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Treatment modality / interventions / strategies

\[If applicable, describe the evidence-based treatment modalities, interventions, and strategies to help the client reach their goals for improving social engagement. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 7.3 Emotional wellbeing / quality of life

\[If applicable, describe the client’s goals for improving their wellbeing and quality of life. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Treatment modality / interventions / strategies

\[If applicable, describe the evidence-based treatment modalities, interventions, and strategies to help the client reach their goals for improving their wellbeing and quality of life. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 7.4 Developing work / training capacity

\[Describe the client’s goals for developing and improving their work and training capacity. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Treatment modality / interventions / strategies

\[Describe the evidence-based treatment modalities, interventions, and strategies to help the client reach their goals for developing and improving their work and training capacity. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 7.5 Other

\[If applicable, describe any other goals that the client has from treatment. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### Treatment modality / interventions / strategies

\[If applicable, describe the evidence-based treatment modalities, interventions, and strategies to help the client reach any other goals they have from treatment. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than two sentences.]

#### 8 Outcome measures – including individual goal attainment scales and validated psychometrics, results, and interpretation.

* \[List the name, date of completion, results, and brief interpretation of psychometric testing undertaken by the client.]

#### 9 Anticipated treatment frequency and delivery mode

#### 9.1 How many sessions do you anticipate are needed within the next 3-6 months?

\[Describe how many sessions the psychologist believes are needed for the client within the next three to six months in order to assist the client to recover from the workplace injury. Provide your response in one sentence.]

#### 9.2 What frequency?

\[Describe the frequency of sessions that the psychologist believes are needed for the client within the next three to six months in order to assist the client to recover from the workplace injury. Provide your response in one sentence.]

#### 9.3 Face to face or telehealth?

\[Describe whether the psychologist plans on seeing the client by face to face or telehealth for their sessions. Provide your response in one sentence.]

#### 9.4 Provide a clinical rationale for the treatment frequency and modality?

\[Describe the psychologist’s rationale for the suggested frequency and modality in order to assist the client to recover from the workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 10 What else could help the worker with their recovery at this time?

\[Describe any other evidence-based suggestions that could help the client with their recovery in addition to other suggestions already made. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than five sentences.]

#### 11 Which other clinicians have you, or will you, collaborate with to enhance the worker’s treatment and recovery?

* \[List the professional disciplines, and names, of each individual that the psychologist will or may collaborate with to enhance the worker’s treatment and recovery from the workplace injury. This list should include health professionals that the psychologist has written to or received reports from.]

#### 12 Psychologist details

Name: \[Name of the Psychologist.]
Address: \[Address of the Psychologist.]
Provider Number: \[Psychologist’s Medicare Provider Number.]

title: WorkSafe Victoria – Psychology Treatment Review Form (PS109)
description: Psychologists working in the WorkSafe (WorkCover) Victoria workers compensation system in Australia are advised to complete a Psychology Treatment
prompt:
(This template is designed to assist psychologists working within the WorkSafe Victoria workers compensation system in Australia. It follows the format of the Psychology Treatment Review Form, also known as the PS109 form, which psychologists must complete after approximately ten sessions of treatment or after six months of treatment, whichever comes first. Psychologists then must submit this form to the insurance company that is managing the client’s case. Please use a formal and professional tone in your output. Only use information that is contained in the transcripts, contextual notes, and psychometric test results. You must strictly adhere to the following format:)

[psychology_treatment_review_form_ps109] Psychology Treatment Review Form (PS109)

[details] Details

Worker’s Name: \[Client’s First and Last Name.]
Claim Number: \[Client’s Claim Number.]
Occupation / Job Title: \[Client’s Occupation or Job Title.]
General Practitioner’s Name: \[GP’s First and Last Name.]
Referrer’s Name: \[Referrer’s First and Last Name (if different from the General Practitioner).]
Date of Referral: \[Date of Referral.]
Number of sessions to date: \[Print the number of sessions to date between the psychologist and client.]

#### Referring to the Psychology treatment planning form, please review the progress and the ongoing recommendations.

#### 1 Your DSM diagnosis (or provisional diagnosis) with respect to the claimable injury, using the latest published version of the DSM.

\[Describe the psychologist’s and / or General Practitioner’s diagnosis according to criteria set out in the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders – 5th Edition – Text Revision, also known as the DSM-5-TR. Print out the relevant diagnostic codes from the DSM-5-TR for any diagnosis given. Include the level of severity of any diagnosis, and any clinically significant co-morbidities. Describe if the diagnosis has changed since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form.]

#### 2 With respect to the claimable injury, describe the psychological and physical symptoms the worker is currently presenting.

\[Describe the psychological and physical symptoms the client is currently experiencing in relation to their workplace injury. Describe if there have been any changes since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

#### 3 With respect to the claimable injury, what changes have occurred to the following, in comparison to the Psychology treatment planning form:

[s_3_1_home_life] 3.1 Home life

\[Describe any changes that have occurred since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form, in relation to the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on their home life, including any issues with family, ability to perform domestic duties, or any other aspect of living at home. If applicable, refer to the client’s ability with these things prior to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

[s_3_2_social_engagement] 3.2 Social engagement

\[Describe any changes that have occurred since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form, in relation to the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on their level of social engagement. If applicable, refer to the client’s level of social engagement prior to their workplace injury. For example, is the client able to socialise effectively, is the client engaging in social isolation, and how does the client’s current level of social engagement compare to their level of social engagement prior to the workplace injury, and compared to the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

[s_3_3_emotional_wellbeing_quality_of_life] 3.3 Emotional wellbeing / quality of life

\[Describe any changes that have occurred since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form, in relation to the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on their emotional wellbeing and quality of life. Describe how the workplace injury has impacted on the client’s mental health. If applicable, refer to the client’s wellbeing and quality of life prior to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

#### 3.4 Readiness to return to work or progress with the support of occupational rehabilitation services

\[Describe any changes that have occurred since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form, in relation to the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on the client’s readiness to return to work or on the client’s progress with the support of occupational rehabilitation services. Describe if the client is ready, or will be ready in the near future, to return to work or engage in occupational rehabilitation services. Describe the overall impact on the client’s mental health and how this may prevent the client from currently participating in employment or any occupational rehabilitation services. Make an explicit statement about the client's current capacity to engage in employment or occupational rehabilitation services. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

[s_3_5_other_areas] 3.5 Other areas

\[Describe any changes that have occurred since completion of the original Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form, in relation to the impact of the client’s psychological and physical symptoms on any other aspect of their lives not captured by 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, or 3.4 above e.g., ability to drive; ability to engage in community activities; or ability to perform any other duty or function. If applicable, refer to the client’s ability with these things prior to their workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

#### 4 Goals – refer to goals identified on the Psychology treatment planning form

* \[List the goals that the client had identified in the Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form. Along with each goal, state the progress with each goal using one of five descriptors: “Setback experienced”; “No progress”; “Progressing towards goal”; “Goal achieved; or “Exceeded goal expectations”. Then, provide a comment about the client’s progress. In your comment for each goal, include information about why the client’s progress is what it is; barriers to progress; and future expectations in relation to the client achieving the goal. There should only be a maximum of four goals. If you recognise that two goals are very similar, bundle them into one goal.]

#### 5 Outcome measures – including individual goal attainment scales and validated psychometrics, results, and interpretation.

#### 5.1 Previous scales, results and interpretation from the Psychology treatment planning form

* \[List the name, date of completion, results, and brief interpretation of psychometric testing undertaken by the client, as listed on the Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form.]

[s_5_2_follow_up_results] 5.2 Follow-up results

* \[List the name, date of completion, results, and brief interpretation of psychometric testing undertaken by the client, since the Psychology Treatment Planning Form, also known as the PS604 form. Do not list any administrations of psychometric testing that are listed on the Psychology Treatment Planning Form.]

[s_6_factors_that_are_impeding_recovery] 6 Factors that are impeding recovery

\[If applicable, describe any factors that are impeding the client’s recovery e.g., lack of social and family support; transport difficulties; family issues; addictions; issues with the client’s employer; and others. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

[how_may_these_be_addressed] How may these be addressed?

\[If applicable, describe any recommendations for potential treatment or additional support that may assist the client to deal with any factors impeding their recovery e.g., mediation; referrals to other specialists or health organisations; support from community organisations; and others. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 7 Details of factors that are improving recovery (personal strengths or situational factors)

\[If applicable, describe any factors that are enhancing the client’s recovery e.g., personal strengths and characteristics; situational factors; family support; and others. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than five sentences.]

[s_8_what_else_would_help_the_worker_with_t] 8 What else would help the worker with their recovery at this time?

\[Describe any other evidence-based suggestions that could help the client with their recovery in addition to other suggestions already made. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than four sentences.]

[s_9_treatment] 9 Treatment

[s_9_1_how_many_sessions_have_you_provided] 9.1 How many sessions have you provided?

\[Describe how many sessions the psychologist has had in total with the client. Describe the timeframe within which those sessions have taken place. Provide your response in one sentence.]

#### 9.2 How many sessions do you anticipate will be required over the next 6-12 months and at what frequency?

\[Describe how many sessions the psychologist anticipates will be required over the next six to twelve months, and at what frequency of sessions that the psychologist believes are needed for the client within the next six to twelve months in order to assist the client to recover from the workplace injury. Provide your response in one sentence.]

[s_9_3_what_is_your_clinical_rationale_for_] 9.3 What is your clinical rationale for the treatment frequency?

\[Describe the psychologist’s rationale for the suggested frequency of sessions required in order to assist the client to recover from the workplace injury. Provide your response in one paragraph of less than three sentences.]

#### 10 Which other clinicians have you, or will you, collaborate with to enhance the worker’s treatment and recovery?

* \[List the professional disciplines, and names, of each individual that the psychologist has collaborated with, or may collaborate with in the future to enhance the worker’s treatment and recovery from the workplace injury. This list should include health professionals that the psychologist has written to or received reports from.]

[s_11_psychologist_details] 11 Psychologist details

Name: \[Name of the Psychologist.]
Address: \[Address of the Psychologist.]
Provider Number: \[Psychologist’s Medicare Provider Number.]
